root.description: Free shadcn components for React and Tailwind CSS. {{count}} copy-ready layouts covering data grids, kanban, charts, forms, and 60+ open-source components compatible with shadcn/ui.
root.intro: Browse {{count}} open-source shadcn components for React and Tailwind CSS. From `@tanstack/react-table`-powered data grids and `@dnd-kit/core` kanban boards to form fields, charts, and navigation, every component is copy-ready, maintained as part of the ReUI library, and fully compatible with your shadcn/ui setup and all five Shadcn Create style themes.

content.title: Shadcn Components: Go Beyond AI Defaults
summary: ReUI is an open-source component library for the shadcn/ui ecosystem. Every component is built from shadcn primitives, styled with Tailwind CSS, and composed inside realistic dashboard layouts, not isolated component playgrounds. Browse {{count}} copy-ready catalog components and drop them directly into your React or Next.js project.

The library covers standard shadcn/ui components such as Button, Badge, and Dialog, plus 17 in-house custom components including Data Grid, Kanban, Filters, File Upload, Sortable, Timeline, and Stepper that extend the ecosystem beyond base shadcn/ui. All in-house components ship in both *Radix UI* and *Base UI* flavors.
Use the sections below to explore popular components, understand standard versus in-house components, check Shadcn Create compatibility, and compare *Radix UI* and *Base UI* variants.

sections.title: What are ReUI Components?
paragraphs: ReUI components are reusable UI solutions composed from shadcn/ui components into real-world use cases: dashboards, forms, data views, and product flows, not isolated playground demos. Each component follows the shadcn copy-and-own model: the source installs directly into your repository via the shadcn CLI (`npx shadcn@latest add`). You own the code, customize it with your Tailwind CSS tokens, and extend it without version conflicts or package lock-in.

The difference between a shadcn primitive and a composed catalog component is scope. A primitive such as Button renders a styled, accessible element. A composed component is: a Data Grid row with inline editing, a Filters panel that persists state to the URL via `nuqs`, or a Stepper wired to per-step `react-hook-form` validation. Catalog components show how primitives combine inside production layouts.

sections.title: Popular Components

sections.title: Shadcn Components vs. In-House Components
paragraphs: ReUI components fall into two categories. Standard shadcn/ui components such as Button, Dialog, and Table extend base shadcn/ui primitives with production-ready layouts you will not find in minimal docs demos. In-house components such as Data Grid, Kanban, and Filters are built and maintained by the ReUI team for dashboard-grade workflows not covered by base shadcn/ui.

All in-house components follow the shadcn copy-and-own installation model and ship in two API flavors: a *Radix UI* version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a *Base UI* version for teams using `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical Tailwind CSS styling; only the underlying headless primitive differs. Each has dedicated documentation covering the full component API, prop references, and usage examples.

sections.title: Shadcn Create Compatibility
paragraphs: All ReUI components are fully compatible with Shadcn Create. The five named themes (Vega, Nova, Maia, Lyra, and Mira) and the style controls for *Radius*, *Font Family*, *Base Color*, and *Color Scale* propagate to every component preview. The component source you copy uses the same CSS variable tokens, so what you see in the preview is exactly what you get in your project.

This applies to both standard and in-house components. In-house components like Data Grid and Kanban reference the same `--radius`, `--background`, `--foreground`, and `--primary` tokens that Shadcn Create writes to your `globals.css`.

sections.title: Radix UI and Base UI Compatibility
paragraphs: ReUI supports both major headless stacks in the React ecosystem. Standard shadcn/ui components follow *Radix UI* primitives. In-house components that need headless state are offered in both *Radix UI* and *Base UI* API versions via `@base-ui/react`, with the same Tailwind styling and layout expectations.

Teams migrating from *Radix UI* to *Base UI*, or starting a new project with *Base UI*, can use the Base UI variants without redoing your theme or markup. In-house component documentation at [ReUI Docs](/docs) explains both API options with prop references and usage examples.

faqs.question: What is the difference between a ReUI component and a shadcn/ui component?
faqs.answer: A shadcn/ui component is a primitive: Button gives you a styled, accessible element. A ReUI component is a composed solution built from one or more shadcn primitives for a real product use case: a filterable data grid, a multi-step onboarding wizard, or a drag-and-drop kanban board. Catalog components show how primitives wire together inside production layouts, not how a single primitive renders in a sandbox.

faqs.question: How is ReUI different from shadcn/ui itself?
faqs.answer: shadcn/ui provides the primitive components and the CLI install model. ReUI builds on top of that foundation: it adds {{count}} composed components, 17 in-house custom components not in base shadcn/ui (Data Grid, Kanban, Filters, File Upload, Sortable, Timeline, Stepper, and more), and presents everything inside realistic dashboard frames rather than isolated demos. If shadcn/ui is the ingredient kit, ReUI is the recipe collection.

faqs.question: Do I need to install an npm package to use ReUI components?
faqs.answer: No. ReUI follows the shadcn copy-and-own model: there is no `@reui` npm package to install. You add individual components via the shadcn CLI (`npx shadcn@latest add`), and the source files land directly in your repository. Some components pull in well-known third-party packages like `@tanstack/react-table`, `@dnd-kit/core`, or `recharts`, but ReUI itself adds no versioned dependency to your lockfile.

faqs.question: Can I use just one or two components without copying the whole library?
faqs.answer: Yes. Components are fully independent. You can copy a single Data Grid component, a single Stepper variant, or any specific layout you need. There is no global install step and no ReUI runtime. Each component brings only the source files and third-party dependencies it actually needs.

faqs.question: When upstream shadcn/ui or ReUI updates a component, do my files auto-update?
faqs.answer: No. You own the files after install. Treat updates like internal code: review changelogs, merge diffs, or re-run the CLI for a fresh copy and resolve conflicts. This is the explicit trade-off of the copy-and-own model: you gain full control and zero lock-in, and you take responsibility for managing updates on your own schedule.

faqs.question: I pasted a component but it does not match the ReUI preview. What should I check first?
faqs.answer: Compare your theme tokens. Previews assume the same CSS variables (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--radius`, and the related color scales and font stacks) as a configured Shadcn Create theme. If `globals.css` is missing variables, imports differ, or you trimmed wrapper classes from the snippet, layout and color will drift. Align tokens first, then compare class-for-class.

faqs.question: Radix UI version or Base UI version for an in-house component: how do I choose?
faqs.answer: Match the headless stack you have already standardized on. If your project uses Radix-oriented shadcn primitives throughout, stay with the *Radix UI* variant for fewer mental models and dependencies. If you are building with `@base-ui/react` or migrating toward Base UI, take the *Base UI* variant. The visual output is identical: only the primitive API and dependency graph differ. You can see both options in [ReUI Docs](/docs).

faqs.question: Are ReUI components compatible with Next.js App Router and server components?
faqs.answer: Yes. Components that use state, effects, or event handlers are client components and include the `'use client'` directive. Purely presentational components can render server-side. The shadcn CLI adds files to your project as source, so you decide where each component sits in your rendering boundary. All Tailwind styling and token-based theming work regardless of whether you use the App Router or Pages Router.

faqs.question: Can I ship a closed-source SaaS product that includes ReUI components?
faqs.answer: Yes. ReUI is MIT licensed. You may use, modify, and distribute copied source in proprietary or commercial products without publishing your application code. Keep the copyright notice in any license files where the MIT license requires it; beyond that, there are no restrictions on commercial use.

accordion.description: Shadcn Accordion components for collapsible FAQs, settings, and onboarding flows. {{count}} free Tailwind CSS layouts for React and Next.js, compatible with shadcn/ui.
accordion.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready Shadcn Accordion components for FAQ layouts, settings panels, onboarding checklists, and help sections. Shadcn Accordion is compatible with *Radix UI* and *Base UI* interaction models and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Accordion: Build Interactive Collapsible Sections
summary: Shadcn Accordion is a collapsible disclosure component built on *Radix UI* primitives, compatible with *Base UI*-style composition, and fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options. Radius, fonts, and theme tokens from the create flow apply consistently to previews and exported code so what you configure is what you ship.

It presents a row of triggers and expands one or more panels on demand, letting users scan headings first and reveal detail only when needed. ReUI lists accordion components inside realistic dashboard layouts so you can judge spacing, icon placement, and density in context, not only in a minimal sandbox.
The sections below explain what the component is, why teams adopt it, which features matter, and how it pairs with other primitives. Frequently asked questions sit at the end.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Accordion?
paragraphs: In UI terms, an accordion is a set of disclosure regions: each header toggles a panel open or closed. Shadcn Accordion follows the same accessibility expectations as *Radix UI* primitives and fits teams that also use *Base UI*-style unstyled building blocks, so you can mix components without fighting the stack.

Components here show arrow and icon variants, status badges on triggers, single-expand and multi-expand configurations, and nested content setups. They are meant to answer whether your product needs a calm FAQ layout, a dense settings stack, or onboarding steps with clear hierarchy, with styling that respects Shadcn Create options end to end.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Accordion?
paragraphs: Accordions reduce noise when many answers or settings share one URL. Marketing FAQs, billing preferences, and in-app help sections commonly use them so scanning stays fast and optional detail stays one click away.

They also align with how people search: question labels map naturally to trigger text, and answers stay in the HTML for indexing when you avoid hiding content behind client-only rendering. When a section needs actions or form inputs alongside disclosure, pair the accordion with a button or field group so the interaction stays cohesive.

featureItems.title: Accessibility-Compliant.
featureItems.description: Shadcn Accordion adheres to WAI-ARIA disclosure guidelines to provide keyboard accessibility and proper roles out of the box.

featureItems.title: Single or Multi-Open Behavior.
featureItems.description: Choose one open panel for focused reading or allow several open at once for comparison, depending on your task flow and content density.

featureItems.title: Collapsible Panels.
featureItems.description: Open and close content regions without a route change, keeping users in context for help sections, FAQs, and settings pages.

featureItems.title: Icons and Badges on Triggers.
featureItems.description: Optional chevrons, status badge labels, or count metadata on the header row improve scan speed and convey state without expanding a panel.

featureItems.title: Full Tailwind CSS Customization.
featureItems.description: The component is styled with Tailwind CSS utility classes and uses shadcn/ui CSS variable tokens, so it integrates cleanly with any design system.

featureItems.title: Shadcn Create Compatible.
featureItems.description: All five Shadcn Create themes (Vega, Nova, Maia, Lyra, and Mira) and the *Radius*, *Font Family*, and *Color Scale* controls apply directly to accordion previews and exported code.

faqs.question: What is a Shadcn Accordion used for in real products?
faqs.answer: Teams use it for FAQs, account and billing settings, product detail add-ons, onboarding checklists, and in-app help. Anywhere users should skim headings first and open only what they need, a collapsible accordion reduces noise without requiring a full page navigation.

faqs.question: Should I use single-open or multi-open accordion behavior?
faqs.answer: Single-open keeps the layout compact when sections compete for attention or when reading order matters, such as troubleshooting steps. Multi-open helps when people compare answers side by side or revisit multiple panels in one session. Match the behavior to task analytics, not only aesthetics.

faqs.question: Is a Shadcn Accordion keyboard accessible out of the box?
faqs.answer: Libraries built on *Radix UI*-style primitives expose the correct roles and keyboard interactions at the component level. You still own copy quality, focus-visible styles in your Tailwind theme, logical heading order, and testing with real assistive technology. Treat baseline behavior as necessary but not sufficient.

faqs.question: How is an accordion different from tabs or a modal dialog?
faqs.answer: Tabs switch between parallel views at the same hierarchy level. Accordions reveal optional detail under one scrollable page. Modals and drawers demand focused attention for a short task or confirmation. If the goal is optional reading while preserving page context, accordions usually fit better than a dialog.

faqs.question: Do accordions hurt SEO if answers are collapsed?
faqs.answer: Search engines index content that is present in the HTML and not blocked by robots or client-only rendering. Avoid hiding your only substantive text behind interactions that never reach the DOM. Write unique, helpful answers and link to deeper resources. Accordions are a layout choice, not a shortcut for thin pages.

faqs.question: How are ReUI Accordion components different from a minimal docs demo?
faqs.answer: They place disclosure inside realistic layouts: dashboard panels, onboarding flows, and settings pages. You evaluate `motion`, density, and companion components together, which is closer to how the same markup will look in production than a standalone component preview.

faqs.question: Is Shadcn Accordion compatible with Base UI, Radix UI, and Shadcn Create?
faqs.answer: Yes. The component follows *Radix UI* disclosure semantics and keyboard behavior, and aligns with *Base UI*-oriented composition when you keep tokens consistent. Shadcn Create style options (*Radius*, *Font Family*, *Color Scale*, and related theme controls) apply to accordion components so what you configure in create matches the UI you export.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Place [Shadcn Button](/components/button) actions inside expanded panels or next to triggers when users must confirm, save, or navigate to another flow. Use a [Shadcn Badge](/components/badge) on the header row for counts, beta labels, or status so closed and open states stay scannable at a glance.

Split long forms with [Shadcn Form](/components/form) field groups per accordion section so validation messages and helper text stay near their inputs. Wrap the whole module in a [Shadcn Card](/components/card) when the page mixes tables, charts, or other content blocks and you need a clear visual boundary.

Use [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) for quick edits or filters without expanding a full section, and [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) for short hints on trigger icons. Together they keep small tasks lightweight while the accordion holds the primary content story.

alert.description: Shadcn Alert components for status banners, validation summaries and feedback notifications. {{count}} free Tailwind CSS layouts for React and Next.js.
alert.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready Shadcn Alert components for success, warning, destructive, and inline actions. Alerts follow accessible roles from the Radix stack, align with Base UI-style composition, and stay fully compatible with Shadcn Create so radius, color, and typography match your configured theme.
alert.docsDescription: Explore ReUI examples for success, warning, destructive, and action-based alerts.

content.title: Shadcn Alert: Status and Feedback for React Apps
summary: Shadcn Alert is the callout primitive for status, validation, billing, and system messages. It maps to WAI-ARIA alert and status roles so assistive technologies announce important content without breaking your layout.

Built for the React and Tailwind ecosystem, it works with Radix UI and Base UI-style projects because styling stays token-driven. Shadcn Create style options apply to alert surfaces so what you tune in the customizer matches exported code.

ReUI lists alert components in real dashboards and forms so you compare density, icons, and actions next to a [Shadcn Card](/components/card) or table, not a lone sandbox. The sections below cover what it is, why teams use it, features, and integration ideas.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Alert?
paragraphs: In product UI, an alert is a persistent or dismissible region that highlights an outcome, risk, or next step. Shadcn Alert composes title, description, and optional icon or actions while keeping roles and labels consistent with WAI-ARIA guidance.

Components here show stacked alerts, billing warnings, and inline actions. They help you decide density, contrast, and placement before you wire the same component into Next.js, Vite, or a server-rendered React tree.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Alert?
paragraphs: Teams use alerts when users must notice state without opening a modal. That includes payment failures, form errors at the top of a page, or irreversible actions that need a short explanation and a primary [Shadcn Button](/components/button).

Searchers often look for "shadcn alert", "tailwind alert", or "react accessible alert". This page answers those intents with concrete layouts and copy that stays technically accurate for the shadcn ecosystem.

featureItems.title: WAI-ARIA Aligned.
featureItems.description: Uses roles and semantics that match WAI-ARIA roles for alerts and status messages so screen readers can surface urgency without guessing.

featureItems.title: Radix UI and Base UI Friendly.
featureItems.description: Styling uses semantic tokens and composition that fit Radix UI projects and Base UI-style unstyled layers when you bring your own primitives.

featureItems.title: Shadcn Styles and Create.
featureItems.description: Fully compatible with Shadcn Create: try the style customizer above the component grid to preview radius, fonts, and colors on the same alert variants you will export.

featureItems.title: React and Tailwind First.
featureItems.description: Written for modern React with Tailwind CSS utility classes; pair with the shadcn CLI workflow (`npx shadcn@latest add alert`) to add source to your repo.

featureItems.title: Variants and Actions.
featureItems.description: Success, warning, destructive, and neutral treatments with optional actions or links, aligned with your design system tokens.

sections.title: ReUI Alert: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Alert is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project: following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Alert extends the standard shadcn Alert with additional variants: success, warning, info, and invert: each backed by dedicated CSS variable tokens. This makes it a drop-in replacement that covers the full semantic range modern products need without patching base shadcn.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling: only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version: API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version: API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: What is a Shadcn Alert used for?
faqs.answer: Inline status and feedback: success after save, validation summaries, billing warnings, and destructive confirmations that should stay visible on the page without blocking the whole viewport.

faqs.question: How do I add Shadcn Alert to my project?
faqs.answer: Use the shadcn CLI for your package manager, for example `npx shadcn@latest add alert`, then import the alert parts from your components folder. Keep the same Tailwind theme tokens as the rest of your shadcn install.

faqs.question: Is Shadcn Alert accessible?
faqs.answer: The implementation follows WAI-ARIA roles and expectations for alerts. You still provide clear copy, focus management when actions are present, and test with real assistive tech in your theme.

faqs.question: Does it work with Next.js App Router?
faqs.answer: Yes. Compose alerts in client or server components as needed; keep interactive actions in client boundaries when they use hooks. Styling stays consistent with your Tailwind and shadcn tokens.

faqs.question: How is an alert different from a toast?
faqs.answer: Alerts usually stay in layout until dismissed or resolved. Toasts from libraries like Sonner are transient and stack in a corner. Use alerts for persistent context on the current screen.

faqs.question: Can I use Shadcn Alert with Radix UI and Base UI stacks?
faqs.answer: Yes. Shadcn Alert is designed for the Radix-oriented shadcn stack and token-based styling that also works when you compose Base UI primitives elsewhere in the app.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair alerts with a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) or link group for primary and secondary actions such as retry, view details, or dismiss when your product allows it.
Place alerts inside a [Shadcn Card](/components/card) or at the top of a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) group so validation errors stay near the inputs they reference.

For blocking decisions, route users to a [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) or [Shadcn Alert Dialog](/components/alert-dialog) while alerts handle non-modal status that should remain visible in the layout.

alert-dialog.description: Shadcn Alert Dialog components for destructive action confirmation and critical warnings. {{count}} free Tailwind CSS layouts for React and Next.js.
alert-dialog.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn alert-dialog components for delete confirmations, destructive actions, and critical user decisions. Alert Dialog is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options.

content.title: Shadcn Alert Dialog: Confirm Destructive Actions
summary: Shadcn Alert Dialog is a modal primitive for irreversible actions: account deletion, permanent data removal, and destructive confirmations. It follows Radix UI accessibility conventions and Base UI-style composition, with full compatibility for Shadcn Create style tokens so your radius, colors, and typography stay consistent.

It overlays the page with a focused dialog that blocks interaction until the user confirms or cancels a critical action, often using a checkbox or text input to prevent accidental destructive operations.

ReUI lists alert-dialog components inside realistic dashboards and forms so you evaluate focus management, button hierarchy, and confirmation strategies next to tables or other workflows, not in isolation. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how it integrates with other primitives.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Alert Dialog?
paragraphs: An alert dialog is a focused modal that blocks interaction with the rest of the page and asks users to confirm a critical, usually irreversible action. Shadcn Alert Dialog follows Radix UI focus trap and keyboard behavior so assistive technologies and keyboard users can dismiss or confirm without mouse.

Components here show delete confirmations with icons, checkbox-gated destructive buttons, text input verification (like 'type DELETE to confirm'), and pairing with primary/secondary button variants so users see intent without confusion.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Alert Dialog?
paragraphs: Teams use alert dialogs when an action is permanent and accidental activation would harm the product or user data. Bulk deletes, account closures, and irreversible state changes need explicit, focused confirmation that a toast or inline message cannot provide.

Searchers look for 'delete confirmation react', 'destructive dialog component', and 'alert dialog shadcn'. This page answers those queries with real layouts showing how to combine text, icons, and button variants to make consequences unmistakable without alarm fatigue.

featureItems.title: Focus Management.
featureItems.description: Focus trap ensures users cannot tab away from the dialog until confirmed or cancelled, keeping attention on the decision.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Navigation.
featureItems.description: Escape to cancel, Enter or Tab to move between buttons, and destructive button styling follow WAI-ARIA alert dialog semantics.

featureItems.title: Destructive Button Variant.
featureItems.description: Pair red/warning button styling with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to signal consequence; secondary button for the safe choice (cancel).

featureItems.title: Checkbox and Text Gating.
featureItems.description: Prevent one-click accidents by requiring users to check a [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) or type confirmation text before delete becomes enabled.

featureItems.title: Icon and Copy Alignment.
featureItems.description: Lucide icons (Trash2, AlertTriangle) paired with clear titles ('Delete Account?') and description make consequence obvious without needing to read a full modal.

faqs.question: When should I use alert-dialog instead of a regular dialog?
faqs.answer: Use alert-dialog only for truly irreversible or high-consequence actions: account deletion, data destruction, irreversible state changes. Use a regular [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) for reversible tasks like editing a name or uploading a file.

faqs.question: Should I add a checkbox or text verification to my delete dialog?
faqs.answer: Add friction (checkbox, text input) when the cost of error is high and users repeat the action infrequently. For daily actions like bulk deletes in a data tool, a one-step confirm may be better; for account closure, require confirmation.

faqs.question: What button variant works best for the destructive action?
faqs.answer: Use the destructive variant (red background) from [Shadcn Button](/components/button), paired with a secondary or outline variant for cancel so the safe choice stands out equally.

faqs.question: How do I handle alert-dialog focus in Next.js?
faqs.answer: Alert-dialog manages its own focus trap via Radix UI primitives. When mounted via state in a client component, focus automatically moves to the first button and returns to the trigger after dismissal.

faqs.question: Can I use alert-dialog on mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes. Ensure button hit targets are 44px+, text is readable, and the modal does not exceed viewport height when scrolling. Test focus and dismissal on touch devices.

faqs.question: Is alert-dialog keyboard accessible?
faqs.answer: Yes, it follows WAI-ARIA guidance: Escape closes it, Tab cycles buttons, Enter confirms. Test with a screen reader to confirm role and description are announced.

faqs.question: How are ReUI alert-dialog components different from a basic storybook demo?
faqs.answer: They show dialogs inside realistic dashboards, forms, and workflows so you can evaluate spacing, button order, and how the overlay interacts with surrounding UI before shipping.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair alert-dialog with a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to trigger it; use the destructive variant for delete and a secondary variant for cancel. Include a [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) in the title if the action affects multiple items ('Delete 5 files?').

Use [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) or [Shadcn Input](/components/input) inside the dialog body to gate the destructive button, preventing accidental clicks. Add a [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) for quick help text if users question why a confirmation is needed.

When the action succeeds or fails, show a [Shadcn Alert](/docs/components/base/alert) on the page confirming the result. Combine alert-dialog with [Sonner](/components/toast) for async operations that take time so users can close the dialog before the operation completes.

aspect-ratio.description: Shadcn Aspect Ratio component for responsive images and embedded video containers. {{count}} free Tailwind CSS layouts for React and Next.js.
aspect-ratio.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn aspect-ratio components for image galleries, video previews, and responsive media. Aspect Ratio is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI stacks and fully aligned with Shadcn Create so radius, colors, and typography match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Aspect Ratio: Maintain Fixed Media Proportions
summary: Shadcn Aspect Ratio is a layout primitive that reserves space in a fixed width-to-height ratio, preventing layout shift when images or video load. It follows Radix UI composition conventions and works seamlessly with Base UI-style unstyled primitives, with full compatibility for Shadcn Create styling.
It wraps media (images, video embeds, iframes) and maintains the ratio regardless of container width, so your galleries and previews scale responsively without jarring reflow when assets load.

ReUI lists aspect-ratio components inside image galleries, dashboard cards, and e-commerce product layouts so you evaluate how they pair with [Shadcn Card](/components/card), [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) for loading, and responsive grids. The sections below cover what the component is, why it matters, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Aspect Ratio?
paragraphs: An aspect ratio container reserves space at a fixed ratio (16:9, 1:1, 4:3) before content loads, preventing cumulative layout shift when images or video arrive. Shadcn Aspect Ratio wraps your media and applies CSS that enforces the ratio on the container itself, not the image.

Components here show 16:9 widescreen video frames, 1:1 square avatars and product photos, and 4:3 traditional aspect ratios in grids, galleries, and card layouts. They help you choose a ratio and see how it scales on mobile without breaking the layout.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Aspect Ratio?
paragraphs: Layouts jump when images load asynchronously, hurting Core Web Vitals and user experience. Aspect ratio containers solve this by claiming space upfront so text and buttons stay put while media loads.

Searchers look for 'react aspect ratio', 'image container responsive', and 'prevent layout shift'. This page answers with concrete gallery and card examples showing how aspect ratio pairs with [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) for loading states and responsive grids so your UI feels polished on all screen sizes.

featureItems.title: Prevents Layout Shift.
featureItems.description: Reserves space before images load so text, buttons, and surrounding content stay in place, improving Core Web Vitals.

featureItems.title: Common Ratios.
featureItems.description: Supports 16:9 (widescreen video), 1:1 (square photos), 4:3 (traditional), and custom ratios:set via the ratio prop.

featureItems.title: Works with Next.js Image.
featureItems.description: Pairs with next/image for optimized, lazy-loaded assets while maintaining proportion without CSS hacks.

featureItems.title: Responsive Scaling.
featureItems.description: Container scales to its parent width while maintaining the ratio, so galleries and card grids look right on all screen sizes.

featureItems.title: Integrates with Skeleton.
featureItems.description: Pair with [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) inside the aspect-ratio container to show a loading placeholder that matches the final image size.

faqs.question: What is aspect ratio and why does it matter for React apps?
faqs.answer: Aspect ratio reserves space at a fixed width-to-height ratio before images load. This prevents layout shift (Cumulative Layout Shift, a Core Web Vital), keeping text and buttons stable while assets arrive.

faqs.question: Which aspect ratio should I choose for product images?
faqs.answer: 1:1 (square) is standard for profiles and product thumbnails; 16:9 for hero images and video; 4:3 for traditional photos. Match your image source and design, then test on mobile.

faqs.question: Can I use aspect-ratio with Next.js Image component?
faqs.answer: Yes. Wrap the Image component with AspectRatio to reserve space while next/image handles optimization. The container enforces the ratio, and the image fills with object-cover.

faqs.question: How do I show a loading state inside aspect-ratio?
faqs.answer: Place a [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) inside the AspectRatio container at the same ratio. When the image loads, swap it for the real asset.

faqs.question: Does aspect-ratio work on mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes. The container is responsive and scales to its parent. No special mobile handling needed; test that images look good at smaller viewports.

faqs.question: Can I set a custom aspect ratio not in the presets?
faqs.answer: Yes. Pass any ratio: `<AspectRatio ratio={3 / 2}>` for 3:2, or `{5 / 3}` for 5:3. Ratio is width ÷ height.

faqs.question: How are ReUI aspect-ratio components different from a basic code snippet?
faqs.answer: They show galleries, cards, and responsive grids so you see how aspect-ratio interacts with [Shadcn Card](/components/card), [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton), and surrounding layout on mobile and desktop.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap images inside [Shadcn Card](/components/card) with an aspect-ratio container so images scale responsively while text and actions stay below. Use a [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) at the same ratio for loading states.

Combine aspect-ratio with [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) overlays to mark images as new, featured, or out-of-stock. Pair with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) as action overlays on hover when images need quick interactions.

In gallery layouts, use aspect-ratio across multiple [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) or lightbox components so all thumbnails maintain consistent proportions. Add [Shadcn Input](/components/input) next to the gallery for search and filtering.

autocomplete.description: Shadcn Autocomplete components with async filtering and keyboard navigation support. {{count}} searchable input examples for React with accessibility.
autocomplete.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn autocomplete components using `@base-ui/react` Autocomplete. Covers basic typeahead, auto-highlight, grouped options, clear and trigger button variants, form integration with React Hook Form and Zod validation, and sizes: built for search inputs, filter fields, and form controls in React and Next.js apps.

content.title: Shadcn Autocomplete: Base UI Typeahead for React Forms
summary: ReUI Autocomplete is built on `@base-ui/react` Autocomplete: the headless, WAI-ARIA-compliant autocomplete primitive from the Base UI library. It manages listbox open state, active option highlight, keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Escape), and screen reader announcements without any CSS assumptions.

Twelve components cover basic typeahead, small and large size variants, auto-highlight on keystroke, grouped options with section labels, clear button, trigger button, combined clear and trigger, search results layout, form integration with React Hook Form, and Zod schema validation.
The sections below explain how ReUI Autocomplete differs from Combobox and Command, real use cases, features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Autocomplete?
paragraphs: ReUI Autocomplete is a custom shadcn component built on `@base-ui/react`'s Autocomplete primitive. It is distinct from the shadcn Command component (which is a full command palette) and from the Combobox (which is built on a different Base UI primitive). Autocomplete is optimized for form fields and search inputs where the user types to filter a list: such as country selectors, user mention inputs, tag inputs, and address autocomplete.

The component wraps the Base UI Autocomplete with shadcn Field, Label, and Input styling, inheriting your design tokens. ScrollArea handles long suggestion lists without a fixed-height overflow container. Components show Autocomplete inside form layouts with React Hook Form and Zod validation, displaying field-level errors inline.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Autocomplete?
paragraphs: Search inputs, tag selectors, and filtered dropdowns all benefit from a fully accessible autocomplete that manages focus, keyboard navigation, and ARIA without custom code. `@base-ui/react`'s headless primitive handles all WAI-ARIA combobox semantics: active descendant management, listbox role, option roles, and live region announcements: leaving styling entirely to Tailwind.

React Hook Form integration means autocomplete values participate in form validation, dirty-state detection, and submit handling. Zod schemas validate selected values server-side and client-side with the same schema. Pair with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for accessible label and error message composition.

sections.title: Libraries and ReUI Autocomplete Primitives
paragraphs: ReUI Autocomplete imports `@base-ui/react` for the headless Autocomplete primitive, `react-hook-form` for form state management, `@hookform/resolvers` for Zod adapter wiring, Zod for schema validation, and Sonner for async submit feedback.
It is distinct from cmdk (Command) and is optimized for typeahead in forms and filters rather than full command palettes.

featureItems.title: Base UI Autocomplete Primitive.
featureItems.description: Built on `@base-ui/react` Autocomplete. Full WAI-ARIA combobox implementation with active-descendant management, listbox and option roles, and Escape/Enter/Arrow key handling out of the box.

featureItems.title: Auto-Highlight on Keystroke.
featureItems.description: The auto-highlight variant immediately highlights the first matching option as the user types, reducing the number of keystrokes needed to select a result in familiar lists.

featureItems.title: Grouped Options with Section Labels.
featureItems.description: Options group into labeled sections: useful for categorized search results, country + dial code pickers, and any list where options naturally cluster by type.

featureItems.title: Clear and Trigger Button Variants.
featureItems.description: Optional clear button removes the selected value; optional trigger button opens the listbox on click like a select. Combine both for a hybrid input that supports typed search and click-to-browse.

featureItems.title: React Hook Form Integration.
featureItems.description: Wraps in a Controller for full React Hook Form compatibility: value, onChange, onBlur, ref, and error state all wire through the standard register API. Form-level and field-level validation work without extra adapters.

featureItems.title: Zod Schema Validation.
featureItems.description: Pair with `@hookform/resolvers`/`zod` to validate autocomplete values against a Zod schema. Inline field errors display via shadcn Field's error slot on failed validation.

featureItems.title: Size Variants.
featureItems.description: Small, default, and large size variants match the sizing scale of shadcn Input. Use small in dense filter toolbars and large in prominent search bars.

featureItems.title: ScrollArea for Long Lists.
featureItems.description: Suggestion listbox uses shadcn ScrollArea for overflow. Long option lists scroll without a fixed-height container hack, and the scroll bar inherits your theme styles.

sections.title: ReUI Autocomplete: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Autocomplete is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project: following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Autocomplete is built on `@base-ui/react` Autocomplete: a primitive not available in base shadcn/ui. It handles WAI-ARIA combobox semantics, listbox state, keyboard navigation, and screen reader announcements while leaving all styling to Tailwind CSS and your design tokens.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling: only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version: API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version: API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: What library does ReUI Autocomplete use?
faqs.answer: `@base-ui/react` Autocomplete. Base UI is the headless component library from the MUI team, offering full WAI-ARIA compliance without any default styles. ReUI wraps it in Tailwind-styled shadcn primitives.

faqs.question: How is Autocomplete different from Combobox and Command?
faqs.answer: All three handle selection from a list, but serve different use cases. Autocomplete (Base UI) is optimized for typed search in forms: country pickers, user mentions, tag inputs. Combobox (also Base UI) supports multi-select with chips and dialog embedding. Command (cmdk) is a full command palette with sections and keyboard shortcuts.

faqs.question: Does Autocomplete work with React Hook Form?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use Controller from `react-hook-form` to wrap the Autocomplete. Pass value, onChange, and ref from the render prop. Zod schemas validate the selected value via `@hookform/resolvers`/`zod`. The form integration component in ReUI shows the exact setup.

faqs.question: How do I implement async search with Autocomplete?
faqs.answer: Debounce the input value change and fire a fetch or SWR revalidation in a useEffect or event handler. Pass the fetched options array as the options prop. Show a loading spinner inside the listbox while fetching: the search results component in ReUI demonstrates this.

faqs.question: Is Autocomplete keyboard accessible?
faqs.answer: Yes, fully. `@base-ui/react` Autocomplete manages active-descendant, listbox and option ARIA roles, and keyboard interactions: ArrowDown/Up to navigate, Enter to select, Escape to close and clear, and Tab to move focus. ReUI adds visible focus-ring styles via Tailwind.

faqs.question: Can I show grouped options with section headings?
faqs.answer: Yes. The grouped options component sections the option list by category with visible labels. Pass a grouped data structure (array of {group, items}) and render a listbox group element for each section. The ReUI component shows the exact markup and Base UI API usage.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Always wrap Autocomplete in [Shadcn Field](/components/field) with a [Shadcn Label](/components/label) for accessible labeling and inline error messages. Use [Shadcn Input Group](/components/input-group) when you need a leading icon or trailing action button alongside the autocomplete input.

For multi-select with chip display, use [Shadcn Combobox](/components/combobox) instead: it is the Base UI-powered multi-select variant optimized for tag inputs and multi-value fields. For full command palettes with sections and actions, use [Shadcn Command](/components/command).

Combine with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for form submission after autocomplete selection, and [Shadcn Sonner](/components/sonner) for async feedback when the selected value triggers a server action.

avatar.description: Shadcn Avatar components for profile images with name and initial fallbacks. {{count}} free Tailwind CSS layouts for React with status indicators.
avatar.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn avatar components for user profiles, team rosters, and presence indicators. Avatar is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI stacks and fully aligned with Shadcn Create styling.

content.title: Shadcn Avatar: Display User Profiles & Initials
summary: Shadcn Avatar is an image placeholder with fallback initials or icons, displaying when user photos fail to load or are unavailable. It follows Radix UI composition conventions and Base UI-style unstyled flexibility, with full compatibility for Shadcn Create styling.
It shows a round or square image container with automatic fallback to initials, labels, or icons, gracefully handling missing or failed images without breaking the layout.

ReUI lists avatar components inside team directories, comment threads, user tables, and profile cards so you evaluate sizing, grouping, and presence indicators next to realistic content. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams use it, key features, and integration ideas.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Avatar?
paragraphs: An avatar component displays a user's photo or initials in a fixed-size circle or square. Shadcn Avatar follows Radix UI composition with an AvatarImage (for the photo) and AvatarFallback (for initials or icon), so the UI stays stable if the image fails or loads slowly.

Components here show single avatars in profiles, grouped avatars for teams (stacked or in a grid), presence indicators (green dot for online), and fallback initials extracted from names. They help you choose a size, decide on fallback content, and see how avatars pair with other components.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Avatar?
paragraphs: User photos humanize interfaces and help identify people at a glance. Avatars are essential in team tools, comment sections, and collaborative apps, but image loading failures and missing photos need graceful fallbacks to avoid blank spaces.

Searchers look for 'react avatar component', 'user profile image fallback', and 'shadcn avatar'. This page answers with real team rosters, comment threads, and profile cards showing how avatars integrate with names, roles, and status indicators.

featureItems.title: Image Fallback.
featureItems.description: Shows initials or an icon if the image fails to load or is not provided, so the UI never breaks.

featureItems.title: Size Variants.
featureItems.description: Supports multiple sizes (xs, sm, md, lg, xl) via Tailwind classes for consistency across lists, cards, and density levels.

featureItems.title: Status Indicators.
featureItems.description: Add a small dot or badge (using [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge)) to show online status, presence, or role without extra space.

featureItems.title: Grouped Avatars.
featureItems.description: Stack or overlap multiple avatars with a count indicator (+3) when space is limited, common in team or group contexts.

featureItems.title: Initials Extraction.
featureItems.description: Derive fallback initials from a name string in the client, or pass explicit initials for custom formatting.

faqs.question: What should I show as an avatar fallback if the image is missing?
faqs.answer: Show the user's initials (first + last letter) extracted from their name. If a name is unavailable, use a generic icon (User icon from Lucide) or a colored background with the first letter.

faqs.question: How do I handle image loading errors gracefully?
faqs.answer: The AvatarImage component from Radix UI automatically shows the AvatarFallback when the image fails to load. No extra code needed; it's built in.

faqs.question: Can I add a status indicator or badge to an avatar?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use absolute positioning to place a [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) or small dot in the bottom-right corner of the avatar. Pair with a class like 'absolute bottom-0 right-0'.

faqs.question: How should I display multiple avatars in a group?
faqs.answer: Use CSS transforms (overlap with negative margin or transform: translateX) to stack avatars on top of each other, or show them side-by-side and add a +N indicator when space is limited.

faqs.question: What size should avatars be in different contexts?
faqs.answer: Lists and tables: sm (32px); comments and mentions: md (40px); profile pages: lg or xl (64px+). Match size to surrounding text and interaction density.

faqs.question: Can I make avatars clickable to open a profile?
faqs.answer: Yes. Wrap the Avatar in a button or link. Use cursor-pointer and add a hover state (opacity, shadow) to signal interactivity.

faqs.question: How are ReUI avatar components different from a standalone component demo?
faqs.answer: They show avatars inside team rosters, comment threads, and user lists so you see sizing, grouping, and how presence indicators pair with names and roles.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair avatars with text (names, roles) inside a [Shadcn Card](/components/card) or list item for user profiles. Use [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) to show roles, status, or organization next to the avatar.

Combine avatars with [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) to show detailed profiles on hover. Use a group of avatars with a [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) to show full team rosters when space is limited.

In comment threads, pair avatars with [Shadcn Textarea](/components/textarea) for input and [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to submit. Use avatars in [Shadcn Table](/components/table) columns to identify users in data rows, styled with [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) as a loading state.

badge.description: Shadcn Badge components including dot indicators and size variants for React apps. {{count}} free Tailwind CSS layouts for React and Next.js components.
badge.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn badge components for status labels, feature tags, role chips, and count indicators. Badge is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI stacks and fully aligned with Shadcn Create styling.

content.title: Shadcn Badge: Status Labels & Indicator Chips
summary: Shadcn Badge is a compact label for status, roles, tags, or counts. It follows Radix UI composition conventions and Base UI-style semantic tokens, with full compatibility for Shadcn Create styling so colors and typography stay consistent.
It renders as a small, inline label with optional icon, often used in tables to show record status, on user rows to display roles, or in headers to flag feature flags or beta labels.

ReUI lists badge components inside tables, user lists, product cards, and dashboards so you evaluate how they pair with [Shadcn Avatar](/components/avatar), [Shadcn Button](/components/button), and [Shadcn Card](/components/card). The sections below explain what the component is, why teams use it, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Badge?
paragraphs: A badge is a small, colored label that communicates status, category, or count without stealing focus. Shadcn Badge is a simple, reusable component that renders text and optional icon(s) with Tailwind utility classes for color and size variants.

Components here show badges for subscription status (Active, Trial, Paused), role labels (Admin, Member, Guest), feature flags (Beta, New), and count indicators (3). They help you choose colors, decide when to use an icon, and see how badges scale on mobile.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Badge?
paragraphs: Status labels at a glance reduce noise and speed up scanning. Tables with 50+ rows become instantly readable when badges show subscription state, approval status, or role without extra columns.

Searchers look for 'react badge component', 'status indicator label', and 'tailwind badge'. This page answers with real tables, cards, and user lists showing how badges pair with avatars, buttons, and text to clarify state without overwhelming the layout.

featureItems.title: Semantic Variants.
featureItems.description: Supports success (green), destructive (red), warning (yellow), and default (gray) variants using Tailwind tokens for consistent theming.

featureItems.title: Icon Support.
featureItems.description: Render optional icons (from Lucide) before or after text. Use sparingly to reinforce meaning without cluttering.

featureItems.title: Size Variants.
featureItems.description: Available in sm, md, lg via padding and font-size classes to fit tables, lists, and dense layouts.

featureItems.title: Dismissible Variant.
featureItems.description: Add an X button to remove the badge, useful for tags or filters that users can clear.

featureItems.title: Accessible.
featureItems.description: Renders as a span with semantic classes; pair with aria-label for clarity when icons are used alone.

sections.title: ReUI Badge: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Badge is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project: following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Badge extends the standard shadcn Badge with semantic color variants: success, warning, info, and invert: each mapped to CSS variables in your Tailwind theme. Use it anywhere you need status-coded labels beyond the default shadcn variant set.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling: only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version: API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version: API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: When should I use a badge vs. text or a button?
faqs.answer: Use badges for read-only status labels. Use buttons when action is needed. Use text for plain descriptions. Badges are for status at a glance.

faqs.question: What colors should I use for different statuses?
faqs.answer: Green for success/active, red for destructive/error, yellow/orange for warning/pending, gray for default/inactive. Match your design tokens.

faqs.question: Should I include an icon in the badge?
faqs.answer: Icons are optional. Use them to reinforce meaning (checkmark for success, X for error), but not every badge needs an icon. Keep visual noise low.

faqs.question: How do I add a dismissible badge for tags?
faqs.answer: Add an X button or close icon inside the badge with onClick handler to remove it. Pair with state management to track selected tags.

faqs.question: Can I use badges in tables to show status?
faqs.answer: Yes. Badges are perfect for status columns in [Shadcn Table](/components/table). They scan faster than plain text and pair well with [Shadcn Avatar](/components/avatar) for user rows.

faqs.question: What if a badge label is too long?
faqs.answer: Truncate with text-ellipsis and max-w-xs, or use a [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) or [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) to show the full text on hover.

faqs.question: How are ReUI badge components different from a code example?
faqs.answer: They show badges inside tables, cards, and user lists so you evaluate context, density, and how colors and icons work together in real interfaces.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair badges with [Shadcn Avatar](/components/avatar) in user rows or team lists to show roles and status. Use badges in [Shadcn Table](/components/table) columns for quick status scanning.

Add badges to [Shadcn Button](/components/button) (e.g., 'Save 3 items') or [Shadcn Input](/components/input) headers to flag counts. Combine badges with [Shadcn Card](/components/card) headers to mark featured items, beta features, or sale tags.

Use dismissible badges with [Shadcn Input](/components/input) or [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for tag selection. Render badge groups inside [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) for detailed filtering, and add [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) for help text on status meanings.

breadcrumb.description: Shadcn Breadcrumb navigation for hierarchical page structure and SEO optimization. {{count}} Next.js components with schema.org markup for search results.
breadcrumb.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn breadcrumb components for navigation trails, app hierarchy, and wayfinding. Breadcrumb is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI stacks and fully aligned with Shadcn Create styling.

content.title: Shadcn Breadcrumb: Navigation Trails & Hierarchy
summary: Shadcn Breadcrumb shows the user's path through an app or documentation hierarchy, with clickable links to parent pages. It follows Radix UI composition conventions and Base UI-style semantic markup, with full compatibility for Shadcn Create styling.
It renders as a horizontal list separated by visual markers (/, >, →) with the current page (non-clickable) at the end, helping users understand where they are and backtrack to parent pages.

ReUI lists breadcrumb components inside e-commerce product pages, documentation hierarchies, and admin dashboards so you evaluate separators, responsive truncation, and mobile stacking. The sections below explain what the component is, why it matters, key features, and integration ideas.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Breadcrumb?
paragraphs: A breadcrumb is a visual navigation trail showing the user's path within a hierarchy: Home / Products / Electronics / Phones. Shadcn Breadcrumb composes BreadcrumbList, BreadcrumbItem, BreadcrumbLink, and BreadcrumbPage so you can render custom separators, icons, and truncation.

Components here show simple breadcrumbs (Home / Page), nested hierarchies (Home / Parent / Current), responsive breadcrumbs with ellipsis on mobile, and icon-based separators. They help you choose a separator style and decide how to handle long paths on small screens.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Breadcrumb?
paragraphs: Breadcrumbs reduce cognitive load by showing users where they are without needing a full navigation menu. They're essential in e-commerce (Home / Category / Subcategory / Product), docs (Home / Guide / Topic / Article), and admin tools (Home / Module / Section / Record).

Searchers look for 'react breadcrumb', 'navigation trail component', and 'wayfinding'. This page answers with real hierarchies showing how breadcrumbs work with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) back actions, links, and Next.js routing.

featureItems.title: Semantic HTML.
featureItems.description: Uses nav with aria-label and ol list markup so screen readers announce the breadcrumb as navigation.

featureItems.title: Custom Separators.
featureItems.description: Change the separator from / to >, →, |, or custom icons. Define the separator once and apply across all items.

featureItems.title: Responsive Truncation.
featureItems.description: Show a BreadcrumbEllipsis (...) when paths are long on mobile, collapsing intermediate pages and revealing them on click.

featureItems.title: Link and Page Elements.
featureItems.description: BreadcrumbLink is clickable; BreadcrumbPage is the current page (non-interactive), styled differently to show position.

featureItems.title: Icon Support.
featureItems.description: Add Lucide icons before the first item or next to separators for visual enhancement, especially in docs or admin contexts.

faqs.question: When should I use a breadcrumb in my app?
faqs.answer: Use breadcrumbs in hierarchical structures: e-commerce (category > product), docs (topic > article), admin tools (module > record). Avoid breadcrumbs in flat navigation like a blog.

faqs.question: What separator character should I use?
faqs.answer: / is standard and widely recognized. > is modern and directional. / suits e-commerce; > suits docs. Choose one and keep it consistent.

faqs.question: How do I handle long breadcrumb paths on mobile?
faqs.answer: Use BreadcrumbEllipsis to collapse intermediate items. Show Home > ... > Current on small screens, and expand to the full path on click or hover.

faqs.question: Should every item in a breadcrumb be clickable?
faqs.answer: All but the last (current page) should be clickable links. The current page is non-interactive to show users where they are.

faqs.question: Do breadcrumbs improve SEO?
faqs.answer: Semantic breadcrumb markup (nav, ol, link elements) helps search engines understand site structure. Include schema.org BreadcrumbList JSON-LD for rich snippets.

faqs.question: Can I add icons to breadcrumbs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Add small icons (home icon before Home, category icons) to enhance meaning, but avoid visual clutter. Icons should reinforce, not replace, text.

faqs.question: How are ReUI breadcrumb components different from a code snippet?
faqs.answer: They show breadcrumbs in real product pages, docs, and admin tools so you evaluate separators, responsive behavior, and how they integrate with page headers and navigation menus.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Place breadcrumbs at the top of a page, above the [Shadcn Card](/components/card) or main heading. Pair with a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) with an icon for a Back action as an alternative.

Combine breadcrumbs with [Shadcn Navigation Menu](/components/navigation-menu) to provide both hierarchical and flat navigation options. Use breadcrumbs in [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) or [Shadcn Sheet](/components/sheet) on mobile to preserve space.

Add breadcrumbs to [Shadcn Table](/components/table) headers to show context for filtered data. In admin tools, pair breadcrumbs with [Shadcn Pagination](/components/pagination) to show the full path while navigating results.

button.description: Shadcn Button components: 61 free layouts with loading states and icon buttons. {{count}} accessible React buttons with Tailwind CSS and variants.
button.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn button components for primary actions, form submissions, destructive actions, and loading states. Button is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI stacks and fully aligned with Shadcn Create styling.

content.title: Shadcn Button: Primary Actions & Form Submission
summary: Shadcn Button is a clickable element for actions, form submission, and navigation. It follows Radix UI composition conventions and Base UI-style semantic HTML, with full compatibility for Shadcn Create styling.
It comes in multiple variants (primary, secondary, destructive, ghost, outline) with optional loading and disabled states, supporting icons, text, or both.

ReUI lists button components inside forms, toolbars, cards, and dialogs so you evaluate hierarchy, spacing, and icon placement next to other components. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams use it, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Button?
paragraphs: A button is the primary interactive element for actions, form submission, and navigation. Shadcn Button composes native HTML button elements with Tailwind variants for color, size, and state so you get consistent styling without CSS battles.

Components here show primary buttons (blue, full-width), secondary buttons (outline, lower priority), destructive buttons (red, delete), and icon buttons. They help you choose variants, decide on text vs. icon, and judge spacing on mobile.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Button?
paragraphs: Buttons are the fundamental interaction element in apps. Consistent styling across variants (primary, secondary, destructive) helps users scan for actions without confusion.

Searchers look for 'react button component', 'form submit button', and 'button variants'. This page answers with real forms, toolbars, and dialog examples showing how buttons pair with loading states, icons, and disabled states.

featureItems.title: Multiple Variants.
featureItems.description: Primary (default), secondary (lower priority), destructive (red, delete), ghost (minimal), outline (bordered), and link variants for different contexts.

featureItems.title: Size Variants.
featureItems.description: sm, md, lg for consistency across density levels and screen sizes, making touch targets at least 44px on mobile.

featureItems.title: Loading State.
featureItems.description: Disable the button and show a spinner while a request is pending, paired with Lucide's Loader2 icon.

featureItems.title: Icon Support.
featureItems.description: Place icons (from Lucide) before or after text, or render icon-only buttons with accessible labels.

featureItems.title: Disabled State.
featureItems.description: Visually distinct disabled button with cursor-not-allowed, preventing clicks and showing why interaction is blocked.

faqs.question: What button variant should I use for the primary action?
faqs.answer: Use the default (blue) variant for the main action users should take. Secondary variant for alternative actions. Destructive (red) for delete or irreversible actions only.

faqs.question: Should buttons be full-width or fixed size?
faqs.answer: Full-width on mobile and in forms for easier touch targeting. Fixed-width in toolbars and inline contexts. Match your layout and touch target guidelines (44px minimum).

faqs.question: How do I show a loading state on a button?
faqs.answer: Disable the button, show a spinner icon, and optionally change text to 'Saving...' or 'Loading...'. Pair with a disabled prop to prevent duplicate clicks.

faqs.question: Can I use a button as a link?
faqs.answer: Yes, use the link variant or render a button-styled link element. In Next.js, use Link component with asChild prop from Radix Slot.

faqs.question: What's the difference between secondary and ghost buttons?
faqs.answer: Secondary is outlined with moderate contrast. Ghost is minimal, low-contrast, used sparingly in toolbars. Secondary works better for alternate actions.

faqs.question: How do I handle button focus states for accessibility?
faqs.answer: Shadcn applies focus-visible so keyboard users see a ring. Ensure sufficient contrast (AA standard) and test with keyboard navigation.

faqs.question: How are ReUI button components different from isolated component demos?
faqs.answer: They show buttons inside forms, dialogs, toolbars, and cards so you evaluate hierarchy, spacing, and icon placement in realistic contexts.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Use primary [Shadcn Button](/components/button) inside [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) or [Shadcn Alert Dialog](/components/alert-dialog) for primary actions, paired with secondary button for cancel.

Combine buttons with [Shadcn Input](/components/input) and [Shadcn Field](/components/field) in forms for submit and reset. Show buttons in [Shadcn Card](/components/card) headers or footers for actions on that card's content.

Group buttons in a [Shadcn Button Group](/components/button-group) for segmented controls or related actions. Use icon buttons with [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) in toolbars for compact, accessible control.

button-group.description: Shadcn Button Group components for toggle buttons and multi-select groups. {{count}} free Tailwind CSS layouts with accessible keyboard navigation.
button-group.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn button-group components for segmented controls, toolbar actions, and view switchers. Button Group is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI stacks and fully aligned with Shadcn Create styling.

content.title: Shadcn Button Group: Segmented Controls & Toggles
summary: Shadcn Button Group is a set of related buttons grouped together for segmented selection (like tabs) or toolbar-style actions. It follows Radix UI composition conventions and Base UI-style semantics, with full compatibility for Shadcn Create styling.
It displays buttons side-by-side or vertically, often with one selected state, used for view toggles, filtering, or tool selection without opening a dropdown.

ReUI lists button-group components inside dashboards, toolbars, and filter headers so you evaluate sizing, selection states, and icon placement next to real content. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams use it, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Button Group?
paragraphs: A button group is a row or column of related buttons, often used as a segmented control where clicking one button selects it and deselects others. Shadcn Button Group composes multiple [Shadcn Button](/components/button) instances with shared state management.

Components here show list/grid view toggles, time period selectors (Day / Week / Month), sorting options (Newest / Popular), and toolbar icon buttons. They help you choose layout, decide on icons, and manage focus states.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Button Group?
paragraphs: Segmented controls save space compared to dropdowns and keep all options visible. They're essential in dashboards for view toggles, filters, and tool selection where users need quick access without opening a menu.

Searchers look for 'react segmented control', 'button group component', and 'toggle button group'. This page answers with real dashboards and toolbars showing how button groups pair with icons and manage selection state.

featureItems.title: Segmented Selection.
featureItems.description: Only one button selected at a time (radio-style) or multiple (checkbox-style), with visual feedback on active state.

featureItems.title: Icon and Text.
featureItems.description: Render buttons with icons only, text only, or both. Icons pair with tooltips for accessibility.

featureItems.title: Size Consistency.
featureItems.description: All buttons in the group match size and spacing for visual alignment and easy scanning.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Navigation.
featureItems.description: Tab moves focus between buttons. Arrow keys navigate within the group when using the radiogroup role.

featureItems.title: Mobile Responsive.
featureItems.description: Stack vertically on small screens or shrink padding to fit. Touch targets remain 44px+ minimum.

faqs.question: Should I use button-group or tabs?
faqs.answer: Button groups for quick toggles and filters. Tabs for switching between major sections or views. Button groups feel lighter; tabs feel more structural.

faqs.question: Can a button group have more than 5 buttons?
faqs.answer: Yes, but watch readability on mobile. After 3-4 buttons, consider a dropdown or filtering panel instead to avoid wrapping and crowding.

faqs.question: How do I show which button is selected?
faqs.answer: Use the primary variant for the active button and secondary or outline for inactive buttons. Add a visual indicator (background, border, color) that matches your design tokens.

faqs.question: Should button groups be keyboard accessible?
faqs.answer: Yes. Test Tab and arrow key navigation. Use the radiogroup role (aria-checked) for single-select and the group role for multi-select.

faqs.question: How are ReUI button-group components different from a code snippet?
faqs.answer: They show button groups in dashboards and toolbars with real content and spacing context, not isolated component demos.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Use button groups above [Shadcn Table](/components/table) or [Shadcn Card](/components/card) for view switching (List / Grid / Kanban). Pair with [Shadcn Dropdown Menu](/components/dropdown-menu) for overflow actions when options exceed ~4.

Combine button groups with [Shadcn Input](/components/input) filters on the same toolbar line. Use buttons with [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) for icon-only groups to explain each button's function.
In forms, use button groups for required selections (Gender, Role). Wrap in [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for labels and validation error display.

calendar.description: Shadcn Calendar component for date selection using react-day-picker library. {{count}} components for controlled dates and localization support.
calendar.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn calendar components for date selection, scheduling flows, and booking interfaces. Calendar is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI stacks and fully aligned with Shadcn Create styling.

content.title: Shadcn Calendar: Interactive Date Selection
summary: Shadcn Calendar displays a month or year grid for date selection, powered by React DayPicker. It follows Radix UI composition conventions and Base UI-style customization, with full compatibility for Shadcn Create styling.
It renders a clickable calendar grid with navigation, highlighting selected dates, and support for date ranges and disabled dates.

ReUI lists calendar components inside date pickers, booking flows, and filter panels so you evaluate month navigation, mobile sizing, and how it pairs with forms. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, key features, and integration strategies.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Calendar?
paragraphs: A calendar is an interactive grid showing days of a month, with clickable dates for selection. Shadcn Calendar wraps React DayPicker with Tailwind styling for a composable, accessible date picker.
Components here show single-date selection, date ranges (from/to), disabled dates, and month/year navigation. They help you choose layouts and integrate calendars with forms or popovers.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Calendar?
paragraphs: Date pickers are essential for booking, scheduling, filtering, and data range inputs. A calendar interface is faster and more reliable than typing dates manually.

Searchers look for 'react calendar', 'date picker component', and 'booking calendar'. This page answers with real forms and filters showing how calendars integrate with [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) and [Shadcn Input](/components/input).

featureItems.title: Single and Range Selection.
featureItems.description: Select one date or a date range (from/to). Pair with state to manage selection.

featureItems.title: Disabled Dates.
featureItems.description: Mark specific dates or date ranges as unavailable (weekends, past dates) to prevent selection.

featureItems.title: Month/Year Navigation.
featureItems.description: Move between months and years with previous/next buttons or dropdown selectors.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Accessible.
featureItems.description: Arrow keys navigate dates, Enter selects, Escape closes. Screen readers announce dates and selection state.

featureItems.title: Customizable Styling.
featureItems.description: Highlight today, selected date, or ranges with Tailwind classes for day, disabled, range start/end states.

faqs.question: Should I use a calendar or type an input?
faqs.answer: Calendar for booking, scheduling, and ranges where visual context helps. Text input for single past dates. Combine both:input with calendar popover:for flexibility.

faqs.question: How do I show a calendar in a popover or modal?
faqs.answer: Wrap [Shadcn Calendar](/components/calendar) inside [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover). On selection, close the popover and update the linked [Shadcn Input](/components/input).

faqs.question: Can I disable weekends or past dates?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use the disabled prop or a disabledDates array. Filter out unwanted dates before rendering.

faqs.question: How do I show a date range calendar?
faqs.answer: Render two calendars side-by-side or one with range selection. Track from/to state and highlight the range between them.

faqs.question: How are ReUI calendar components different from a code example?
faqs.answer: They show calendars inside booking forms, date range filters, and mobile popovers, not isolated storybook components.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap [Shadcn Calendar](/components/calendar) in a [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) triggered by an [Shadcn Input](/components/input) field. On date selection, populate the input and close the popover.
Use calendars in [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for form validation and error display. Pair with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for 'Clear' or 'Today' actions.
In booking flows, show two calendars (from/to) inside a [Shadcn Card](/components/card). Display availability or pricing on hover using [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip).

card.description: Shadcn Card components: 21 free layouts for content containers in React apps. {{count}} clickable cards and skeletons with responsive Tailwind CSS.
card.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn card components for dashboard widgets, product cards, and content containers. Card is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI stacks and fully aligned with Shadcn Create styling.

content.title: Shadcn Card: Flexible Content Containers
summary: Shadcn Card is a container primitive for grouping related content with consistent spacing and borders. It follows Radix UI composition conventions and Base UI-style flexibility, with full compatibility for Shadcn Create styling.
It renders with optional header, footer, title, description, and action elements, using flexbox to arrange content without rigid structure.
