ReUI lists card components inside dashboards, product grids, and list views so you evaluate density, actions, and how cards pair with images, badges, and tables. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Card?
paragraphs: A card is a rectangular container with consistent padding, borders, and background color used to group related content. Shadcn Card composes CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, and CardFooter so you can build flexible layouts.

Components here show stat cards (KPI + chart), product cards (image + title + price + button), user cards (avatar + name + role + action), and list cards (checkbox + text + actions). They help you choose layouts and understand density.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Card?
paragraphs: Cards create visual hierarchy and make scanning easier. Dashboards, product grids, and list views use cards to separate content blocks and reduce cognitive load.
Searchers look for 'react card component', 'dashboard card', and 'product card layout'. This page answers with real dashboards and grids showing how cards pair with images, buttons, and metadata.

featureItems.title: Flexible Composition.
featureItems.description: CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent, CardFooter are optional:compose only what you need.

featureItems.title: Optional Actions.
featureItems.description: Add action buttons or menus in CardHeader or CardFooter for delete, edit, or primary CTA.

featureItems.title: Image Support.
featureItems.description: Place [Shadcn Aspect Ratio](/components/aspect-ratio) with image at the top for product or preview cards.

featureItems.title: Hover and Focus States.
featureItems.description: Add cursor-pointer, shadow-lg on hover, and focus rings for interactive cards and links.

featureItems.title: Responsive Density.
featureItems.description: Cards scale padding and text size on mobile; use grid to stack into single-column layouts.

faqs.question: When should I use a card vs. a list item or table row?
faqs.answer: Cards for rich content (images, multiple fields, actions). List items for simple text. Tables for tabular data. Cards are heavier; use them when space and presentation matter.

faqs.question: Should cards have a click action?
faqs.answer: Yes, if they're selectable or link to detail pages. Make the entire card clickable with cursor-pointer. Use nested [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for secondary actions (not the whole card).

faqs.question: How should I handle card actions?
faqs.answer: Put primary action (view, edit, select) at card level or in CardFooter. Secondary actions (delete, share) in a menu or CardHeader. Keep action affordance clear.

faqs.question: Can I make cards horizontally scrollable on mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use overflow-x-auto and set flex basis for consistency. Pair with snap-scroll for better UX.

faqs.question: How are ReUI card components different from code snippets?
faqs.answer: They show cards in grids, lists, and dashboards with real content and spacing, not isolated components.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Place [Shadcn Aspect Ratio](/components/aspect-ratio) with images inside CardHeader for product or gallery cards. Use [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for status or category overlays.
Add [Shadcn Button](/components/button) in CardFooter for actions. Use [Shadcn Dropdown Menu](/components/dropdown-menu) for overflow menus with delete, edit, and share.
Combine cards with [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) for bulk selection rows. Wrap cards in [Shadcn Sheet](/components/sheet) or [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) on mobile for detail views.

carousel.description: Shadcn Carousel components using Embla Carousel. Touch-friendly slides with autoplay, thumbnails, dots navigation, vertical orientation, and responsive.
carousel.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn carousel components using Embla Carousel. Covers single item, overlay thumbnail navigation, dots with content overlays, vertical orientation, multiple visible items, responsive basis, autoplay plugin, centered slide alignment, custom spacing, images with overlays, and thumbnail navigation.

content.title: Shadcn Carousel: Embla-Powered Slides and Galleries
summary: Shadcn Carousel is built on Embla Carousel: the most widely used React carousel library: wrapped in shadcn-compatible primitives. Embla handles touch, pointer, and keyboard scroll snapping, slide transitions, and plugin architecture without prescribing visual styles.

Eleven components cover basic single-item carousel, overlay thumbnail navigation, dots navigation with content overlays, vertical orientation, multiple visible items, responsive slide basis, autoplay with the `embla-carousel-autoplay` plugin, centered slide alignment, custom inter-slide spacing, images with overlays, and thumbnail strip navigation.
The sections below cover the Embla foundation, real use cases in product and marketing pages, feature details, and composition guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Carousel?
paragraphs: Shadcn Carousel wraps Embla Carousel with shadcn-compatible markup and Tailwind styling. Embla Carousel provides the scroll-snapping engine: it tracks pointer and touch velocity, snaps to the nearest slide on release, handles loop mode, and exposes a plugin API for autoplay, lazy loading, and class names.

ReUI components show the Carousel in realistic contexts: product image galleries with thumbnail strips, marketing hero slideshows with dots navigation, vertical feature carousels, and responsive grids that show 1, 2, or 3 slides depending on container width.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Carousel?
paragraphs: Image galleries, product photo viewers, testimonial sliders, feature showcases, and media players all need a touch-friendly, keyboard-accessible slide container. Embla Carousel is the de facto standard for React carousels in 2024–2025: it is lightweight (6 kB gzipped), framework-agnostic at the core, and supports CSS-native scroll behavior.

The autoplay plugin (`embla-carousel-autoplay`) handles play/pause on hover and focus, respects reduced-`motion` preferences, and integrates with the Embla event system. Pair with [Shadcn Aspect Ratio](/components/aspect-ratio) to keep slide images consistent across different screen sizes.

sections.title: Libraries and ReUI Carousel Primitives
paragraphs: ReUI Carousel imports `embla-carousel-react` for the core carousel engine and `embla-carousel-autoplay` for the optional autoplay plugin. It composes shadcn Card for slide containers and Button for Previous and Next navigation arrows.

The thumbnail navigation component renders a secondary mini-carousel below the main one: clicking a thumbnail scrolls the main carousel to the corresponding slide. Both carousels share the same Embla API via useEmblaCarousel.

featureItems.title: Embla Carousel Engine.
featureItems.description: Powered by Embla Carousel: the scroll-snapping library used by Vercel, Radix, and thousands of production React apps. Touch, pointer, and keyboard interactions with physics-based velocity snapping.

featureItems.title: Autoplay Plugin.
featureItems.description: `embla-carousel-autoplay` plugin adds configurable autoplay with delay, play/pause on hover and focus, stop-on-interaction, and loop mode. Respects prefers-reduced-`motion` by pausing on reduced-`motion` media query.

featureItems.title: Thumbnail Navigation.
featureItems.description: Thumbnail strip component renders a synchronized mini-carousel below the main one. Clicking a thumbnail scrolls the main carousel. Both carousels use useEmblaCarousel and share scroll state via the Embla API.

featureItems.title: Dots Navigation with Overlays.
featureItems.description: Dots navigation component renders indicator dots that snap position with the active slide. Content overlays sit inside the slide for text, badge, or CTA placement on top of images.

featureItems.title: Vertical Orientation.
featureItems.description: Vertical carousel scrolls slides top to bottom. Useful for mobile-first feature lists, vertical story viewers, and stacked card animations.

featureItems.title: Multiple Visible Items.
featureItems.description: Multi-item carousel shows 2–4 slides simultaneously with gap spacing. Slide basis adapts to the number of visible items. Responsive basis variant changes the visible count at different container widths.

featureItems.title: Centered Slide Alignment.
featureItems.description: Centered alignment mode snaps to the slide center rather than the start edge. Used for carousels where partial peek of adjacent slides on both sides is the intended visual treatment.

featureItems.title: Keyboard and Touch Accessible.
featureItems.description: Keyboard users navigate between slides with arrow keys. Touch users swipe with native velocity snapping. Previous and Next buttons are shadcn Buttons with accessible labels.

faqs.question: What library does shadcn Carousel use?
faqs.answer: Embla Carousel (`embla-carousel-react`). It is the most widely adopted React carousel library, used by Vercel's own design system and Radix UI examples. It provides touch-first scroll snapping, a clean plugin API, and weighs under 6 kB gzipped.

faqs.question: How does autoplay work?
faqs.answer: Install `embla-carousel-autoplay` and pass it to the plugins prop of useEmblaCarousel. Configure delay (default 4000ms), stopOnInteraction (pause on user touch/click), and stopOnMouseEnter (pause on hover). The plugin respects prefers-reduced-`motion` by not advancing slides when the media query matches.

faqs.question: Can I show multiple slides at once?
faqs.answer: Yes. Set flex-basis on each slide element to control how many are visible: basis-1/2 shows 2, basis-1/3 shows 3. Use gap utilities for spacing between slides. The responsive basis component changes basis at different breakpoints to show 1 on mobile and 3 on desktop.

faqs.question: How does thumbnail navigation work?
faqs.answer: Create two useEmblaCarousel instances: one for the main carousel, one for the thumbnail strip. Use the Embla scrollTo API to synchronize them: clicking a thumbnail calls mainEmbla.scrollTo(index); main scroll events update the active thumbnail via onSelect.

faqs.question: Does the carousel work on touch screens?
faqs.answer: Yes. Embla Carousel uses pointer events to handle touch, stylus, and mouse drag natively. It calculates release velocity and snaps to the nearest slide using physics-based momentum: no CSS scroll-snap is needed.

faqs.question: How do I make the carousel accessible for keyboard users?
faqs.answer: Previous and Next buttons are shadcn Buttons with descriptive aria-labels. The slide container receives tabIndex={0} for keyboard focus. Arrow key navigation between slides fires the Embla scroll API. ReUI components include all of these attributes.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap carousel slides in [Shadcn Aspect Ratio](/components/aspect-ratio) to enforce consistent image proportions across different slide content sizes. Use [Shadcn Card](/components/card) as the slide container for testimonials, product cards, and content slides.

Pair with [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for slide labels and category chips overlaid on images, and [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for Previous, Next, and CTA actions inside slides.

Use [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) while carousel images load to prevent layout shift on initial render. For static image galleries without slide interaction, consider [Shadcn Aspect Ratio](/components/aspect-ratio) in a CSS grid layout instead.

chart.description: Shadcn Chart components powered by Recharts and Motion. Bar, area, line, pie, and radar charts with animations, gradients, and responsive layouts. 25 production.
chart.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn chart components built on Recharts and Motion. Covers bar, vertical bar, area, step area, stacked area, line, forecast, donut, pie, radar, and 3D isometric chart types with gradient fills, animation, stripe component overlays, glowing markers, and crosshatch textures.

content.title: Shadcn Chart: Recharts and Motion Data Visualization
summary: ReUI Chart wraps Recharts: the composable charting library for React: and Motion for animation, surfacing production-ready chart components inside shadcn-compatible containers. All charts use design tokens from your shadcn theme so colors, radius, and typography stay consistent with the rest of the UI.

Twenty-five components span bar, vertical bar, stacked area, step area, line, forecast, donut, multi-dataset pie, radar, and 3D isometric layouts with visual treatments including gradient fills, stripe component overlays, crosshatch textures, glowing dot markers, and animated entrance transitions.
The sections below cover the library stack, chart type catalogue, visual feature set, and how charts compose with Card, Badge, and Skeleton.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Chart?
paragraphs: ReUI Chart is a custom shadcn component built on Recharts and Motion. Recharts handles the SVG rendering, scales, axes, tooltips, and legends. Motion adds entrance animation via animate props. The component exposes the Recharts API without hiding it: you can pass any Recharts prop through to customize ticks, domains, reference lines, or custom tooltips.

Components show charts inside shadcn Card containers with headers, stat badges, and loading skeleton states: not isolated chart playgrounds. This lets you evaluate how a donut chart with center stats, a forecast line chart with a shaded zone, or a 3D isometric bar chart reads inside a real analytics dashboard.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Chart?
paragraphs: Analytics dashboards, SaaS metrics pages, financial summaries, and KPI reports all need charts that match the design system. Raw Recharts is powerful but requires manual token mapping, container sizing, and animation setup. ReUI Chart components provide these out of the box, composing directly with shadcn Card and using CSS variables from your Tailwind theme for colors.

Recharts is React-first, tree-shakeable, and SVG-based: it renders server-side for initial paint and hydrates on the client for interactivity. Motion adds smooth entrance animations without a large bundle penalty. Pair with [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) for loading states and [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for metric labels on chart cards.

sections.title: Libraries and ReUI Chart Primitives
paragraphs: ReUI Chart imports Recharts for SVG chart rendering and Motion for entrance animations. Charts render inside shadcn Card with Badge for metric headers and Skeleton for loading states.

Visual treatments: gradient fills, stripe components, crosshatch textures, and glowing effects: are implemented as SVG defs (linearGradient, component) inside the Recharts ResponsiveContainer. No external image assets required.

featureItems.title: Recharts SVG Rendering.
featureItems.description: All charts use Recharts for composable, React-first SVG output. ResponsiveContainer handles resize, custom tooltips use Recharts' render props, and chart state is plain React state.

featureItems.title: Motion Entrance Animations.
featureItems.description: Charts animate in on mount using Motion. Bar and line charts grow from the baseline; donut charts rotate from 0 to their final arc. All animations are GPU-composited and respect prefers-reduced-`motion`.

featureItems.title: Bar and Vertical Bar Charts.
featureItems.description: Standard and vertical bar chart variants with animated monochrome, duotone gradient, diagonal stripe, dotted component, 3D gradient, and 3D isometric visual treatments.

featureItems.title: Area Charts with Component Fills.
featureItems.description: Gradient area, stacked area, step area, and forecast line charts with visual fills: stripe overlay, crosshatch component, dotted fill, and glowing dot markers on data points.

featureItems.title: Donut and Pie Charts.
featureItems.description: Donut chart with center total, donut with center stats, multi-dataset pie with stripe components, active segment donut, and Lighthouse scores radial with labels: covering the full range of part-to-whole visualization needs.

featureItems.title: Radar Charts.
featureItems.description: Radar chart with gradient fill and filled radar with glowing stroke: useful for performance profiling, skill matrices, and competitive comparison dashboards.

featureItems.title: Design Token Integration.
featureItems.description: All chart colors, border radius, and font sizes reference CSS variables from your Tailwind and shadcn theme. Shadcn Create style options propagate to chart surfaces automatically.

featureItems.title: Responsive and Accessible.
featureItems.description: Recharts ResponsiveContainer adapts to any container width. Tooltips are keyboard-accessible, and charts include title and description attributes for screen readers.

faqs.question: Which chart library does ReUI Chart use?
faqs.answer: Recharts for SVG rendering and Motion for animations. Recharts is composable, React-first, and tree-shakeable. It renders SVG server-side and hydrates for interactivity on the client. Motion adds entrance animations without a large bundle cost.

faqs.question: What chart types are available?
faqs.answer: Bar (standard, vertical, monochrome, 3D, isometric), area (gradient, stacked, step, forecast), line, donut (center total, center stats, active segment), pie (multi-dataset, stripe components), radar (gradient fill, glowing stroke), and Lighthouse scores radial. 25 components in total.

faqs.question: How are gradient and component fills implemented?
faqs.answer: As SVG defs inside the Recharts ResponsiveContainer. Gradients use linearGradient elements; stripe, crosshatch, and dotted components use SVG component elements. No image assets are required: all fills render as pure SVG.

faqs.question: Can I customize chart colors to match my theme?
faqs.answer: Yes. All chart colors reference CSS variables from your Tailwind and shadcn theme. Change the chart-1 through chart-5 color tokens in your globals.css and all chart components update automatically.

faqs.question: Do charts animate on load?
faqs.answer: Yes. Motion animates chart entrance. Bar charts grow from the baseline, line and area charts draw from left to right, and donut charts rotate from zero. All animations respect prefers-reduced-`motion`.

faqs.question: Are charts responsive?
faqs.answer: Yes. Recharts' ResponsiveContainer observes its parent width and redraws the SVG at the correct size. Charts adapt to sidebar widths, card grids, and full-width dashboard sections without any additional resize code.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap charts in [Shadcn Card](/components/card) with a header showing metric title and [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for percentage change labels. Use [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) for the loading state while chart data fetches.
Pair charts with [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid) for a full analytics dashboard: charts show aggregate trends at the top and the grid shows the underlying row-level data below.

Use [Shadcn Tabs](/components/tabs) to switch between chart types (bar vs. area, weekly vs. monthly) and [Shadcn Select](/components/select) or [Shadcn Button Group](/components/button-group) for date range selectors that control the chart data window.

checkbox.description: {{count}} free shadcn checkbox components for form selection, multi-select lists. Capture multi-value input in React forms with Tailwind CSS-styled checkboxes.
checkbox.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn checkbox components for form validation, multi-select lists, terms and conditions, and bulk actions. Shadcn Checkbox is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Checkbox: Accessible Form Selection Controls
summary: Shadcn Checkbox is a toggle control built on Radix UI primitives that stays compatible with Base UI components for checked state, focus, and keyboard behavior, while using Tailwind-friendly styles. It is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options, so radius, fonts, and theme tokens from the create flow apply consistently to previews and exported UI.
It renders a labeled control that users check or uncheck, often paired with a label and help text to capture single or grouped selections in forms.

ReUI lists checkbox components inside realistic layouts so you can judge spacing, density, and integration with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) groups next to other form controls, not only a minimal story. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams adopt it, which features matter, and how it pairs with validation and accessibility rules.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Checkbox?
paragraphs: In UI terms, a checkbox is a toggle control that users click to set a binary state: checked or unchecked. Shadcn Checkbox follows the same accessibility expectations as Radix UI primitives and fits teams that also use Base UI-style unstyled building blocks, so you can mix components without fighting the stack.

Components here show single checkboxes, checkbox lists, bulk actions, and integration with form field labels and error states. They are meant to answer whether your product needs inline selections, a terms acceptance flow, or multi-select filters, with styling that respects Shadcn Create options end to end.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Checkbox?
paragraphs: Checkboxes are the native control for multi-select scenarios on the web. Teams use them for terms acceptance, filter lists, bulk actions on tables, and opt-in preferences. When users must select one or more items without radio-button constraints, checkboxes keep the intent clear.

Form validation becomes easier when you pair checkboxes with a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) group to show error states together. Use them alongside a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to submit selections or inside a [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid) for row selection. They also work well in [Shadcn Filters](/docs/components/base/filters) components to let users refine large datasets.

featureItems.title: Accessibility-Compliant.
featureItems.description: Shadcn Checkbox adheres to WAI-ARIA guidelines and Radix UI keyboard accessibility conventions, focus management, and screen reader compatibility out of the box.

featureItems.title: Controlled and Uncontrolled.
featureItems.description: Use defaultChecked for uncontrolled checkboxes or checked and onCheckedChange to control state from parent components, giving you flexibility in form architecture.

featureItems.title: Form Integration.
featureItems.description: Pair with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) and FieldLabel for proper layout, labeling, and error indication so validation feedback stays near the control.

featureItems.title: Indeterminate State.
featureItems.description: Support partial selection for parent-child or bulk action components where some but not all items are selected, improving UX for complex forms.

featureItems.title: Custom Styling.
featureItems.description: Fully customizable with Tailwind CSS and Shadcn Create tokens; adjust size, color, and shape to match your design system without modifying component logic.

faqs.question: When should I use a checkbox instead of a switch or radio button?
faqs.answer: Use checkboxes when users can select zero or more items from a set. Use radio buttons when only one choice applies. Use a [Shadcn Switch](/components/switch) for binary on/off toggles that enable or disable a feature, not selection lists.

faqs.question: How do I handle checkbox groups in forms?
faqs.answer: Wrap multiple [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) components inside a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) with FieldLabel and FieldDescription. Add the aria-invalid attribute to each checkbox and the data-invalid prop to the Field to show validation errors together.

faqs.question: What is an indeterminate checkbox state?
faqs.answer: Indeterminate occurs when a parent checkbox controls child items and some (but not all) are checked. Set the indeterminate prop to true to show a visual dash instead of a check, signaling partial selection. Clear it when all or none are selected.

faqs.question: Can I use uncontrolled checkboxes in React Hook Form?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use the form control to register the checkbox and let Hook Form manage state. The checkbox will work as an uncontrolled input if you do not override onCheckedChange; Hook Form binds to the underlying input value.

faqs.question: How is Shadcn Checkbox different from a native HTML checkbox?
faqs.answer: Shadcn Checkbox is a styled, accessible wrapper around Radix UI primitives. It provides consistent theming with Tailwind and Shadcn Create while maintaining semantic HTML and full keyboard support. Use it for design consistency across your app.

faqs.question: Is the checkbox accessible for screen readers without extra ARIA attributes?
faqs.answer: Yes. Radix UI handles roles and labeling automatically. Always wrap the checkbox in a label element or pair it with FieldLabel so screen readers announce the purpose. Avoid relying on placeholder text for labels.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap checkboxes inside a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) with FieldLabel, FieldDescription, and error states to compose accessible form groups. Use FieldGroup to stack multiple field-wrapped checkboxes together with consistent spacing.

Place [Shadcn Button](/components/button) actions next to or below checkbox groups so users can submit selections, clear all, or apply filters. Combine with a [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid) to add row-level checkboxes for bulk operations like delete or export.

Use checkboxes inside [Shadcn Collapsible](/components/collapsible) sections or [Shadcn Drawer](/components/drawer) modals to build advanced [Shadcn Filters](/docs/components/base/filters) that narrow down large datasets. Add a [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) to show active filter counts when checkboxes are selected.

collapsible.description: Shadcn Collapsible components for expandable sections. Single disclosure region with icons, badges, and nested support. Works in sidebars, file trees, and.
collapsible.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn collapsible components for sidebar navigation, expandable sections, file trees, and nested content. Shadcn Collapsible is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Collapsible: Single Expandable Panel Trigger
summary: Shadcn Collapsible is a single expandable/collapsible panel built on Radix UI primitives that stays compatible with Base UI components for disclosure, focus, and keyboard behavior, while using Tailwind-friendly styles. It is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options, so radius, fonts, and theme tokens from the create flow apply consistently to previews and exported UI.
It renders a trigger element and a content region that users toggle open and closed, often paired with nested collapsibles to build file trees or hierarchical navigation.

ReUI lists collapsible components inside realistic layouts so you can judge nesting depth, icons, and density next to a [Shadcn Sidebar](/components/sidebar) or navigation chrome, not only a minimal story. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams adopt it, which features matter, and how it pairs with other primitives.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Collapsible?
paragraphs: In UI terms, a collapsible is a single disclosure region: a trigger element that toggles a panel open and closed. Shadcn Collapsible follows the same accessibility expectations as Radix UI primitives using aria-expanded, aria-controls, and keyboard interaction, and fits teams that also use Base UI-style unstyled building blocks.

Components here show single collapsibles, nested hierarchies for file trees, and integration with icons and badges. They are meant to answer whether your product needs expandable help text, a navigation menu with subgroups, or a hierarchical file browser, with styling that respects Shadcn Create options end to end.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Collapsible?
paragraphs: Collapsibles reduce visual clutter when related content sits nested. Teams use them for hierarchical navigation, file and folder trees, advanced search filters, and expandable help sections. A single collapsible is lighter weight than an [Shadcn Accordion](/components/accordion) when you only need one disclosure region.

Nested collapsibles build file trees and category browsers that scale to hundreds of items without pagination. Pair collapsibles with a [Shadcn Sidebar](/components/sidebar) to collapse menu sections or use them inside a [Shadcn Drawer](/components/drawer) for mobile-friendly navigation. They also work well in [Shadcn Filters](/docs/components/base/filters) to show optional filter categories.

featureItems.title: Accessibility-Compliant.
featureItems.description: Shadcn Collapsible adheres to WAI-ARIA disclosure semantics with aria-expanded and keyboard support so assistive technologies announce expanded state and users navigate with Enter and Space.

featureItems.title: Controlled State.
featureItems.description: Use open and onOpenChange props to manage expanded/collapsed state from parent components, enabling you to sync with URL, local storage, or form state.

featureItems.title: Nestable Design.
featureItems.description: Build multi-level file trees and hierarchical navigation by nesting collapsibles, allowing deep category browsing while keeping each level compact.

featureItems.title: Icon and Badge Support.
featureItems.description: Trigger can include chevrons, folder icons, or status badges to signal expandable content and provide visual hierarchy in navigation and tree layouts.

featureItems.title: Smooth Animations.
featureItems.description: Height animations and smooth transitions signal disclosure state change, improving perceived responsiveness and visual feedback when users toggle panels.

faqs.question: When should I use collapsible instead of accordion?
faqs.answer: Use collapsible when you need a single expandable region or when you are nesting multiple levels. Use [Shadcn Accordion](/components/accordion) when you have multiple parallel sections and want only one open at a time, such as a FAQ or settings panel.

faqs.question: How do I build a file tree or nested navigation with collapsibles?
faqs.answer: Nest collapsible components recursively, each with its own trigger and content. Use open and onOpenChange to manage state. Render child items inside content, wrapping folders with nested collapsibles and leaf nodes as plain items or links.

faqs.question: Is collapsible keyboard accessible?
faqs.answer: Yes. Radix UI handles aria-expanded, focus management, and Enter/Space activation automatically. Ensure trigger text or icons are descriptive, and nest semantic heading elements when building file trees so screen readers understand the hierarchy.

faqs.question: Can I control collapsible state from a parent component?
faqs.answer: Yes. Pass open and onOpenChange props to make the collapsible controlled. Update open in parent state to expand or collapse programmatically, useful for synchronizing with URL or form state.

faqs.question: How do I style the open and closed states differently?
faqs.answer: Use the data-open attribute added to the trigger and content by Radix UI to apply conditional Tailwind styles. Target [data-open] to style the expanded state differently from closed.

faqs.question: How are ReUI collapsible components different from a minimal docs demo?
faqs.answer: ReUI shows collapsibles nested in sidebars, navigation menus, and file browsers so you see spacing, icon density, and interaction density next to real layout chrome. That is closer to production context than an isolated storybook block.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Nest collapsibles inside a [Shadcn Sidebar](/components/sidebar) to build collapsible navigation menus where each menu item can expand to show sublinks. Add a [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) to triggers to show notification counts or unread indicators.

Use collapsibles in a [Shadcn Drawer](/components/drawer) on mobile to provide hierarchical navigation that does not take up page space. Pair with a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) inside the collapsed content for actions like edit or delete.

Build advanced [Shadcn Filters](/docs/components/base/filters) by grouping filter controls inside collapsible categories, letting users hide irrelevant filter sections. Use [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) or [Shadcn Radio Group](/components/radio-group) inside for selection.

combobox.description: Shadcn Combobox components using Base UI. Multi-select with chips, grouped options, async search, and form integration. 20 production examples.
combobox.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn combobox components using `@base-ui/react` Combobox. Covers single select, multi-select with removable chips, non-removable chips, user tag creation, dialog-embedded combobox, grouped options with separators, custom item rendering, large list performance, clear button, auto-highlight, and form integration.

content.title: Shadcn Combobox: Multi-Select and Grouped Options
summary: ReUI Combobox is built on `@base-ui/react` Combobox: the headless multi-select primitive that handles listbox state, keyboard navigation, selection model, and WAI-ARIA combobox roles. It supports single and multi-select modes, chip display for selected values, and inline filtering.

Twenty components cover basic single-select, popup-rendered combobox, form integration, multi-select with removable chips, non-removable chips, invalid state, user tag creation, custom item rendering, dialog-embedded combobox, custom trigger icon, large list performance, grouped options with separators, icon addons, and disabled states.
The sections below cover the Base UI foundation, real use cases, feature details, and how Combobox composes with Dialog, Field, and Input Group.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Combobox?
paragraphs: ReUI Combobox is a custom shadcn component built on `@base-ui/react`'s Combobox primitive. It is distinct from the shadcn Autocomplete (optimized for single-value typed search) and from the Command palette (full keyboard-driven command interface). Combobox handles multi-select with chip display, user-defined tag creation, and grouped option lists: use cases that need a richer selection model than a basic autocomplete.

The component renders selected values as removable chips inside the input field, supports an invisible trigger mode for inline tag inputs, and embeds inside Dialog for modal selection flows. Twenty components show all major variants inside realistic form and dashboard layouts.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Combobox?
paragraphs: Multi-select is one of the most requested form controls in SaaS products: skill tags, team member assignment, category classification, permission role selection, and label management all need a searchable multi-select with chip display. `@base-ui/react` provides the headless foundation with full ARIA compliance; ReUI adds the Tailwind styling, chip removal, and the 20 production components developers actually need.

The large list performance component handles hundreds of options without virtualization: Base UI's internal filtering keeps renders minimal. For truly enormous lists, pair with [Shadcn Autocomplete](/docs/components/base/autocomplete) in async search mode. For full command palettes, use [Shadcn Command](/components/command).

sections.title: Libraries and ReUI Combobox Primitives
paragraphs: ReUI Combobox imports `@base-ui/react` for the headless Combobox primitive and Sonner for async operation feedback. It composes shadcn Input Group, Button, Dialog, Field, Avatar, and Item for chip display and layout.
The invisible combobox variant uses a transparent trigger for inline tag inputs in chat, task, and assignment interfaces where the control should blend into the surrounding text layout.

featureItems.title: Base UI Combobox Primitive.
featureItems.description: Powered by `@base-ui/react` Combobox. Full WAI-ARIA multi-select combobox with listbox, option, and group roles, active-descendant management, and keyboard navigation.

featureItems.title: Multi-Select with Removable Chips.
featureItems.description: Selected values display as removable chip badges inside the input. Backspace removes the last chip; click × removes individual selections. The chip list scrolls horizontally in constrained containers.

featureItems.title: Non-Removable and Locked Chips.
featureItems.description: Chips can be rendered without a remove button for locked selections: useful for required team members, non-removable system tags, or read-only assigned categories.

featureItems.title: User Tag Creation.
featureItems.description: The user tags component allows creating new option values that are not in the predefined list: type a new tag and press Enter to add it. Useful for custom label, skill, and keyword fields.

featureItems.title: Dialog-Embedded Combobox.
featureItems.description: A combobox inside a shadcn Dialog for selection flows that need more space: long option lists, preview panels, or confirmation before applying selections.

featureItems.title: Grouped Options with Separators.
featureItems.description: Options group into labeled sections with optional Separator primitives between groups. Useful for categorized lists like country codes, department members, or mixed content types.

featureItems.title: Custom Item Rendering.
featureItems.description: Each option renders a custom React node: Avatar, Badge, description text, metadata. The custom rendering component shows user avatars with name and email inside each listbox option.

featureItems.title: Large List Performance.
featureItems.description: Base UI's internal filtering handles hundreds of options without full re-renders. The large list component shows 500+ options with no perceptible lag on filter input.

featureItems.title: Auto-Highlight and Clear Button.
featureItems.description: Auto-highlight immediately selects the first matching option as the user types. The clear button removes all selections with one click: both optional props on the same Combobox instance.

faqs.question: What library does ReUI Combobox use?
faqs.answer: `@base-ui/react` Combobox: the headless multi-select primitive from the Base UI library (by the MUI team). It handles ARIA roles, keyboard navigation, and selection state without any default styles. ReUI adds Tailwind styling and chip display.

faqs.question: How is Combobox different from Autocomplete?
faqs.answer: Combobox supports multi-select with chip display, user tag creation, and dialog embedding. Autocomplete is optimized for single-value typed search in form fields. Both use `@base-ui/react` but different primitives with different interaction models.

faqs.question: Can users create new tags not in the option list?
faqs.answer: Yes. The user tags component detects when the typed value doesn't match any existing option and presents a 'Create tag' option. Selecting it adds the new value to both the options list and the selected chips.

faqs.question: How does the dialog-embedded combobox work?
faqs.answer: The combobox trigger opens a shadcn Dialog containing the full combobox with a larger option list and optional preview. Selections are staged inside the dialog and applied on confirm, avoiding partial state updates in the form.

faqs.question: Does Combobox handle hundreds of options without lag?
faqs.answer: Yes. `@base-ui/react`'s internal filter runs on each keystroke against the options array. For 500+ options the filter completes in under 16ms in the large list component. For truly large remote datasets, switch to async search in Autocomplete with server-side filtering.

faqs.question: Can I show avatars and metadata inside each option?
faqs.answer: Yes. The custom item rendering component shows Avatar, name, and email inside each listbox option. Pass any React node as the option content: Base UI's active-descendant management works with any child structure.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap Combobox in [Shadcn Field](/components/field) with [Shadcn Label](/components/label) for accessible labeling and error display. Use [Shadcn Input Group](/components/input-group) when an icon or action button sits alongside the combobox trigger.

For single-value typed search in a form field use [Shadcn Autocomplete](/docs/components/base/autocomplete). For full command palettes with sections, actions, and shortcuts use [Shadcn Command](/components/command). For a lightweight single-select without search use [Shadcn Select](/components/select).

Embed Combobox inside [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) for modal selection flows, or inside [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) for inline panel selection. Use [Shadcn Sonner](/components/sonner) for async feedback when selections trigger server mutations.

command.description: Shadcn Command components for keyboard-driven interfaces. Command palettes, search filters, and navigation shortcuts with grouped commands and custom rendering.
command.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn command components for command palettes, quick-action menus, and searchable shortcuts. Shadcn Command is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Command: Keyboard-Driven Quick Action Palette
summary: Shadcn Command is a searchable command menu built on the cmdk library that stays compatible with Radix UI and Base UI components for keyboard navigation, focus management, and disclosure, while using Tailwind-friendly styles. It is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options, so radius, fonts, and theme tokens from the create flow apply consistently to previews and exported UI.
It combines a search input with grouped action items, shortcuts, and hierarchical navigation so users invoke commands by typing or clicking.

ReUI lists command components inside realistic layouts: command palettes in dialogs, quick-action toolbars, and nested command flows. This helps you judge density, grouping, and integration next to other UI chrome, not only a minimal story. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams adopt it, which features matter, and how it pairs with other primitives.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Command?
paragraphs: In UI terms, a command menu is a text input paired with a list of actions grouped by category. Shadcn Command follows accessibility conventions for focus, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support, and fits teams that also use Base UI-style unstyled building blocks.

Components here show simple command menus, grouped commands with icons, nested actions, and keyboard shortcuts. They are meant to answer whether your product needs a global search bar, a quick-action palette, or a hierarchical menu system, with styling that respects Shadcn Create options end to end.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Command?
paragraphs: Command palettes are the fastest way for power users to invoke actions without reaching for a mouse. Teams use them for global search, application shortcuts, and quick admin actions. Keyboard-first navigation improves efficiency and accessibility.

Pair command menus with a [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) to build a modal command palette triggered by keyboard shortcut like Cmd+K. Use [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) to show keyboard hints or command status. Combine with a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) or custom item renderers to trigger actions directly from the menu.

featureItems.title: Searchable Input.
featureItems.description: Type to filter commands in real time, reducing visual noise and focusing on relevant actions for the current task.

featureItems.title: Grouped Commands.
featureItems.description: Organize commands by category (File, Edit, View, Help) so related actions stay together and users build mental models of where to find commands.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Shortcuts.
featureItems.description: Display keyboard hints next to commands using CommandShortcut so users learn shortcuts and invoke commands faster without menu interaction.

featureItems.title: Fully Accessible.
featureItems.description: Full keyboard navigation with Arrow keys, Enter, Escape, and Tab, plus screen reader support for commands and shortcuts via cmdk and Radix UI components.

featureItems.title: Rich Item Content.
featureItems.description: Render custom content in command items including icons, descriptions, badges, or avatars to provide context and visual hierarchy.

faqs.question: What is the difference between a command palette and a combobox?
faqs.answer: A command palette invokes actions from a menu (like copy or delete), while a [Shadcn Combobox](/components/combobox) selects a value from a list (like a user or country). Palettes are action-oriented; comboboxes are selection-oriented.

faqs.question: How do I trigger a command palette with Cmd+K?
faqs.answer: Put the command inside a [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) and use a keyboard event listener (or library like cmdk) to detect Cmd+K. Toggle the dialog open state on that hotkey, and let the user type in the CommandInput.

faqs.question: Can I nest subcommands or hierarchical actions?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use CommandGroup to organize commands and render sub-items or secondary actions inside CommandItem. You can also render a secondary palette triggered by a parent command.

faqs.question: How do I display keyboard hints next to commands?
faqs.answer: Use the CommandShortcut sub-component inside each CommandItem to show the keyboard combo (e.g., Cmd+S for Save). Users see the hint and learn to use the shortcut next time.

faqs.question: Is command palette accessible for screen readers?
faqs.answer: Yes. cmdk and Radix UI handle roles, labels, and keyboard navigation. Ensure command text is descriptive and shortcuts are announced. Test with a screen reader to verify items are readable.

faqs.question: Can I use command palette on mobile?
faqs.answer: Command palettes are best on desktop where keyboard shortcuts shine. On mobile, consider a searchable menu or a [Shadcn Drawer](/components/drawer) with touch-friendly actions instead of keyboard-dependent shortcuts.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Place command palette inside a [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) triggered by Cmd+K hotkey. Use CommandInput for search and CommandItem to render actions that modify app state or navigate to routes.

Render [Shadcn Button](/components/button) or custom content inside command items to trigger secondary flows like opening a [Shadcn Drawer](/components/drawer) for detail editing or showing a [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) for quick actions.

Combine command with a [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) to show keyboard shortcuts and help users learn hotkeys. Use CommandGroup to organize commands and CommandSeparator to divide categories.

context-menu.description: Shadcn Context Menu components for right-click actions. Mobile-friendly menus with nested items, icons, and keyboard navigation. Production-ready.
context-menu.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn context-menu components for right-click interactions, bulk table actions, and destructive operations. Shadcn Context Menu is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Context Menu: Right-Click and Destructive Bulk
summary: Shadcn Context Menu is a right-click menu built on Radix UI primitives that stays compatible with Base UI components for focus, keyboard behavior, and disclosure, while using Tailwind-friendly styles. It is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options, so radius, fonts, and theme tokens from the create flow apply consistently to previews and exported UI.
It displays a menu of actions triggered by right-click or context-click on a target element, with support for checkboxes, radio groups, and nested submenus.

ReUI lists context-menu components inside realistic layouts: tables with row actions, file lists with bulk operations, and asset galleries. This helps you judge trigger zones, menu width, and placement next to other UI elements, not only a minimal story. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams adopt it, which features matter, and how it pairs with data-driven interactions.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Context Menu?
paragraphs: In UI terms, a context menu is a popup menu displayed near the cursor on right-click, showing actions specific to the clicked element. Shadcn Context Menu follows the same accessibility expectations as Radix UI primitives and fits teams that also use Base UI-style unstyled building blocks.

Components here show simple context menus, checkbox and radio items, nested submenus, and destructive actions with confirmation. They are meant to answer whether your product needs bulk row operations on a table, asset management shortcuts, or quick edits on a canvas, with styling that respects Shadcn Create options end to end.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Context Menu?
paragraphs: Context menus surface actions without cluttering primary UI. Teams use them for file operations (copy, move, delete), table row bulk actions, and drawing canvas interactions. Power users expect right-click to work and appreciate the speed.

Pair context menus with a [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid) to add copy, move, or delete actions on rows. Use [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) items for multi-select toggles and [Shadcn Radio Group](/components/radio-group) for exclusive choices like sort order. Show destructive actions with variant="destructive" styling and pair with a [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) for critical confirmations.

featureItems.title: Right-Click Trigger.
featureItems.description: Display menu on right-click or Shift+F10 on keyboard, providing a familiar interaction component for power users and accessibility.

featureItems.title: Checkbox Items.
featureItems.description: Use ContextMenuCheckboxItem to toggle boolean states like mute, pin, or archived status directly from the context menu.

featureItems.title: Radio Groups.
featureItems.description: Use ContextMenuRadioGroup and ContextMenuRadioItem for exclusive choices like sort order, view mode, or access level.

featureItems.title: Nested Submenus.
featureItems.description: Use ContextMenuSub to nest related actions, reducing menu length and organizing complex command trees.

featureItems.title: Destructive Actions.
featureItems.description: Style dangerous actions with variant="destructive" to warn users. Pair with a [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) or confirmation toast for irreversible operations.

faqs.question: Where should I place the ContextMenuTrigger in my HTML?
faqs.answer: Wrap the element you want to right-click (a table row, file item, or canvas area) with ContextMenuTrigger. The trigger region inherits the size and position of its children, so place it as close as possible to the target element.

faqs.question: How do I show a confirmation dialog for destructive context menu actions?
faqs.answer: Use the ContextMenuContent to render a ContextMenuItem that opens a [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) when clicked. Inside the dialog, confirm the destructive action before executing it.

faqs.question: Can I use context menu on mobile or touch devices?
faqs.answer: Context menus are best for desktop with right-click. On mobile, use a long-press gesture or a visible action menu inside each item. Some libraries detect long-press and show a context menu, but [Shadcn Drawer](/components/drawer) or [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) menus are more mobile-friendly.

faqs.question: How do I disable context menu for certain elements?
faqs.answer: Only wrap elements you want to show a context menu with ContextMenuTrigger. For others, the native context menu will appear. Use e.preventDefault() in a onContextMenu handler to suppress the menu entirely if needed.

faqs.question: Is context menu keyboard accessible?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use Shift+F10 to open the context menu on the focused element. Arrow keys navigate items, Enter activates, and Escape closes. Ensure trigger text is descriptive so screen readers announce the context menu purpose.

faqs.question: How do I keep context menu positions consistent across submenus?
faqs.answer: Radix UI handles positioning automatically. Use side="right" and align="start" props on ContextMenuSub to control submenu placement. Test across screen sizes to ensure menus do not overflow the viewport.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap rows or items in a [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid) with ContextMenuTrigger to add bulk actions like copy, move, or delete. Use CheckboxItem to toggle selection and RadioItem to change status.

Render [Shadcn Button](/components/button) actions inside ContextMenuContent for critical operations, and pair with a [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) to confirm destructive changes like delete or archive.
Use context menus inside file browsers or galleries with [Shadcn Collapsible](/components/collapsible) folders to provide quick access to move, copy, or delete operations on files and directories.

data-grid.description: Shadcn Data Grid components using TanStack React Table with virtual scrolling. Sortable, filterable columns, row selection, column resizing. 29 examples.
data-grid.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn data grid components built on TanStack Table v8 and TanStack Virtual. Covers pagination, sorting, column resizing, drag-and-drop rows and columns, row and column pinning, infinite scroll, CRUD, expandable rows, footer aggregates, and virtualized rendering: fully compatible with Shadcn Create style tokens.
keywords: shadcn table with sorting filtering pagination
data-grid.docsDescription: Explore ReUI datagrid examples with sorting, filtering, pagination, virtualization, row pinning, drag-and-drop, and infinite scroll.

content.title: Shadcn Data Grid: TanStack Table, Virtual, and DnD
summary: ReUI Data Grid is built on TanStack Table v8 and TanStack Virtual: the headless table and virtualization engines trusted by thousands of production React apps. It handles sorting, filtering, pagination, column sizing, pinning, selection, grouping, and virtual row rendering without dictating markup.

Components cover 29 real layouts including drag-and-drop rows and columns via @dnd-kit, local and remote infinite scroll, CRUD with Sonner feedback, expandable rows, sub-tables, footer aggregates, and dense or borderless visual variants.

ReUI shows every data grid component inside realistic dashboards and frame containers so you can evaluate density, sticky headers, and column controls next to real chrome: not an isolated sandbox. The sections below cover architecture, features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Data Grid?
paragraphs: ReUI Data Grid is a custom shadcn component built on TanStack Table v8 for state management and TanStack Virtual for row and column virtualization. It exposes the full TanStack Table API: columnDef, getCoreRowModel, getSortedRowModel, getFilteredRowModel, getPaginationRowModel, and more: while wrapping the markup in Tailwind-styled, accessible table primitives that follow shadcn conventions.

Components range from simple paginated lists to full CRUD interfaces with drag-and-drop column reordering via `@dnd-kit/sortable`, pinned rows and columns, expandable sub-tables, aggregate footers, loading skeletons, and both local and server-driven infinite scroll. Every layout is shown inside a realistic frame or dashboard container so you judge composition in context.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Data Grid?
paragraphs: Admin panels, analytics dashboards, CRM tables, audit logs, and billing history all need a table that scales with data. TanStack Table v8 handles thousands of rows without re-rendering the entire DOM: TanStack Virtual extends that to tens of thousands by only mounting visible rows. Together they let you ship a fast, accessible table without building sort state, filter logic, or pagination from scratch.

The DnD Kit integration adds drag-and-drop column reordering and row reordering that works with keyboard and pointer devices. Pair with [Shadcn Filters](/docs/components/base/filters) for faceted search, [Shadcn Pagination](/components/pagination) for standalone controls, and [Sonner](/components/toast) for async feedback on CRUD operations.

sections.title: Libraries and ReUI Data Grid Primitives
paragraphs: ReUI Data Grid imports `@tanstack/react-table` for the headless table engine, `@tanstack/react-virtual` for row virtualization, `@dnd-kit/core` and `@dnd-kit/sortable` for drag-and-drop, and Sonner for toast feedback. `class-variance-authority` handles variant styling across dense, bordered, striped, and borderless layouts.

The ScrollArea primitive wraps the table for overflow control. Frame containers group data grid components with toolbars, filter bars, and pagination into shippable dashboard blocks rather than bare table demos.

featureItems.title: TanStack Table Core.
featureItems.description: Powered by TanStack Table v8. Full access to getSortedRowModel, getFilteredRowModel, getPaginationRowModel, getGroupedRowModel, and getCoreRowModel: headless, typed, and framework-agnostic state.

featureItems.title: TanStack Virtual Row Virtualization.
featureItems.description: TanStack Virtual renders only visible rows in the DOM. Handles tens of thousands of rows without layout shift, supports overscan, and works with both fixed and dynamic row heights.

featureItems.title: Drag and Drop Rows and Columns.
featureItems.description: Built on `@dnd-kit/core` and `@dnd-kit/sortable`. Reorder rows or move columns with pointer and keyboard. Drag overlays prevent layout jump during active drags.

featureItems.title: Column Pinning.
featureItems.description: Pin columns to the left or right so identity columns (name, ID) and action columns (edit, delete) stay visible while users scroll wide datasets horizontally.

featureItems.title: Row Pinning.
featureItems.description: Sticky row support keeps summary rows, totals, or selected records fixed at the top or bottom of the visible viewport across scroll.

featureItems.title: Column Resizing.
featureItems.description: Drag column edges to resize width. Respects min and max constraints and persists across re-renders through TanStack Table column sizing state.

featureItems.title: Sortable Columns.
featureItems.description: Click-to-sort with multi-column sort support, directional indicators, and controlled or uncontrolled sort state. Integrates cleanly with server-side sort APIs via manualSorting.

featureItems.title: Column Visibility Controls.
featureItems.description: Show or hide columns at runtime via a dropdown panel. State is serializable for URL persistence with `nuqs` or localStorage, enabling shareable filtered views.

featureItems.title: Pagination and Infinite Scroll.
featureItems.description: Switch between client-side pagination, local infinite scroll, and remote infinite scroll. The remote component integrates with SWR, React Query, or Next.js Server Actions for cursor-based APIs.

featureItems.title: Row Selection and CRUD.
featureItems.description: Single and multi-row selection with indeterminate checkbox states. Full CRUD components with inline editing, confirm dialogs, row action menus, and Sonner toast feedback for async save and delete.

featureItems.title: Expandable Rows and Sub-Tables.
featureItems.description: Expand rows to reveal nested detail panels or full sub-tables: useful for order lines, log entries, invoice items, or hierarchical entity relationships.

featureItems.title: Footer Aggregates.
featureItems.description: Column totals, summary stats, and per-column aggregate footers for financial tables, inventory dashboards, and reporting views.

featureItems.title: Loading Skeleton.
featureItems.description: Column-aware skeleton state renders placeholder rows while data loads. Matches real row density to prevent layout shift and signal correct table structure before data arrives.

featureItems.title: Dense, Striped, and Borderless Layouts.
featureItems.description: Variant system built with `class-variance-authority`. Switch between default, dense, borderless, striped, and card-container layouts to match audit logs, dashboards, or data-heavy admin views.

featureItems.title: Sticky Header.
featureItems.description: Header row stays visible as users scroll through long datasets. Works with column pinning and column visibility controls for maximum navigability in wide tables.

sections.title: ReUI Data Grid: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Data Grid is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project: following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Data Grid is a comprehensive custom component not available in base shadcn/ui. It is built on `@tanstack/react-table` for headless table state, `@tanstack/react-virtual` for row virtualization, and `@dnd-kit/core` with `@dnd-kit/sortable` for accessible drag-and-drop. The component ships as 10 composable sub-files covering the table, DnD rows, DnD columns, virtual table, scroll area, column header, column filter, column visibility, and pagination: each independently importable.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling: only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version: API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version: API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: Does ReUI Data Grid support TanStack Virtual?
faqs.answer: Yes. ReUI Data Grid integrates `@tanstack/react-virtual` for row virtualization, allowing tables to render tens of thousands of rows without DOM bloat. Only visible rows are mounted, with configurable overscan for smooth scrolling.

faqs.question: Which table library does ReUI Data Grid use?
faqs.answer: TanStack Table v8 (`@tanstack/react-table`). It is headless and framework-agnostic, so you own the markup while TanStack handles sort, filter, pagination, grouping, and selection state. ReUI wraps it in Tailwind-styled shadcn-compatible primitives.

faqs.question: Can I drag and drop columns and rows in the data grid?
faqs.answer: Yes. Column reordering and row reordering use `@dnd-kit/core` and `@dnd-kit/sortable`. Both pointer and keyboard drag interactions are supported, and drag overlays prevent layout shift during active drags.

faqs.question: How does infinite scroll work with a remote API?
faqs.answer: The remote infinite scroll component uses a sentinel element observed by IntersectionObserver. When it enters the viewport, a next-page fetch fires. Pair with React Query or SWR for cursor-based APIs and set manualPagination: true on the TanStack Table instance.

faqs.question: Can I pin rows and columns at the same time?
faqs.answer: Yes. TanStack Table supports independent column and row pinning state. Pin identity columns left, action columns right, and keep summary or selected rows fixed at the top: all composable in the same table instance.

faqs.question: How do I persist column visibility and sort state in the URL?
faqs.answer: Pass column visibility and sort state to `nuqs` or serialize it into URL search params manually. TanStack Table state is plain objects so serialization is straightforward. ReUI components show the controlled state component you need to wire to URL persistence.

faqs.question: Does data grid work with Next.js App Router and Server Actions?
faqs.answer: Yes. Fetch data in Server Components and pass it as props to the client-side table wrapper. For CRUD, wire row actions to Server Actions or API routes: Sonner handles toast feedback on the client boundary.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Pair data grid with [Shadcn Filters](/docs/components/base/filters) to add a faceted filter panel above the table. Filters integrates with TanStack Table's column filter API and supports URL state via nuqs, so users can share filtered views.

Use [Shadcn Pagination](/components/pagination) as a standalone control below the table when you need custom pagination UI beyond the built-in component, and [Shadcn Select](/components/select) for page-size pickers. [Shadcn Button](/components/button) and [Shadcn Dropdown Menu](/components/dropdown-menu) wire naturally into row action columns.

Wrap data grid in a [Shadcn Frame](/docs/components/base/frame) to get a toolbar, filter bar, and pagination row as a single shippable dashboard block. Use [Shadcn Sonner](/components/sonner) for CRUD feedback and [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) or the built-in loading skeleton for data fetch states.

date-selector.description: Shadcn Date Selector components using React Day Picker and date-fns. Date range selection, single dates, disabled specific dates, locale support.
date-selector.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn date picker components for single-date selection, date ranges with presets, and calendar integration. Shadcn Date Picker is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Date Picker: Calendar Dates and Date Ranges
summary: Shadcn Date Picker is a calendar-based date selection control built on Radix UI and [Shadcn Calendar](/components/calendar) primitives that stays compatible with Base UI components for focus, keyboard behavior, and disclosure, while using Tailwind-friendly styles. It is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options, so radius, fonts, and theme tokens from the create flow apply consistently to previews and exported UI.
It combines a text input, popover trigger, and calendar grid so users select a single date or date range from a visual calendar or by typing.

ReUI lists date-picker components inside realistic forms and filters so you can judge spacing, preset buttons, and integration with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) labels next to other controls, not only a minimal story. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams adopt it, which features matter, and how it pairs with form validation.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Date Picker?
paragraphs: In UI terms, a date picker is a text input paired with a popover calendar that users click to select dates. Shadcn Date Picker follows the same accessibility expectations as Radix UI primitives and fits teams that also use Base UI-style unstyled building blocks.

Components here show single-date selection, date ranges with start and end inputs, and preset buttons for common ranges like "This Month" or "Last 7 Days". They are meant to answer whether your product needs a simple date field, a reporting period selector, or a complex date-range filter, with styling that respects Shadcn Create options end to end.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Date Picker?
paragraphs: Date pickers eliminate typing errors and reduce cognitive load compared to text inputs. Teams use them for event scheduling, booking systems, reporting filters, and financial transactions. A visual calendar helps users confirm the correct date.

Pair date pickers with a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for labels and error states. Use preset buttons inside a date-range picker to offer common periods like "Today", "Last 30 Days", or "This Year". Combine with [Shadcn Filters](/docs/components/base/filters) to let users narrow reports by date. Use inside a [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) for advanced search with multiple date constraints.

featureItems.title: Calendar Navigation.
featureItems.description: Browse months and years with arrow buttons, clicking day cells to select dates. Keyboard navigation with Arrow keys moves focus; Enter selects.

featureItems.title: Single and Range Selection.
featureItems.description: Select a single date or a date range with start and end pickers. Preset buttons allow quick selection of common periods.

featureItems.title: Date Input Field.
featureItems.description: Type or paste dates directly into an input field, which syncs with the calendar and updates the popover state.

featureItems.title: Disabled Dates.
featureItems.description: Disable specific dates, date ranges, or weekdays so users cannot select unavailable periods, useful for booking systems and availability calendars.

featureItems.title: Popover Integration.
featureItems.description: Calendar opens in a [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) that positions smartly near the input, adjusting placement to stay in viewport on small screens.

sections.title: ReUI Date Selector: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Date Selector is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project: following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Date Selector is a custom inline date picker component not available in base shadcn/ui. Built on `react-day-picker` for the calendar UI and `date-fns` for date formatting and locale support, it renders in a Popover, Dialog, or Dropdown Menu trigger depending on your layout needs.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling: only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version: API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version: API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: Should I use a date picker or a text input for dates?
faqs.answer: Use a date picker when users need visual confirmation or when a calendar context helps, such as event scheduling. Use a text input if the date is well-known (birthdate) or if you expect frequent direct typing. Combine both for flexibility.

faqs.question: How do I handle date ranges with preset buttons?
faqs.answer: Create preset buttons (Last 7 Days, This Month, Last Year) that update the date-range state. Clicking a preset sets the start and end dates and updates both input fields and the calendar view.

faqs.question: Can I disable certain dates or date ranges?
faqs.answer: Yes. Pass a disabled prop or disabledDates array to the [Shadcn Calendar](/components/calendar) component. Dates in the array will render as disabled and users cannot click them.

faqs.question: How do I integrate a date picker with form validation?
faqs.answer: Wrap the date picker inside a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) with FieldLabel, FieldDescription, and error states. Connect to your form library (React Hook Form, TanStack Form) using the field register or control.

faqs.question: Is the date picker accessible for screen readers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Radix UI handles roles, labels, and keyboard navigation automatically. Ensure the text input has a label and the [Shadcn Calendar](/components/calendar) dates are announced. Test with a screen reader to confirm navigation works.

faqs.question: How do I format the date display?
faqs.answer: Use `date-fns` or similar libraries to format the selected date. The calendar returns a Date object; convert it to a readable string using format(date, 'PPP') or your preferred format string.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap date picker inside a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) with FieldLabel, FieldDescription, and error states to create accessible form fields with proper labeling and validation feedback.

Use date pickers inside [Shadcn Filters](/docs/components/base/filters) to let users narrow reports or searches by date range. Combine with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to apply or reset filters.

Place preset buttons inside a [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) or at the bottom of a [Shadcn Calendar](/components/calendar) to offer quick selections for common periods like "Today", "Last Week", or "This Quarter".

dialog.description: Shadcn Dialog components for modal windows. Focus trapping, async forms, stacked dialogs, and scroll containment. Production-ready components.
dialog.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn dialog components for confirmation modals, form layouts, scrollable content, and overlay interactions. Shadcn Dialog is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Dialog: Modal Forms and Confirmation Overlays
summary: Shadcn Dialog is a modal overlay primitive built on Radix UI that stays compatible with Base UI components for focus management, keyboard behavior, and dismissal, while using Tailwind-friendly styles. It is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options, so radius, fonts, and theme tokens from the create flow apply consistently to previews and exported UI.
It displays content in a centered overlay that overlays the main page, blocking interaction with the page until the dialog is dismissed, and supporting full-page forms, confirmations, and alerting.

ReUI lists dialog components inside realistic page layouts so you can judge overlay opacity, width, and integration with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) actions next to other chrome, not only a minimal story. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams adopt it, which features matter, and how it pairs with form handling and validation.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Dialog?
paragraphs: In UI terms, a dialog is a modal overlay that demands focus and prevents interaction with the page until dismissed. Shadcn Dialog follows the same accessibility expectations as Radix UI primitives and fits teams that also use Base UI-style unstyled building blocks.

Components here show confirmation dialogs, form modals with long content, scrollable body with sticky header, and nested dialogs. They are meant to answer whether your product needs a simple yes/no confirmation, a multi-step form modal, or a full-screen content browser, with styling that respects Shadcn Create options end to end.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Dialog?
paragraphs: Dialogs focus user attention on a single task or decision. Teams use them for confirmations, critical settings changes, form submissions, and alerts. Unlike tooltips or popovers, dialogs block page interaction until resolved.

Pair dialogs with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for primary and secondary actions. Use inside to render [Shadcn Field](/components/field) groups for multi-field forms. Combine with a [Shadcn Alert Dialog](/components/alert-dialog) for destructive confirmations that require explicit acknowledgment. Use [Shadcn Command](/components/command) inside a dialog for command palettes triggered by Cmd+K.

featureItems.title: Focus Trap.
featureItems.description: Focus stays trapped inside the dialog until dismissed, preventing accidental interaction with page content behind the overlay.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Dismissal.
featureItems.description: Press Escape to close the dialog. Tab and Shift+Tab navigate within the dialog, ensuring keyboard-only users can interact fully.

featureItems.title: Scrollable Content.
featureItems.description: DialogContent supports scrolling when content exceeds viewport height. Header and footer can stick while body scrolls for better UX on mobile.

featureItems.title: Close Button.
featureItems.description: Optional built-in close button in the header. Hide with showCloseButton={false} if your dialog has action buttons instead.

featureItems.title: Overlay Control.
featureItems.description: Customize overlay opacity, blur, and color. Control whether clicking the overlay dismisses the dialog or pressing Escape only.

faqs.question: When should I use a dialog instead of a drawer or page navigation?
faqs.answer: Use a dialog for short, focused tasks like confirmations or single-step forms. Use a [Shadcn Drawer](/components/drawer) on mobile for slides-in side content. Use page navigation for longer workflows or when you want a back button.

faqs.question: How do I handle form submission in a dialog?
faqs.answer: Render your form fields inside DialogContent. Put [Shadcn Button](/components/button) actions (Submit, Cancel) inside DialogFooter. On form submission, update state and optionally close the dialog with open state or DialogClose.

faqs.question: Can I nest dialogs or show multiple dialogs at once?
faqs.answer: Yes, but avoid deep nesting. Radix UI stacks dialogs so the topmost one receives focus. Use a [Shadcn Drawer](/components/drawer) or [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) for secondary interactions instead of nested dialogs for better UX.

faqs.question: How do I scroll content inside a dialog on mobile?
faqs.answer: DialogContent supports scrolling when content exceeds viewport. Use DialogHeader and DialogFooter outside the scrollable area so they stick while body scrolls, improving mobile usability for tall forms.

faqs.question: Is the dialog accessible for screen readers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Radix UI handles focus management, roles, and keyboard navigation automatically. Ensure DialogTitle and DialogDescription are present and clear so screen readers announce the dialog purpose on open.

faqs.question: How do I prevent dialog dismissal for unsaved changes?
faqs.answer: Listen to onOpenChange and check if the form has unsaved changes. If so, show a confirmation dialog before allowing dismissal, or disable the close button and Escape until the form is submitted or reset.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
