integrationBody: Compose dialogs with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) groups, [Shadcn Button](/components/button) actions, and [Shadcn Alert](/docs/components/base/alert) for validation feedback. Use [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) for options and [Shadcn Radio Group](/components/radio-group) for exclusive choices.

Use [Shadcn Alert Dialog](/components/alert-dialog) instead of Dialog for destructive confirmations that require explicit user confirmation. It provides a stronger semantic meaning and forces consideration of the action.

Embed [Shadcn Command](/components/command) inside a dialog for command palettes triggered by Cmd+K. Pair with [Shadcn Tabs](/components/tabs) to organize dialog content into sections like "Settings", "Help", and "About".

drawer.description: Shadcn Drawer components for slide-out panels. Side and bottom drawers, snap points, scroll containment, focus management. Mobile-optimized components.
drawer.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn drawer components for mobile navigation, side panels, and slide-out content. Shadcn Drawer is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Drawer: Mobile Slide-Out Side Navigation Panel
summary: Shadcn Drawer is a slide-out panel built on Radix UI and Vaul that stays compatible with Base UI components for focus management, keyboard behavior, and dismissal, while using Tailwind-friendly styles. It is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options, so radius, fonts, and theme tokens from the create flow apply consistently to previews and exported UI.
It displays content in a side panel that slides in from the viewport edge, often used for mobile navigation, filters, or secondary content that should not interrupt page flow.

ReUI lists drawer components inside realistic responsive layouts so you can judge width, animation, and integration with mobile navigation next to desktop chrome, not only a minimal story. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams adopt it, which features matter, and how it pairs with responsive design.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Drawer?
paragraphs: In UI terms, a drawer is a slide-out panel that opens from the edge of the screen, usually the left or bottom. Shadcn Drawer follows the same accessibility expectations as Radix UI primitives and fits teams that also use Base UI-style unstyled building blocks.

Components here show navigation drawers for mobile, filter drawers for refining searches, and side panels for secondary content. They are meant to answer whether your product needs a hamburger menu on mobile, a filter sidebar, or a details panel, with styling that respects Shadcn Create options end to end.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Drawer?
paragraphs: Drawers save screen space on mobile and provide smooth animations that feel native. Teams use them for responsive navigation, filter panels, shopping carts, and secondary details. Swipe-to-close on mobile improves mobile UX.

Pair drawers with a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to open and close. Use inside for navigation links, filters, or forms. Combine with [Shadcn Collapsible](/components/collapsible) for nested menu sections. Use alongside a [Shadcn Sheet](/components/sheet) when you need both a drawer and a modal overlay in the same flow.

featureItems.title: Smooth Animation.
featureItems.description: Hardware-accelerated slide animation from viewport edge, providing native-feeling `motion` on mobile and desktop.

featureItems.title: Swipe to Close.
featureItems.description: Mobile users can swipe the drawer closed without tapping the close button, improving touch interaction and discoverability.

featureItems.title: Position Control.
featureItems.description: Open from left, right, top, or bottom. Configure direction to match your app layout and design expectations.

featureItems.title: Overlay Control.
featureItems.description: Optional overlay behind the drawer. Show or hide to adjust focus and context; overlays help mobile users understand that content is secondary.

featureItems.title: Responsive Width.
featureItems.description: Set drawer width as a percentage or fixed value. Use responsive classes to adjust for mobile, tablet, and desktop screen sizes.

faqs.question: When should I use a drawer instead of a dialog or popover?
faqs.answer: Use a drawer for mobile navigation or large side panels that need full height. Use a [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) for centered overlays and confirmations. Use a [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) for small, lightweight overlays that float near a trigger.

faqs.question: How do I make a responsive mobile menu with a drawer?
faqs.answer: Create a mobile-only button with a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) that opens the drawer on small screens. Use Tailwind responsive classes (hidden md:block) to hide on desktop and show a navigation bar instead.

faqs.question: Can I nest a form inside a drawer?
faqs.answer: Yes. Render form [Shadcn Field](/components/field) components inside DrawerContent. Use [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for submit and cancel actions. On form submission, close the drawer programmatically by setting the open state to false.

faqs.question: How do I handle swipe-to-close on mobile?
faqs.answer: Vaul, the library behind Shadcn Drawer, handles swipe detection automatically. Users swipe from the drawer edge toward the viewport edge to close. Configure the direction prop to ensure swipe direction matches animation.

faqs.question: Is the drawer accessible for screen readers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Radix UI and Vaul handle focus management, roles, and keyboard navigation (Escape to close). Ensure DrawerTitle and DrawerDescription are present so screen readers announce the drawer purpose on open.

faqs.question: How do I position the drawer on the right side?
faqs.answer: Vaul does not expose a side prop directly. Use CSS transforms or Tailwind translate utilities to position from the right. Alternatively, use [Shadcn Sheet](/components/sheet) which may offer more control over positioning.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Use drawer for mobile navigation by pairing with a hamburger menu [Shadcn Button](/components/button). Include navigation links and [Shadcn Collapsible](/components/collapsible) sections for nested menus inside the drawer.

Build filter drawers by placing [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) and [Shadcn Radio Group](/components/radio-group) controls inside DrawerContent. Add a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to apply filters and close the drawer.

Combine drawer with a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) group to create slide-out forms for editing or creating content on mobile. Use DrawerTitle and DrawerDescription for context, and pair with validation feedback.

dropdown-menu.description: Free shadcn dropdown menu components for button-triggered menus with checkboxes. Expose contextual React actions with Tailwind CSS-designed dropdown menus.
dropdown-menu.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn dropdown menu components for action buttons, checkbox toggles, radio selection, and keyboard shortcuts. Shadcn Dropdown Menu is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Dropdown Menu: Popover Actions and Selection
summary: Shadcn Dropdown Menu is a popover menu triggered by a button, built on Radix UI primitives that stays compatible with Base UI components for focus, keyboard behavior, and disclosure, while using Tailwind-friendly styles. It is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options, so radius, fonts, and theme tokens from the create flow apply consistently to previews and exported UI.
It displays a list of actions, checkboxes, or radio options that appear when a trigger button is clicked.

ReUI lists dropdown-menu components inside realistic layouts so you can judge trigger positioning, menu width, and integration with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) groups next to other chrome, not only a minimal story. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams adopt it, which features matter, and how it pairs with actions.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Dropdown Menu?
paragraphs: In UI terms, a dropdown menu is a popover menu triggered by clicking a button, showing a list of actions, toggles, or selections. Shadcn Dropdown Menu follows the same accessibility expectations as Radix UI primitives and fits teams that also use Base UI-style unstyled building blocks.

Components here show simple action menus, checkbox items for toggles, radio groups for exclusive choices, and nested submenus. They are meant to answer whether your product needs a button with quick actions, a settings menu, or a view-mode selector, with styling that respects Shadcn Create options end to end.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Dropdown Menu?
paragraphs: Dropdown menus surface secondary actions without cluttering the primary UI. Teams use them for user settings, view options, bulk actions, and export choices. They are faster to open than dialogs and keep users in context.

Pair dropdown menus with a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) and customize the button icon or label. Use DropdownMenuCheckboxItem for toggles like mute, pin, or archive. Use DropdownMenuRadioGroup for exclusive choices like sort order or view. Combine with keyboard shortcuts displayed via DropdownMenuShortcut to help users learn hotkeys.

featureItems.title: Trigger Button.
featureItems.description: Use any button as a trigger; the menu appears below or above depending on viewport space and align props.

featureItems.title: Checkbox Items.
featureItems.description: Use DropdownMenuCheckboxItem to toggle boolean states like mute, pin, or archived without leaving the menu.

featureItems.title: Radio Groups.
featureItems.description: Use DropdownMenuRadioGroup with DropdownMenuRadioItem for exclusive choices like sort order or view mode.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Shortcuts.
featureItems.description: Display keyboard hints with DropdownMenuShortcut so users learn to invoke actions directly without menu interaction.

featureItems.title: Nested Submenus.
featureItems.description: Use DropdownMenuSub to nest related actions, reducing menu length and organizing command trees hierarchically.

faqs.question: What is the difference between a dropdown menu and a combobox?
faqs.answer: A dropdown menu shows a fixed list of actions or settings when clicked. A [Shadcn Combobox](/components/combobox) allows typing to filter options and returns a selected value. Use menus for actions; use comboboxes for selection.

faqs.question: How do I nest actions or submenus in a dropdown menu?
faqs.answer: Use DropdownMenuSub with a trigger and content. The nested menu appears on hover or focus, showing related actions grouped together. This is useful for organizing view modes or export options.

faqs.question: Can I use checkbox items to toggle features on and off?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use DropdownMenuCheckboxItem and bind checked state to your app state. When users click, toggle the feature and update the item's checked attribute so the menu shows the current state.

faqs.question: How do I show keyboard shortcuts in the menu?
faqs.answer: Add DropdownMenuShortcut inside DropdownMenuItem to display the keyboard combo (e.g., Cmd+S). Users see the hint and can learn to use the shortcut next time instead of opening the menu.

faqs.question: Is the dropdown menu keyboard accessible?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arrow keys navigate items, Enter or Space activates, and Escape closes. Ensure items have descriptive text so screen readers announce actions. Test with a screen reader to verify navigation works.

faqs.question: How do I position the dropdown menu on the right of the trigger button?
faqs.answer: Use the align prop on DropdownMenuContent: align="end" positions the menu right-aligned with the button. Use side="top" or side="bottom" to control vertical position.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Use dropdown menus with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to create quick-action buttons. Common components include More Actions (three dots), View Options, or Settings menus.

Combine DropdownMenuCheckboxItem with [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) to show active filters or settings count. When a user toggles items, update the badge to reflect the current selection.
Nest multiple dropdown menus with DropdownMenuSub to organize complex command trees. Each submenu appears on hover, building hierarchical interactions for export, import, or advanced settings.

empty.description: Free shadcn empty state components with illustrations for analytics, search, payments. Convert no-data screens into onboarding moments with Tailwind CSS.
empty.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn empty state components for zero-data screens, search results, onboarding, and error states. Covers analytics, bookmarks, tasks, automations, comments, products, search, payments, integrations, events, inbox, team members, notifications, and more: all with custom illustrations.

content.title: Shadcn Empty State: Zero-Data Screens with Illustrations
summary: ReUI Empty is a custom shadcn component for rendering intentional empty states: screens where data is absent, searches return no results, or users have not yet taken an onboarding action. It structures the empty state as an icon or illustration, a title, a description, and an optional call-to-action.

Twenty components cover a broad range of product contexts: analytics no-data, bookmarks, tasks, automations with toggle illustration, comments with isometric board, products with stacked cards and blur, search with file cards, payments with credit card illustration, integrations with connected nodes, events with calendar, inbox, team members, notifications, dashed upload zones, and document upload.
The sections below cover the component structure, why empty states matter for retention, all twenty components, and composition with Button, Input, and Kbd.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Empty?
paragraphs: ReUI Empty is a custom shadcn component for empty state screens. It renders a centered layout with an illustration or icon, a concise heading, a one-to-two sentence description, and a primary action button. The illustration slot accepts SVG, image, or any React node: ReUI ships inline SVG illustrations for each product context so no external image hosting is required.

Twenty components cover the most common empty state contexts in SaaS products. Each component is designed for a specific product screen: the payments empty state uses a credit card illustration, the integrations empty state shows connected node graphics, and the search empty state shows a stacked file card animation. All illustrations use Tailwind colors and CSS variables so they adapt to light and dark themes.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Empty?
paragraphs: Empty states are one of the highest-leverage UX touchpoints in a product. A blank table with no message signals a bug; a well-designed empty state with a clear action converts first-time users into active ones. Analytics dashboards, inbox views, integration marketplaces, and task lists all need empty states that explain the absence and guide the user toward the action that fills the space.

ReUI Empty states include inline SVG illustrations specific to each product context rather than generic 'no data' icons: the difference between an empty state that users understand in two seconds and one that requires reading the description. Pair with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for the primary action and [Shadcn Input Group](/components/input-group) for search-based empty states.

featureItems.title: Custom ReUI Component.
featureItems.description: ReUI Empty is a custom component not in base shadcn/ui. It provides a consistent centered layout with illustration, heading, description, and action slots for all zero-data screens.

featureItems.title: Context-Specific Illustrations.
featureItems.description: Twenty inline SVG illustrations match specific product contexts: analytics charts, credit card, connected nodes, calendar, stacked file cards, chat bubbles, isometric board, and more. No external image hosting required.

featureItems.title: Light and Dark Theme Illustrations.
featureItems.description: All illustrations use CSS variables from your Tailwind theme. Switch between light and dark mode and illustrations adapt their fill and stroke colors automatically.

featureItems.title: Dashed Upload Empty State.
featureItems.description: Dashed border dropzone variant doubles as an empty state and file upload trigger — used in document management and media library screens where upload is the primary action.

featureItems.title: Search Input and Keyboard Shortcut.
featureItems.description: Empty state with search input embeds a shadcn Input Group for immediate search entry. Kbd component shows keyboard shortcuts inline — useful for command palette trigger hints on empty screens.

featureItems.title: Add Button and Onboarding Actions.
featureItems.description: Empty states include a prominent shadcn Button as the primary action: 'Add task', 'Connect integration', 'Invite team member', 'Upload document'. The action label matches the product context of each illustration.

featureItems.title: Stacked Card and Blur Effects.
featureItems.description: Product and payments empty states use layered card or credit card illustrations with blur effects to preview what the filled state looks like — reducing uncertainty about what users are enabling.

featureItems.title: Grid and Inline Variants.
featureItems.description: Basic empty state renders in a centered full-page layout. Card and outline variants embed inside data grids, table bodies, or constrained panels where the empty state sits inline with other content.

faqs.question: What is a shadcn empty state used for?
faqs.answer: Empty states communicate to users that a list, table, dashboard, or feed has no content: and tell them what to do next. They appear when users have no data yet (onboarding), search returns no results, filters eliminate all items, or a feature has not been activated.

faqs.question: Why use an illustration instead of just text?
faqs.answer: Illustrations make empty states scannable and memorable. A credit card illustration on the payments empty state communicates context without reading the heading. Users process images faster than text, and a context-specific illustration signals intentional design rather than an error.

faqs.question: Do the illustrations support dark mode?
faqs.answer: Yes. All illustrations use CSS variables from your Tailwind and shadcn theme. Switching to dark mode changes illustration colors automatically because fills and strokes reference the same color tokens as your UI components.

faqs.question: How do I use the dashed upload empty state?
faqs.answer: The dashed upload empty state wraps a file input in a dropzone with a dashed border. It serves as both the empty state for a document or media library and the upload trigger. On file selection, transition to the filled state with file list or image grid.

faqs.question: Can I embed an empty state inside a data grid?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use the card or basic variant without the full-page centering. Pass it as the table body content when the row count is zero. The outline variant has reduced padding suitable for inline placement inside a bordered table container.

faqs.question: Are there empty states for specific SaaS product contexts?
faqs.answer: Yes: that is the defining feature of ReUI Empty. Components match specific screens: analytics (no data chart outline), payments (credit card illustration), integrations (connected nodes), inbox (chat bubbles), notifications (bell), team members (avatars), automations (toggle), and more.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Always pair Empty with a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) as the primary action: 'Add task', 'Connect integration', 'Upload document'. The action should directly trigger the flow that fills the empty state.

Use [Shadcn Input Group](/components/input-group) with a search icon for search-result empty states, and [Shadcn Kbd](/components/kbd) to show keyboard shortcuts that trigger the action (⌘K for command palette, ⌘N for new item).

Embed empty states inside [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid), [Shadcn Table](/components/table), or [Shadcn Card](/components/card) containers for inline zero-data handling. Use [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) while data loads: transition to the empty state only after a confirmed empty response from the API.

field.description: Free shadcn field components for form layout, labels, and error feedback. Compose accessible React form fields with Tailwind CSS label and error layout.
field.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn field components for accessible form composition, validation feedback, and label organization. Shadcn Field is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Field: Semantic Accessible Form Composition
summary: Shadcn Field is a wrapper component for composing accessible form fields with labels, descriptions, and error states, built on Radix UI components that stays compatible with Base UI composition, while using Tailwind-friendly styles. It is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options, so radius, fonts, and theme tokens from the create flow apply consistently to previews and exported UI.
It combines label, control, hint text, and error messaging so form inputs stay properly labeled and validation feedback is clear.

ReUI lists field components inside realistic forms and workflows so you can judge spacing, validation density, and integration with [Shadcn Input](/components/input), [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox), and other controls next to complete form layouts, not only isolated stories. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams adopt it, which features matter, and how it pairs with form libraries.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Field?
paragraphs: In UI terms, a field is a labeled input control with optional hint text and error messaging. Shadcn Field wraps these elements in a semantic container that follows accessibility conventions from Radix UI and fits teams that also use Base UI-style unstyled building blocks.

Components here show vertical stacked fields, horizontal label-control alignment, responsive fields that stack on mobile, and grouped fields with separators. They are meant to answer whether your product needs simple input groups, complex multi-control layouts, or validation-rich forms, with styling that respects Shadcn Create options end to end.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Field?
paragraphs: Composing forms correctly requires label association, proper spacing, and validation feedback. Shadcn Field handles layout and semantics so you focus on field content. Teams use it for every input, checkbox, select, and textarea to ensure consistency.

Pair fields with React Hook Form or TanStack Form for state and validation management. Use [Shadcn Input](/components/input), [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox), [Shadcn Select](/components/select), and other controls inside Field. Group related fields with FieldGroup and FieldSeparator. Show validation errors with data-invalid and FieldError for clear user feedback.

featureItems.title: Label Association.
featureItems.description: FieldLabel automatically associates with controls via htmlFor, ensuring screen readers announce labels correctly and clicks focus inputs.

featureItems.title: Layout Orientations.
featureItems.description: Use vertical (default) for mobile-first stacking or horizontal for side-by-side label and control. Set orientation="responsive" for automatic column layout on containers.

featureItems.title: Validation Feedback.
featureItems.description: Add data-invalid to Field to switch to error state. Use FieldError to display error messages that stay visually linked to the control.

featureItems.title: Helper Text.
featureItems.description: Use FieldDescription for hints, requirements, or examples. It appears below the label and helps users understand what input is expected.

featureItems.title: Field Grouping.
featureItems.description: Use FieldGroup to stack multiple fields and FieldSeparator to divide sections, maintaining consistent spacing and visual hierarchy.

faqs.question: What should go inside a Field component?
faqs.answer: Place an [Shadcn Input](/components/input), [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox), [Shadcn Select](/components/select), [Shadcn Combobox](/components/combobox), or [Shadcn Textarea](/components/textarea) inside FieldContent. Add FieldLabel, FieldDescription, and FieldError for complete accessibility.

faqs.question: How do I show validation errors in a Field?
faqs.answer: Add data-invalid to Field when validation fails. Include FieldError with the error message. Use the aria-invalid prop on the control to signal invalid state to screen readers.

faqs.question: Can I use Field with React Hook Form or TanStack Form?
faqs.answer: Yes. Register the control with your form library and pass the error state to Field with data-invalid. The Field handles label and error layout while the form handles state and validation logic.

faqs.question: How do I stack multiple fields horizontally?
faqs.answer: Set orientation="horizontal" on Field to align label and control side by side. Use FieldContent to wrap the control and keep hint text below. This layout works best on desktop screens.

faqs.question: What is the difference between FieldDescription and FieldError?
faqs.answer: FieldDescription is help text shown when the field is valid, providing hints or examples. FieldError is shown when validation fails, explaining what is wrong. Only show one at a time.

faqs.question: Is Field accessible without extra ARIA attributes?
faqs.answer: Yes. Field handles label association and error linking automatically. You still own heading hierarchy, grouping semantics, and testing with real assistive tech to ensure your specific form is accessible.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap [Shadcn Input](/components/input), [Shadcn Select](/components/select), [Shadcn Textarea](/components/textarea), and [Shadcn Combobox](/components/combobox) components inside Field with FieldLabel, FieldDescription, and error states to create accessible form layouts.

Use [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) and [Shadcn Radio Group](/components/radio-group) inside Field for multi-select and exclusive-choice forms. Group related checkboxes with FieldGroup for visual organization.
Stack fields with FieldGroup and add FieldSeparator to divide logical form sections. Combine with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for submit and reset actions at the end of the form.

file-upload.description: Free shadcn file upload components with validation and preview. Custom React component with drag-drop, type validation, progress tracking for Next.js.
file-upload.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn file upload components for drag-and-drop zones, image upload with preview, gallery upload with progress tracking, avatar and cover image replacement, table-based file management, and sortable image grids: all built with accessible inputs and Sonner feedback.

content.title: Shadcn File Upload: Drag, Drop, Preview, and Manage Files
summary: ReUI File Upload is a custom shadcn component for handling file input, drag-and-drop zones, image previews, and multi-file management. It wraps the native file input in an accessible label-based trigger, announces selection state to assistive technologies, and emits Sonner toast feedback for upload errors.

Ten components cover basic file input, cover image upload, avatar replacement, drag-and-drop zones, gallery upload, upload with progress tracking, table-based file management, image upload with cropping intent, image upload with drag-to-reorder via Sortable, and card-style upload UI.
The sections below cover what the component provides, real use cases in SaaS and B2B products, features, and integration with Sortable for reordering uploaded images.

sections.title: What is Shadcn File Upload?
paragraphs: ReUI File Upload is a custom component for React and Next.js that replaces the native file input with an accessible, stylable trigger. It supports single and multi-file selection, drag-and-drop zones, image preview with remove actions, and upload progress visualization. It is built on top of shadcn primitives: Card, Button, Badge, Progress, Alert, Dialog, and Table: so it inherits your design tokens automatically.

Components include avatar and cover image upload for profile settings, gallery upload for image-heavy products, table-based upload for document management, and a sortable image grid that composes with the ReUI Sortable component so users can reorder uploaded images before saving.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn File Upload?
paragraphs: File upload is one of the most common SaaS interactions: user avatars, cover images, document attachments, product photos, and media galleries all require a polished upload experience. Native file inputs are unstyled and difficult to customize. ReUI components show the full spectrum from a minimal one-click replacement to a full drag-and-drop gallery with progress bars and sortable reordering.

Sonner integration handles upload progress and error state with non-blocking toasts, keeping the user in context. Compose with [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) for image crop flows, [Shadcn Table](/components/table) for document lists, and [Shadcn Sortable](/docs/components/base/sortable) for reorderable image grids.

featureItems.title: Drag and Drop Zone.
featureItems.description: A styled dropzone detects dragover, dragleave, and drop events. The zone highlights on hover and accepts single or multiple files. Accessible via click-to-select as a fallback.

featureItems.title: Image Upload with Preview.
featureItems.description: After selection, images render as inline previews with remove buttons. Previews use object-URL revocation to prevent memory leaks when files are removed.

featureItems.title: Upload Progress Tracking.
featureItems.description: Per-file progress bars using shadcn Progress. Wire to XMLHttpRequest onprogress or fetch stream events for real upload feedback, or simulate progress for local uploads.

featureItems.title: Avatar and Cover Image Upload.
featureItems.description: Profile-oriented components replace avatar and cover image with a single click or drag. Overlay triggers appear on hover, maintaining a clean default state.

featureItems.title: Gallery and Multi-File Upload.
featureItems.description: Grid gallery component supports multiple image previews with individual remove actions. Files display in a responsive grid that adapts to the number of selections.

featureItems.title: Table File Management.
featureItems.description: Table-based component lists uploaded files with name, size, type, status, and remove action in a shadcn Table. Useful for document upload flows where file metadata matters.

featureItems.title: Sortable Image Grid.
featureItems.description: Composes with ReUI Sortable (``@dnd-kit/sortable``) to let users drag-and-drop uploaded images into a custom order before saving — essential for product photo management and gallery ordering.

featureItems.title: Card and Dialog Variants.
featureItems.description: Card-style upload wraps the zone in a shadcn Card for settings pages. Dialog variant launches the upload UI in a modal with crop or confirm actions.

featureItems.title: Validation and Error States.
featureItems.description: File type, size, and count validation with inline error messages via shadcn Alert. Sonner toasts report async upload errors without blocking the layout.

sections.title: ReUI File Upload: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI File Upload is a custom shadcn component for multi-file upload with validation, preview, and progress tracking.
Available in Radix UI and Base UI flavors via `@base-ui/react`. ReUI maintains 17 such in-house components.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version — API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version — API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: Does ReUI File Upload handle drag and drop?
faqs.answer: Yes. The drag-and-drop zone listens to dragover, dragleave, and drop events on the label element. It highlights on dragover to signal the active target and accepts files on drop. Click-to-select via the native input is always available as a fallback.

faqs.question: How do I show upload progress?
faqs.answer: Wire the Progress component to your upload's progress events. For XHR use request.upload.onprogress; for fetch with ReadableStream use a custom reader. ReUI's progress upload component shows the local state structure: replace the simulated progress with real upload events.

faqs.question: Can users reorder uploaded images?
faqs.answer: Yes. The sortable image grid component composes File Upload with ReUI Sortable (``@dnd-kit/sortable``). After upload, images appear in a draggable grid. Users reorder them with drag-and-drop or keyboard, then save the ordered array to the backend.

faqs.question: How do I validate file type and size?
faqs.answer: Check file.type and file.size in the change or drop handler before adding files to state. Reject invalid files and display an error with shadcn Alert or a Sonner toast. Set the accept attribute on the input as a first-pass filter.

faqs.question: How does avatar upload work?
faqs.answer: The avatar upload component wraps the existing avatar image in a label with an invisible file input. On hover, an overlay icon appears. On selection, the new image previews immediately using URL.createObjectURL. Submit the file to your upload endpoint on confirm.

faqs.question: Does file upload work with React Hook Form?
faqs.answer: Yes. Register the file input with register('file') or use Controller with a custom onChange handler. Access FileList from the event and validate with Zod's z.instanceof(File) or a custom refine: the same component ReUI uses in form-integrated upload examples.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Pair File Upload with [Shadcn Sortable](/docs/components/base/sortable) to build a complete image management flow: upload first, then drag-and-drop to set display order before saving. The sortable image gallery component in ReUI demonstrates this exact composition.

Use [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) to wrap the upload zone for a modal crop-and-confirm flow, [Shadcn Progress](/components/progress) for per-file upload bars, and [Shadcn Table](/components/table) for document list views with file metadata and remove actions.

Combine with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) and [Shadcn Label](/components/label) for form-integrated upload fields. Use [Shadcn Sonner](/components/`sonner`) for non-blocking upload error and success toasts, and [Shadcn Alert](/docs/components/base/alert) for persistent validation messages.

filters.description: Free shadcn filters components for data filtering with URL persistence. Custom React component for client and server-side filtering with date ranges.
filters.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn filter components integrating TanStack Table column filters, Zod validation, ``nuqs`` URL state persistence, ``react-day-picker`` date ranges, and i18n support. Covers field type variety, custom triggers, data grid integration, async filter mode, and size variants.
keywords: react filter with url state nuqs

content.title: Shadcn Filters: Faceted Search with TanStack and ``nuqs``
summary: ReUI Filters is a custom shadcn component for building filter panels that connect directly to TanStack Table's column filter API. It renders a composable set of field types: text, select, multi-select, date range, number range, and boolean toggles: validates input with Zod, and persists active filters to the URL with ``nuqs`` for shareable, bookmarkable filtered views.

Nine components cover field variety, input validation, custom trigger buttons, compact and large size variants, custom control rendering, full data grid integration, async filter mode for server-side filtering, and i18n support with locale-aware labels and date formatting.
The sections below explain the architecture, why URL-persistent filters matter, feature details, and how Filters integrates with Data Grid and other ReUI primitives.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Filters?
paragraphs: ReUI Filters is a custom shadcn component that does not exist in the base shadcn/ui library. It generates a filter panel from a schema of field definitions: each field maps to a column in a TanStack Table instance or a standalone filter state. Field types include text search, single and multi-select dropdowns, calendar date ranges via ``react-day-picker``, number ranges, and boolean toggles. Zod validates filter values before they reach the table or API.

Filter state can be controlled locally or serialized to URL search parameters using ``nuqs``: the typed URL state library built for Next.js App Router. This means users can share filtered views, the browser back button restores filter state, and analytics tools capture filter combinations as URL parameters.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Filters?
paragraphs: Every data-heavy interface needs filters: analytics dashboards, CRM contact lists, order management tables, log viewers, and product catalogs all benefit from a consistent, type-safe filter panel. Building filters ad-hoc per page leads to duplicated logic, inconsistent UX, and URL state management debt. ReUI Filters centralizes all of that in a composable schema-driven component.

The async mode component fires a debounced callback on filter change instead of updating a local table instance: enabling server-side filtering for large datasets where full client-side data is impractical. Pair with [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid) for the most complete data management interface in ReUI.

sections.title: Libraries and ReUI Filters Primitives
paragraphs: ReUI Filters integrates ``@tanstack/react-table`` for column filter state, ``nuqs`` for URL search param serialization, Zod for filter value validation, ``date-fns`` for date formatting and localization, and ``react-day-picker`` for calendar date range inputs.

The filter panel composes shadcn Dropdown Menu, Popover, Calendar, Badge, Button, and ScrollArea. Badge renders applied filter chips; Popover contains the date range calendar; ScrollArea handles long option lists in multi-select fields.

featureItems.title: TanStack Table Column Filter API.
featureItems.description: Filters map directly to TanStack Table column definitions. Set column.getFilterValue() and column.setFilterValue() for client-side filtering, or use manualFiltering with an async callback for server-side filtering.

featureItems.title: ``nuqs`` URL State Persistence.
featureItems.description: Active filter state serializes to URL search parameters via ``nuqs``. Users can share filtered views, the browser back button restores filters, and Next.js App Router handles hydration without extra code.

featureItems.title: Zod Filter Validation.
featureItems.description: Filter values pass through a Zod schema before reaching the table or API call. Invalid inputs are rejected before triggering a filter update, preventing malformed API requests.

featureItems.title: Date Range Filters.
featureItems.description: Date range fields use ``react-day-picker`` inside a Popover for accessible calendar-based date selection. Integrates with ``date-fns`` for locale-aware formatting and range validation.

featureItems.title: Multi-Field Type Support.
featureItems.description: Text search, single select, multi-select with checkboxes, date ranges, number ranges, and boolean toggles — all defined in a single field schema and rendered by the filter panel automatically.

featureItems.title: Applied Filter Chips.
featureItems.description: Active filters render as shadcn Badge chips with remove actions. A clear-all button resets all filters and, if ``nuqs`` is active, clears the URL state.

featureItems.title: Async and Server-Side Filter Mode.
featureItems.description: The async filter component debounces filter changes and fires a callback instead of updating local table state. Wire to SWR, React Query, or Next.js Server Actions for large datasets where client-side filtering is not feasible.

featureItems.title: i18n Support.
featureItems.description: Field labels, placeholder text, and date formatting accept locale props. The i18n filter component shows English and localized variants side by side using ``date-fns`` locale utilities.

featureItems.title: Size Variants and Custom Controls.
featureItems.description: Compact (small), default, and large size variants adapt to dense admin toolbars or spacious marketing dashboards. Custom control rendering allows any shadcn input component inside a filter field.

sections.title: ReUI Filters: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Filters is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project: following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Filters is a custom schema-driven filter panel not available in base shadcn/ui. It integrates directly with ``@tanstack/react-table`` column filter APIs, persists filter state to URL search params via ``nuqs``, validates inputs with ``zod``, and supports async server-side filtering for large datasets. The i18n and custom control components extend it for global products.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on ``@base-ui/react``. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling: only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version — API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version — API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: How do filters connect to TanStack Table?
faqs.answer: Each filter field maps to a TanStack Table column via its accessorKey or id. The filter panel calls column.setFilterValue() on change and reads column.getFilterValue() to initialize state. Set filterFn on the column definition to control matching logic (exact, includes, inDateRange, etc.).

faqs.question: How does URL filter state work with ``nuqs``?
faqs.answer: Filter state is parsed from and written to URL search parameters via ``nuqs`` typed query hooks. In Next.js App Router, ``nuqs`` uses the router to update the URL without full navigation. Filters hydrate from the URL on mount, so shared links restore the exact filter state.

faqs.question: Can filters work with server-side data?
faqs.answer: Yes. Enable manualFiltering: true on the TanStack Table instance and use the async filter component. The filter panel debounces input and fires a callback with the current filter values. Pass these to your API route, SWR key, or React Query queryFn to fetch filtered results from the server.

faqs.question: How are date range filters implemented?
faqs.answer: Date range fields use ``react-day-picker`` in range mode inside a shadcn Popover. Selected dates are validated and formatted with ``date-fns``. The range value passes to TanStack Table's inDateRange filter function or to your API as ISO date strings.

faqs.question: Can I add custom filter controls beyond the built-in types?
faqs.answer: Yes. The custom controls component accepts any React node as a field renderer. Pass a shadcn Select, Combobox, Slider, or any other input component. The field wrapper handles the label, applied state, and clear action.

faqs.question: Does the filter panel support i18n?
faqs.answer: Yes. Field labels and placeholder text are props. Date formatting uses ``date-fns`` locale objects. The i18n filter component shows how to pass a locale to ``react-day-picker`` and ``date-fns`` simultaneously for consistent date display.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Filters is designed to pair with [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid). Use Filters above the grid for a full data management interface with column filtering, sort, pagination, and CRUD: the data grid with filters components show this complete composition.

Use [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for applied filter chips, [Shadcn Calendar](/components/calendar) inside [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) for date range fields, and [Shadcn Select](/components/select) or [Shadcn Combobox](/components/combobox) for option-based fields that need search.

Wrap filter panels in [Shadcn Sheet](/components/sheet) for a slide-out mobile filter drawer, or [Shadcn Collapsible](/components/collapsible) for an inline expandable filter section above a table. Use [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for the custom trigger component.

frame.description: Free shadcn frame components for responsive layout with sidebar. Custom React component for page frames, responsive navigation, and fixed headers.
frame.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn frame components for bounded layouts, viewport sections, and responsive containers. Shadcn Frame is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI layout components and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Frame: Build Bounded Layout Sections
summary: Shadcn Frame is a bounded container primitive that maintains semantic HTML structure while providing flexible layout composition. It aligns with Radix UI and Base UI components for container semantics, and is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options so radius, spacing, and theme tokens apply consistently.
It creates a visual boundary for page sections, dashboard regions, or viewport-aware containers so content stays organized and readable at any screen size.

ReUI lists frame components inside realistic page layouts so you evaluate spacing, depth, and nesting next to a [Shadcn Card](/components/card) or [Shadcn Table](/components/table), not only a standalone block. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how it pairs with other primitives.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Frame?
paragraphs: In layout terms, a frame is a bounded container that creates visual and semantic sections. Shadcn Frame follows semantic HTML and maintains accessibility expectations for region landmarks, so it works well when you also build with Radix UI primitives.

Components here show max-width containers, nested frames, and dashboard-style sections. They help you decide depth, padding, and composition before shipping the same structure into Next.js or a server-rendered React application.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Frame?
paragraphs: Frames reduce noise by creating clear visual boundaries between page regions. They make layout predictable when many sections or cards sit on one URL, and they help responsive design by centralizing max-width and padding logic in one reusable component.

Searchers often look for "shadcn frame", "tailwind container", or "react bounded layout". This page answers those intents with concrete layouts that show how frames nest with [Shadcn Card](/components/card), [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog), and form regions.

featureItems.title: Semantic HTML.
featureItems.description: Uses section or div with proper landmarks so assistive tech recognizes page structure and regions stay accessible.

featureItems.title: Max-Width Control.
featureItems.description: Bounded container widths centered on screen, aligned with design system breakpoints and spacing scale.

featureItems.title: Responsive Padding.
featureItems.description: Tailwind utility classes adjust container padding at different breakpoints for consistent inner and outer spacing.

featureItems.title: Nesting Support.
featureItems.description: Frames nest cleanly so dashboard chrome, modals, and page sections all respect the same max-width and alignment.

featureItems.title: Shadcn Create Compatible.
featureItems.description: Radius, spacing, and color tokens from Shadcn Create apply to frame surfaces so previews match exported UI.

sections.title: ReUI Frame: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Frame is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project: following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Frame is a custom container component not available in base shadcn/ui. It provides a consistent layout shell: title, toolbar slot, action button, and scrollable content area: used across Data Grid, Kanban, Timeline, Chart, and Sortable components to produce self-contained dashboard panels without writing per-page chrome code.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on ``@base-ui/react``. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling: only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.
