faqs.question: What is a frame used for in modern product layouts?
faqs.answer: Frames create bounded regions on pages so content stays readable and centered at wide viewports. They organize dashboards, pages, and modal bodies while centralizing max-width and padding logic. Teams use them to keep layout consistent when mixing cards, tables, and other sections.

faqs.question: Should I use a frame or a card for a page section?
faqs.answer: Use a frame for top-level layout regions and page boundaries. Use a [Shadcn Card](/components/card) when you need a styled container with border, shadow, or elevated appearance. Frames are layout primitives; cards are content containers. You often nest cards inside frames.

faqs.question: How does a frame differ from a container in Tailwind CSS?
faqs.answer: The Tailwind container applies max-width at certain breakpoints but doesn't include semantic structure or built-in padding options. Shadcn Frame uses semantic HTML, respects Shadcn Create tokens, and pairs with other primitives for consistent, accessible layouts.

faqs.question: Can I use frames with Radix UI and Base UI?
faqs.answer: Yes. Shadcn Frame uses semantic HTML so it composes cleanly with Radix UI primitives and Base UI unstyled components. The frame handles layout; your composed content handles interaction and state.

faqs.question: Is Shadcn Frame responsive out of the box?
faqs.answer: Frames use Tailwind breakpoints and responsive utilities so padding, max-width, and spacing adjust at tablet and mobile sizes. You customize responsive behavior by modifying the underlying Tailwind classes to match your design system.

faqs.question: How are ReUI frame components different from a minimal frame block?
faqs.answer: ReUI frames sit inside realistic dashboards, pages, and modal layouts. You see how frames compose with [Shadcn Table](/components/table), [Shadcn Pagination](/components/pagination), and form regions together, which is closer to production reality than a standalone block.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap a [Shadcn Card](/components/card) group or [Shadcn Table](/components/table) inside a frame to establish clear page boundaries. Pair frames with [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) or [Shadcn Drawer](/components/drawer) to maintain layout alignment when modals or panels slide in.

Use frames inside dashboards with [Shadcn Tabs](/components/tabs) or [Shadcn Accordion](/components/accordion) to organize top-level sections. Nest frames when building nested layouts like page header, main content, and footer regions.
Combine frames with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) groups and [Shadcn Button](/components/button) actions for forms and data entry workflows that span multiple viewport sizes.

hover-card.description: Free shadcn hover card components for rich previews on hover. Accessible React tooltips with delay, touch device support, Tailwind CSS hover content.
hover-card.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn hover-card components for preview popovers, profile cards, and rich metadata displays. Shadcn Hover Card is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI popover components and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Hover Card: Rich Preview on Hover
summary: Shadcn Hover Card is a floating popover that opens on hover or focus, displaying rich content like previews or metadata. It maps to Radix UI Hover Card semantics so keyboard and screen reader behavior stays predictable, and it works with Radix UI and Base UI-style projects.
It shows detailed information or preview content when users hover over a trigger element, keeping the page clean while offering supplementary context on demand.

ReUI lists hover-card components in realistic dashboards and link-rich pages so you evaluate trigger sizing, popover positioning, and animations next to a [navigation elements](/components/link) or [Shadcn Button](/components/button) group, not only a minimal story. The sections below cover what it is, why teams use it, features, and integration ideas.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Hover Card?
paragraphs: In UI terms, a hover card is a popover triggered by mouse or keyboard focus. Shadcn Hover Card follows Radix UI popover components for positioning, focus management, and dismissal, so it integrates smoothly with teams already using Radix primitives.

Components here show user profile cards, link previews, and metadata panels. They help you decide card size, icon usage, and content density before deploying the same markup into Next.js or a full-featured React app.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Hover Card?
paragraphs: Hover cards reduce noise by hiding rich detail until users request it. They work well for user mentions, link previews, or product details when you want to avoid modal overhead and keep users in scanning mode. Marketing sites, dashboards, and social features often use them.

Searchers often look for "shadcn hover card", "react popover", or "tailwind preview card". This page answers those intents with layouts that show hover cards next to a [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) or [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) to clarify when each makes sense.

featureItems.title: Hover and Focus Triggered.
featureItems.description: Opens automatically on mouse enter or keyboard focus, closes on mouse leave or blur with configurable delays.

featureItems.title: Flexible Positioning.
featureItems.description: Side and align props control popover placement relative to trigger, preventing overlap and respecting viewport edges.

featureItems.title: Rich Content Support.
featureItems.description: Render text, images, [Shadcn Button](/components/button) links, or any React component inside the card so preview detail matches your brand.

featureItems.title: Accessible by Default.
featureItems.description: Built on Radix UI so focus management, roles, and keyboard navigation follow Radix standards and WAI-ARIA guidance.

featureItems.title: Shadcn Create Styles.
featureItems.description: Card background, border, shadow, and radius respect Shadcn Create tokens so previews and exported UI match your theme.

faqs.question: When should I use a hover card instead of a tooltip or popover?
faqs.answer: Use hover card for rich content like user profiles, product summaries, or link previews—content that benefits from multiple lines, images, or actions. Tooltips are for brief hints (one line). Popovers are for interactive forms or complex controls. Hover cards balance density and interactivity.

faqs.question: Is a hover card accessible for keyboard and screen reader users?
faqs.answer: Yes. Built on Radix UI, it exposes the right roles and keyboard interactions at the component level. You own testing with real assistive tech, focus-visible styles in your Tailwind theme, and copy quality. Treat baseline behavior as necessary but not sufficient.

faqs.question: How do I control hover card timing and delays?
faqs.answer: Use openDelay and closeDelay props on the HoverCard root to set how long the user must hover before the card opens or how long it waits before closing. Delays prevent flicker when users move quickly across triggers.

faqs.question: Can hover cards show on click or only on hover?
faqs.answer: The base component opens on hover or focus. For click-based behavior, use a [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) instead. Hover cards are designed for passive discovery; click controls are more explicit and often clearer for mobile users.

faqs.question: How do I prevent hover card content from being cut off at screen edges?
faqs.answer: Use the side and align props to adjust positioning. Radix automatically flips the popover if it would overflow the viewport. You can also set maxWidth or use scrollable inner content if the card gets large.

faqs.question: Are ReUI hover-card components different from a storybook component?
faqs.answer: Yes. ReUI components place hover cards in realistic dashboards, list views, and link-rich pages so you see trigger density, animations, and integration with [Shadcn Avatar](/components/avatar) or [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) badges. This is closer to production than a single block preview.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Use hover cards with a [Shadcn Avatar](/components/avatar) and [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for user profile previews in comments or mention contexts. Add [Shadcn Button](/components/button) links inside cards for actions like follow, message, or view profile.

Pair hover cards with [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) for a two-tier disclosure component: tooltip for a label, hover card for rich preview when users linger. Use [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) when the content is interactive or requires explicit interaction.

Combine hover cards with a [link component](/components/link) group or [Shadcn Table](/components/table) for product previews, user summaries, or metadata detail that stays lightweight while scanning a list.

input.description: Free shadcn input components with prefixes, suffixes, masking, and addons. Password toggle, clearing support. Copy-ready 17 React input variations.
input.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn input components for text entry, search fields, passwords, and real-time validation. Shadcn Input is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI form components and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Input: Text Entry with Validation
summary: Shadcn Input is a controlled text input component that maps to HTML input semantics while providing Tailwind styling and Radix compatibility. It works with Radix UI and Base UI-oriented form layers, and is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options.
It accepts text, numbers, emails, passwords, and other input types while staying accessible for keyboards, screen readers, and validation workflows.

ReUI lists input components inside realistic forms, login pages, and search flows so you evaluate field density, icons, and error states next to a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) or [Shadcn Button](/components/button) group, not only a minimal block. The sections below cover what it is, why teams use it, features, and integration.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Input?
paragraphs: In form terms, an input is a text entry element. Shadcn Input wraps the HTML input element with Tailwind styling and passes through standard HTML attributes, so it behaves like a native input but with design system colors and focus states.

Components here show text fields, email inputs, password fields, and search boxes. They help you decide placeholder text, icon placement, and validation feedback before integrating the same component into login, checkout, or account settings flows.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Input?
paragraphs: Teams use inputs in every form—login, signup, search, filters, and data entry. A consistent, accessible input saves time and keeps UX predictable. Inputs pair with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for labels, descriptions, and error messages so validation stays near the field.

Searchers often look for "shadcn input", "react text input", or "tailwind form field". This page answers those intents with layouts that show inputs with [Shadcn Label](/components/label), [Shadcn Input Group](/components/input-group), and error states together.

featureItems.title: Standard HTML Types.
featureItems.description: Supports text, email, password, number, url, tel, and more so you pick the right keyboard and browser validation for each field.

featureItems.title: Tailwind Styling.
featureItems.description: Border, focus ring, and placeholder colors respect your Shadcn Create tokens so the input matches your design system.

featureItems.title: Disabled and ReadOnly States.
featureItems.description: Visual feedback for disabled, readonly, and error states so users understand field interactivity and validation status.

featureItems.title: Controlled component.
featureItems.description: Works as a controlled component with value and onChange props, allowing real-time validation and state synchronization with React.

featureItems.title: Integration with Field.
featureItems.description: Pairs with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for labels, descriptions, and error messages so form structure stays semantic and accessible.

faqs.question: What is the Shadcn Input used for in real products?
faqs.answer: Login forms, signup flows, search fields, filters, checkout steps, account settings, and any form that needs text entry. It's the foundation of form-heavy features like data import, bulk operations, and user profile editing.

faqs.question: How do I add validation to a Shadcn Input?
faqs.answer: Pair it with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) which manages label, description, and error text. Use controlled component state (value and onChange) to validate input, then display errors via the field's error prop. Libraries like React Hook Form or TanStack Form can manage complex validation logic.

faqs.question: Can I use the input type prop to enforce specific input types?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use type="email" for email fields, type="number" for numeric input, type="password" for passwords, and type="tel" for phone numbers. Each type triggers the appropriate mobile keyboard and applies browser-native validation.

faqs.question: Is Shadcn Input accessible?
faqs.answer: The input element is semantically correct and keyboard accessible. You still need to pair it with a [Shadcn Label](/components/label) using htmlFor to create a proper label-input association, and test focus states and error announcements with real assistive technology.

faqs.question: How do I handle real-time input validation in React?
faqs.answer: Use onChange to update state as the user types, validate the value in real-time, and display error messages. Be mindful of performance with large lists; debounce if validation calls an API. Show success or pending states to guide users toward valid data.

faqs.question: Are ReUI input components different from a minimal input block?
faqs.answer: Yes. ReUI components show inputs inside login forms, search bars, filters, and multi-field checkout flows. You see input density, icon placement, and error states together with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) and [Shadcn Field](/components/field) companions, which is closer to production reality.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap inputs with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) to add labels, descriptions, and error messages. Pair with [Shadcn Label](/components/label) to create proper form associations so screen readers announce the input correctly.

Use [Shadcn Input Group](/components/input-group) to add icons, buttons, or text addons before or after the input. Combine with [Shadcn Input OTP](/components/`input-otp`) for verification codes and [Shadcn Number Field](/docs/components/base/number-field) for numeric entry with step controls.

Add [Shadcn Button](/components/button) next to or inside the input for search, submit, or clear actions. Use [Shadcn Button Group](/components/button-group) when multiple input-related actions need to sit together.

input-group.description: Free shadcn input group components for combining inputs with addons and buttons. Align icons, buttons, labels with Tailwind CSS input group layouts.
input-group.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn input-group components for text fields with prefixes, suffixes, icons, and buttons. Shadcn Input Group is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI form components and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Input Group: Inputs with Addons
summary: Shadcn Input Group is a flex wrapper that pairs an [Shadcn Input](/components/input) with icons, text, or buttons. It maintains proper focus and keyboard navigation when addons sit alongside the input, and aligns with Radix UI and Base UI components.
It adds visual context like currency symbols, unit labels, or action buttons so inputs stay compact while improving clarity about what data users should enter.

ReUI lists input-group components in search flows, filters, checkout forms, and settings pages so you evaluate icon sizing, button placement, and input width next to real [Shadcn Button](/components/button) actions and [Shadcn Field](/components/field) groups. The sections below cover what it is, why teams use it, and integration components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Input Group?
paragraphs: In form terms, an input group is a container that holds an input and one or more addons—text, icons, or buttons—arranged horizontally. Shadcn Input Group manages focus and layout so the input and addons feel like one unit.

Components here show currency inputs, search fields with buttons, URL fields with copy icons, and password toggles. They help you decide addon placement and sizing before shipping inputs with rich context into production forms.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Input Group?
paragraphs: Input groups reduce cognitive load by showing context right next to the field. Currency symbols, URL schemes, and action icons are all visible without extra labels or popovers. They save space in dense forms and make intent clear at a glance.

Searchers often look for "shadcn input group", "react input with button", or "tailwind input addon". This page answers those intents with layouts that combine input groups with [Shadcn Field](/components/field), [Shadcn Button](/components/button), and validation components.

featureItems.title: Prefix and Suffix Addons.
featureItems.description: Text, icons, or currency symbols before or after the input so context is always visible and aligned.

featureItems.title: Button Integration.
featureItems.description: Buttons sit natively inside the group for search, clear, toggle, or copy actions without needing extra spacing logic.

featureItems.title: Focus Management.
featureItems.description: Focus states work correctly whether user focuses the input or an interactive addon, preventing focus loops and keeping keyboard nav smooth.

featureItems.title: Icon Support.
featureItems.description: Render icons from any library (lucide-react, heroicons) as visual hints or interactive toggles next to the input.

featureItems.title: Responsive Layout.
featureItems.description: Addons and input automatically adjust spacing and sizing at different breakpoints via Tailwind utilities.

faqs.question: When should I use an input group instead of a plain input?
faqs.answer: Use input groups when the field has prefix context (currency, URL scheme), a visible action (search, clear, copy), or a toggle (show/hide password). For simple text without addons, a plain [Shadcn Input](/components/input) is cleaner.

faqs.question: How do I add a search button or icon to an input group?
faqs.answer: Render a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) as the suffix inside the InputGroup. Set the button variant to ghost or outline so it visually sits inside the group. Wire the button onClick handler to your search or submit logic.

faqs.question: Can I use input groups with validation and error messages?
faqs.answer: Yes. Wrap the input group with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) to add error messages and descriptions. The field handles validation display while the input group handles prefix/suffix layout.

faqs.question: Is an input group accessible for screen readers?
faqs.answer: Yes, when you use semantic labels and maintain proper focus order. Pair the input group with a [Shadcn Label](/components/label) using htmlFor, and ensure any icon buttons inside the group have accessible labels or aria-labels so screen readers understand their purpose.

faqs.question: How do I add a currency or unit symbol to an input?
faqs.answer: Use InputGroupText or InputGroupAddon as the prefix or suffix, passing the symbol (like "$" or "kg"). The addon sits next to the input and doesn't receive focus, making it purely visual context.

faqs.question: Are ReUI input-group components different from a minimal example?
faqs.answer: Yes. ReUI shows input groups in realistic search flows, checkout steps, and filter panels. You see button placement, focus states, and integration with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) and [Shadcn Button](/components/button) together, which is closer to production than a single example.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair input groups with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) to add labels and error messages. Wrap both together for a complete, accessible form field that includes context, input, and validation feedback.

Use [Shadcn Button](/components/button) as suffixes inside input groups for search, submit, or clear actions. Combine with [Shadcn Dropdown Menu](/components/dropdown-menu) when the suffix needs a menu of options.

Add icons from lucide-react or heroicons as prefix elements. Use [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) on icon buttons to explain their purpose, and combine with [Shadcn Input OTP](/components/`input-otp`) for verification code groups with separators.

input-otp.description: Free shadcn OTP input components using the input-otp package. Accessible one-time-password fields with paste handling, auto-submit, React OTP components.
input-otp.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn OTP input components built on the ``input-otp`` library. Covers basic OTP entry, numeric-only digits, multiple separator styles, alphanumeric codes, PIN inputs, and verification code layouts: accessible, mobile-friendly, and composable with shadcn Field and Button.

content.title: Shadcn Input OTP: Verification Codes and PIN Entry
summary: ReUI Input OTP is built on the ``input-otp`` library: the lightweight, accessible OTP input component used by shadcn/ui itself. It renders individual character slots as a single controlled input, handles paste from clipboard, auto-advances focus on digit entry, and announces completion state to screen readers.

Six components cover basic OTP entry, numeric-digit-only validation, multiple separator styles between slot groups, alphanumeric verification codes, PIN code entry, and a complete verification code flow with submit action.
The sections below cover the ``input-otp`` library integration, real authentication use cases, features, and how OTP input composes with Field and Button for complete verification forms.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Input OTP?
paragraphs: Shadcn Input OTP is the official OTP input component in shadcn/ui, built on the ``input-otp`` library. It renders a segmented input: each character occupies a styled slot: while the underlying DOM element is a single text input. This gives native mobile keyboard support (numeric keypad on iOS/Android), correct autocomplete='one-time-code' behavior for SMS autofill, and clipboard paste that distributes characters across slots automatically.

ReUI components show Input OTP in realistic authentication and security flows: SMS verification, email confirmation, two-factor authentication, PIN entry for payment confirmation, and alphanumeric license key entry. Each component includes the submit trigger and error state so you can evaluate the complete user flow.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Input OTP?
paragraphs: OTP inputs appear in two-factor authentication, email and phone verification, PIN-gated payment confirmation, and license key entry. The core challenge is building a segmented visual with a single DOM input: maintaining native mobile keyboard behavior, paste support, and accessibility simultaneously. The ``input-otp`` library solves this correctly; hand-rolled OTP inputs frequently break SMS autofill or paste.

The autocomplete='one-time-code' attribute enables iOS and Android SMS autofill: the operating system reads the SMS, extracts the code, and fills the input automatically. ReUI OTP components include this attribute and the correct inputmode='numeric' for numeric-only codes.

sections.title: Libraries and ReUI Input OTP Primitives
paragraphs: ReUI Input OTP imports the ``input-otp`` library for the OTPInput and OTPInputSlot primitives. It composes shadcn Field, Label, and Button for complete verification form layouts.
The library is the same one shadcn/ui uses internally for its own OTP input component.

featureItems.title: ``input-otp`` Library Foundation.
featureItems.description: Built on the ``input-otp`` library — the same library used by shadcn/ui. Single DOM input with segmented visual rendering, native mobile keyboard, SMS autofill via autocomplete='one-time-code', and clipboard paste.

featureItems.title: Auto-Advance and Auto-Submit.
featureItems.description: Focus moves to the next slot automatically after each character entry. When the last slot fills, an optional onChange callback fires with the complete value — wire to auto-submit or immediate validation.

featureItems.title: Paste Support.
featureItems.description: Pasting a multi-character string (from SMS autofill, clipboard, or password manager) distributes characters across slots correctly. Works in iOS, Android, and desktop browsers.

featureItems.title: Numeric, Alphanumeric, and PIN Components.
featureItems.description: Constrain input to digits only (numeric OTP, PIN), letters and digits (license keys, alphanumeric codes), or mixed. Use inputmode='numeric' and component='[0-9]*' for numeric-only mobile keyboards.

featureItems.title: Separator Variants.
featureItems.description: Separator elements render between slot groups — single dash, double dash, or no separator. Useful for license keys (XXXX-XXXX-XXXX), phone number segments, or date-part inputs.

featureItems.title: SMS Autofill.
featureItems.description: The autocomplete='one-time-code' attribute enables iOS and Android to intercept incoming SMS codes and offer one-tap fill. Combined with inputmode='numeric' this gives the full native autofill experience.

featureItems.title: Accessible Slot Labeling.
featureItems.description: Each slot is labeled with its position for screen readers. Completion state is announced via a live region. Focus management follows the natural reading order of the segmented input.

faqs.question: What library does ReUI Input OTP use?
faqs.answer: The ``input-otp`` library: a lightweight, accessible OTP input built specifically for React. It is the same library used by shadcn/ui's built-in OTP input component. It renders a single DOM input with segmented visual slots.

faqs.question: Does SMS autofill work on iOS and Android?
faqs.answer: Yes, when you set autocomplete='one-time-code' on the input element. iOS and Android detect incoming SMS messages containing a numeric code and offer one-tap fill. The ``input-otp`` library exposes this attribute through OTPInput props.

faqs.question: How does paste work?
faqs.answer: Pasting a multi-character string distributes characters across slots. If you paste '483920' into a 6-slot OTP input, each character fills a slot and focus lands on the last filled slot or moves to the first empty one. Works with clipboard paste on desktop and SMS autofill on mobile.

faqs.question: Can I restrict input to digits only?
faqs.answer: Yes. Set inputMode='numeric' and component='[0-9]*' on the OTPInput element. The ``input-otp`` library validates keystrokes against the component and rejects non-matching characters. This also triggers the numeric keypad on mobile instead of the full keyboard.

faqs.question: How do I implement auto-submit when all slots are filled?
faqs.answer: Pass an onChange handler that fires when the value length equals the maxLength. If the final value passes validation, call your submit function. The verification code component in ReUI shows an onChange handler that checks value.length === 6 and triggers form submission.

faqs.question: What is the difference between OTP and PIN components?
faqs.answer: OTP (one-time password) components use autocomplete='one-time-code' for SMS autofill and are temporary: used once and discarded. PIN components are reusable numeric passwords for payment confirmation or app unlock, where SMS autofill is not desired. ReUI has separate components for each use case.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Always wrap Input OTP in [Shadcn Field](/components/field) with a [Shadcn Label](/components/label) for the slot group label and an error message slot for invalid code feedback. Use [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for the verify action and [Shadcn Spinner](/components/spinner) inside the button during async verification.

Combine with [Shadcn Sonner](/components/`sonner`) for resend-code confirmation toasts and expired-code error notifications. Use [Shadcn Alert](/docs/components/base/alert) for persistent error messages when the code is incorrect or has expired.
Pair with [Shadcn Stepper](/docs/components/base/stepper) in multi-step authentication flows where OTP verification is one step in a larger identity confirmation sequence.

item.description: Free shadcn item components for list items and interactive elements. Static and interactive list items with keyboard navigation, React item components.
item.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn item components for list rows, user profiles, content cards, and selectable lists. Shadcn Item is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI primitives and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Item: Flexible List Rows and Cards
summary: Shadcn Item is a flex container for rows, cards, and content blocks that combines media (avatars, icons), title, description, and optional actions. It aligns with Radix UI and Base UI primitives for composition, and is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options.
It displays consistent, scannable list rows across dashboards, user mentions, search results, and content feeds without requiring custom grid logic.

ReUI lists item components in dashboards, selectable lists, and user mention flows so you evaluate media sizing, action placement, and density next to a [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid) or [Shadcn Table](/components/table). The sections below cover what it is, why teams use it, and integration components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Item?
paragraphs: In UI terms, an item is a row or card that displays structured content. Shadcn Item provides sub-components for media, title, description, and actions so layout stays consistent without manual grid alignment.

Components here show user list rows with avatars, search results with icons and metadata, and selectable items with checkboxes or radio buttons. They help you decide item height, action visibility, and hover states before using the same component across dashboards and feeds.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Item?
paragraphs: Items speed up list building by providing a ready-made row structure. Instead of writing grid or flex boilerplate for each media-title-description combination, you use ItemMedia, ItemContent, ItemTitle, and add actions as needed.

Searchers often look for "shadcn item", "react list row", or "tailwind content card". This page answers those intents with layouts that show items inside [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid), [Shadcn Sortable](/docs/components/base/sortable) lists, and selectable [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) flows.

featureItems.title: Media Slot.
featureItems.description: ItemMedia renders avatars, icons, or images so visual context is consistently sized and aligned.

featureItems.title: Title and Description.
featureItems.description: ItemContent, ItemTitle, and description text together create scannable rows with clear hierarchy.

featureItems.title: Actions Support.
featureItems.description: Optional trailing buttons or menus for delete, edit, or detail actions without breaking the row structure.

featureItems.title: Selectable Variants.
featureItems.description: Pair with [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) or [Shadcn Radio Group](/components/radio-group) so list items become interactive without custom state logic.

featureItems.title: Size and Variant Control.
featureItems.description: Adjust height and visual style (transparent, outlined, muted) to match your density and hierarchy needs.

faqs.question: What is a Shadcn Item used for in real products?
faqs.answer: User list rows, search results, mention dropdowns, file lists, and notification feeds. Anywhere you need a consistent media-title-description row layout, an item is faster than writing grid boilerplate.

faqs.question: How do I make items selectable with checkboxes?
faqs.answer: Render a [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) inside the item or as a leading element. Manage checkbox state in a parent component and wire onChange to your selection logic. This component works well inside [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid) or custom list wrappers.

faqs.question: Can I use items as links to detail pages?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use the asChild prop and wrap the item with a Link component from Next.js or React Router. Focus and hover states will apply to the anchor element so the entire row feels clickable.

faqs.question: How do I control item height and spacing?
faqs.answer: Use the size prop (sm, default, lg) to adjust padding and internal spacing. For custom heights, modify the underlying Tailwind classes. Keep items aligned with your spacing scale so they nest cleanly inside [Shadcn Card](/components/card) or page sections.

faqs.question: Is Shadcn Item accessible for keyboard and screen reader users?
faqs.answer: The component uses semantic HTML (divs with proper roles when needed). Pair with [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) or native button/link semantics for interaction. Test focus order and announce actions clearly so screen readers understand list structure and available commands.

faqs.question: Are ReUI item components different from a single item example?
faqs.answer: Yes. ReUI shows items inside dashboards, searchable lists, and sortable groups. You see item density, action menus, and integration with [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid), [Shadcn Sortable](/docs/components/base/sortable), and [Shadcn Button](/components/button) groups together.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Use items inside a [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid) or custom list wrapper for rows. Pair with [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) or [Shadcn Radio Group](/components/radio-group) for selection. Add [Shadcn Dropdown Menu](/components/dropdown-menu) as a trailing action for edit, delete, or detail links.

Combine items with [Shadcn Avatar](/components/avatar) for user rows and [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for status labels. Use [Shadcn Sortable](/docs/components/base/sortable) to make item lists drag-and-drop reorderable.

Render items inside a [Shadcn Card](/components/card) section or [Shadcn Scroll Area](/components/scroll-area) for bounded lists. Add [Shadcn Input](/components/input) search above the list to filter items in real-time.

kanban.description: {{count}} free shadcn kanban board components for drag-and-drop task boards, sprint planning, and roadmap management. Copy-ready React kanban with Tailwind CSS.
kanban.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn kanban board components using `@dnd-kit/core` and `@dnd-kit/sortable`. Covers placeholder and dynamic drag overlays, frame-column layouts, minimal stacked boards, and feature roadmap views with progress tracking : all composable with shadcn Card, Badge, and Avatar.

content.title: Shadcn Kanban: Drag-and-Drop Task Boards for React
summary: ReUI Kanban is a custom shadcn component built on `@dnd-kit/core` and `@dnd-kit/sortable` : the same collision-detection engine used across ReUI's Sortable and Data Grid components. Cards drag between columns with pointer and keyboard support, drag overlays prevent layout shift, and the board state is plain React state you can wire to any backend.
Five components cover placeholder overlay, dynamic overlay, frame-column layout, minimal stacked boards, and a feature roadmap view with per-column progress tracking.
The sections below cover what ReUI Kanban provides, why teams adopt it for product management and CRM, and how it composes with Card, Badge, Avatar, and other shadcn primitives.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Kanban?
paragraphs: ReUI Kanban is a custom drag-and-drop board component for React, not part of the base shadcn/ui library. It is built on `@dnd-kit/core` for sensor and collision detection, `@dnd-kit/sortable` for the sortable context around cards and columns, and composes shadcn Card, Badge, Button, Avatar, and Progress for card content.

Each board component exposes columns and cards as plain data structures : arrays of column objects containing card arrays. So you can sync state to a REST API, GraphQL mutation, or Next.js Server Action after each drag. The drag overlay duplicates the active card during a drag, keeping the source column stable and giving users a clear visual target.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Kanban?
paragraphs: Kanban boards appear in project management tools, product roadmaps, CRM pipelines, sprint planning views, and content calendars. DnD Kit is the industry standard for accessible drag-and-drop in React : it handles touch, mouse, and keyboard sensors, ARIA announcements, and scroll-container detection without a large bundle footprint.

ReUI components show boards in realistic frame containers with real card content : assignees, labels, due dates, progress. So you can judge composition and density before implementation. Pair with Shadcn Data Grid for list views and Shadcn Filters for board-level filtering.

featureItems.title: DnD Kit Core and Sortable.
featureItems.description: Built on `@dnd-kit/core` and `@dnd-kit/sortable`. Pointer, touch, and keyboard sensors work out of the box. Collision detection handles both same-column reordering and cross-column card moves.

featureItems.title: Placeholder and Dynamic Drag Overlays.
featureItems.description: Two overlay strategies: placeholder overlay leaves an empty slot in the source column during drag; dynamic overlay animates the card without a placeholder. Choose based on desired visual feedback.

featureItems.title: Frame-Column Layout.
featureItems.description: Columns render inside shadcn Frame containers with scrollable card lists. Keeps board chrome consistent with the rest of a dashboard layout.

featureItems.title: Feature Roadmap Variant.
featureItems.description: Built-in roadmap component groups cards by status with per-column Progress indicators. Ready to wire into product planning, ticket tracking, or release management systems.

featureItems.title: Composable Card Content.
featureItems.description: Cards compose shadcn Card, Badge, Avatar, Button, and Progress. Add assignees, priority labels, due dates, and story points without breaking DnD Kit pointer-event targets.

featureItems.title: Plain State : Wire to Any Backend.
featureItems.description: Board state is an array of column objects. After each drag, call your Server Action, REST endpoint, or GraphQL mutation with the new order. No proprietary state management required.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Accessible Reordering.
featureItems.description: DnD Kit's keyboard sensor lets users move cards with arrow keys and confirm drops with Space or Enter : meeting WCAG 2.1 success criteria for drag-and-drop alternatives.

sections.title: ReUI Kanban: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Kanban is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project : following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Kanban is a custom drag-and-drop board component not available in base shadcn/ui. It is powered by `@dnd-kit/core` for collision detection and sensor management, and `@dnd-kit/sortable` for the sortable context around cards and columns. Five components cover placeholder overlay, dynamic overlay, frame-column layout, minimal stacked board, and feature roadmap with Progress tracking.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling : only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 17 such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version : API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version : API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: How do I persist board state after dragging a card to a new column?
faqs.answer: Call your backend in the onDragEnd callback after updating local state. Use optimistic updates with Sonner toasts for error rollback : the same component ReUI uses in the Sortable and Data Grid CRUD examples.

faqs.question: Can I restrict cards from dragging between certain columns?
faqs.answer: Yes. In your onDragEnd handler, check the source and destination column IDs. If the move is not allowed, skip the state update and optionally show a Sonner toast explaining why the drag was cancelled.

faqs.question: How do I enable keyboard drag and drop for accessibility?
faqs.answer: DnD Kit's keyboard sensor handles this out of the box. Use arrow keys to move cards within a column, then press Space or Enter to confirm. Test with a screen reader to ensure ARIA announcements are clear and users understand the drag interaction.

faqs.question: How do I implement optimistic UI when dragging cards?
faqs.answer: Update local state immediately when the drag ends. Show a Sonner toast with undo action. If the API call fails, revert the local state and show an error toast. This gives users fast feedback while keeping the backend in sync.

faqs.question: Does the kanban board handle many cards efficiently?
faqs.answer: DnD Kit is performant with hundreds of cards. Virtual scrolling within columns (using TanStack React Virtual) can further improve performance for very large boards. Test with your target data volume to ensure smooth dragging.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Pair kanban with Shadcn Data Grid to offer a list-view toggle. Users can switch between the board and a sortable, filterable table of the same records : a common component in project management and CRM tools.
Use Shadcn Badge for priority and status labels on cards, Shadcn Avatar for assignees, and Shadcn Progress for story-point or task-completion indicators inside each card.
Wrap the board in a Shadcn Frame for a toolbar with view toggles, Shadcn Filters for board-level filtering, and Shadcn Sonner for async feedback when card moves are persisted to a backend.

kbd.description: {{count}} free shadcn kbd components for keyboard shortcuts, command hints, and hotkey references. Display React keyboard keys with Tailwind CSS styling.
kbd.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn kbd components for displaying keyboard shortcuts, command hints, and hotkey references. Shadcn Kbd is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI components and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Kbd: Keyboard Shortcuts and Commands
summary: Shadcn Kbd is a semantic component for displaying keyboard keys and key combinations. It maps to HTML kbd semantics while providing Tailwind styling and Radix compatibility, and aligns with Shadcn Create style options.
It renders keyboard shortcuts, command hints, and hotkey references in a consistent, recognizable style so users discover keyboard navigation without tooltips.

ReUI lists kbd components in help sections, command palettes, and documentation so you evaluate spacing, grouping, and context next to a Shadcn Button or Shadcn Tooltip. The sections below cover what it is, why teams use it, and integration components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Kbd?
paragraphs: In UI terms, a kbd is a semantic element that renders keyboard key or key combination. Shadcn Kbd wraps the HTML kbd element with Tailwind styling so shortcuts look polished and recognizable.

Components here show single keys (Enter, Esc), modifier combinations (Cmd+K, Ctrl+Shift+P), and groups of related shortcuts. They help you decide key styling, spacing, and placement before documenting commands in help, settings, or command palettes.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Kbd?
paragraphs: Kbds teach users about keyboard shortcuts without interrupting their flow. A quick visual reference (Cmd+K) is clearer than prose ("press Command and K together"). Teams use them in help sections, tooltips, and in-app command hints to make power-user features discoverable.

Searchers often look for "shadcn kbd", "react keyboard shortcut", or "tailwind key display". This page answers those intents with layouts that combine kbd elements with Shadcn Button hints, Shadcn Tooltip, and Shadcn Command palettes.

featureItems.title: Semantic HTML.
featureItems.description: Uses the HTML kbd element so search engines and assistive tech recognize it as keyboard input.

featureItems.title: Single and Combined Keys.
featureItems.description: Display single keys (Enter) or combinations (Cmd+K) with proper spacing and separators.

featureItems.title: KbdGroup for Organization.
featureItems.description: Group related shortcuts together so documentation stays scannable and organized.

featureItems.title: Tailwind Styling.
featureItems.description: Background, border, and typography respect Shadcn Create tokens so keys match your design system.

featureItems.title: Integration with Buttons and Tooltips.
featureItems.description: Pair with Shadcn Button titles or Shadcn Tooltip content to hint at available shortcuts.

faqs.question: Should I use the semantic kbd element or just style text visually?
faqs.answer: Always use kbd for semantic value. Screen readers can understand they are keyboard controls, search engines can index them properly, and your design system can style them consistently. Plain text feels inconsistent.

faqs.question: How do I display Mac (Cmd) and Windows (Ctrl) shortcuts together?
faqs.answer: Check the user's platform (navigator.platform or useEffect) and render the appropriate key (Cmd vs Ctrl). Some apps show both with a slash (Cmd/Ctrl+K) for clarity, though this can feel cluttered. A single platform-appropriate version is usually better.

faqs.question: What is the best placement for keyboard shortcut hints on a button?
faqs.answer: Display shortcuts in button title attributes or a Shadcn Tooltip on hover. This pairs the visual action with its keyboard equivalent without cluttering the button label. Command palettes and help documentation can show comprehensive lists.

faqs.question: Can I render a kbd inside a tooltip or button?
faqs.answer: Yes. Render kbd inside a Shadcn Button title attribute or a Shadcn Tooltip to show the shortcut hint. This visually pairs the action with its keyboard equivalent and teaches users shortcuts through exploration.

faqs.question: Is the Shadcn Kbd component accessible for all users?
faqs.answer: Yes. The semantic kbd element tells assistive tech it's a keyboard control. You still need to verify that the keyboard shortcut actually works (wired correctly in your app) and that documentation is clear. Avoid relying on kbd alone without prose context.

faqs.question: How do ReUI kbd components differ from a minimal single-key example?
faqs.answer: ReUI shows kbd in command palettes, help sections, and button tooltips. You see grouping, platform variants, and integration with Shadcn Button and Shadcn Tooltip together, which is closer to production documentation.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Add kbd elements inside Shadcn Button title attributes to hint at keyboard shortcuts. Use Shadcn Tooltip to display fuller shortcut hints on hover or focus.
Display kbd groups in a Shadcn Card help section or inside a Shadcn Dialog for a dedicated shortcuts reference. Pair with prose and Shadcn Accordion to organize shortcuts by category.
Use kbd in a Shadcn Command palette to show available shortcuts. Combine with Shadcn Input search to filter commands and links to actions.

label.description: {{count}} free shadcn label components for form field labels and accessible associations. Connect React form inputs to labels with Tailwind CSS styling.
label.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn label components for accessible form fields, label associations, and semantic form structure. Shadcn Label is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI components and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Label: Accessible Form Field Labels
summary: Shadcn Label is a semantic component for associating text labels with form inputs. It maps to HTML label semantics while providing Tailwind styling and Radix compatibility, and aligns with Shadcn Create style options.
It creates proper label-input associations so screen readers announce context and users get a larger click area on the label to focus inputs.

ReUI lists label components alongside form Shadcn Field, Shadcn Input, Shadcn Checkbox, and Shadcn Radio Group so you see complete, accessible forms. The sections below cover what it is, why it matters, and integration components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Label?
paragraphs: In form terms, a label is text that identifies a field or control. Shadcn Label wraps the HTML label element with Tailwind styling and manages htmlFor association so clicking the label focuses the input.

Components here show required field indicators, optional badges, and label alignment with inputs, checkboxes, and radio buttons. They help you decide label placement, font weight, and density before building accessible forms at scale.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Label?
paragraphs: Labels reduce confusion by clarifying what data each field expects. They also provide larger click targets on the label text itself, improving usability for all users, especially on mobile. Screen readers rely on label-input association to explain controls correctly.

Searchers often look for "shadcn label", "react form label", or "accessible label html". This page answers those intents with layouts that pair labels with Shadcn Input, Shadcn Checkbox, Shadcn Field, and validation components.

featureItems.title: HtmlFor Association.
featureItems.description: The htmlFor prop creates a proper label-input association so clicking the label focuses the input.

featureItems.title: Semantic HTML.
featureItems.description: Uses the native HTML label element so screen readers and browsers understand it correctly.

featureItems.title: Tailwind Styling.
featureItems.description: Font, weight, and color respect Shadcn Create tokens so labels match your form design system.

featureItems.title: Optional and Required Indicators.
featureItems.description: Render required asterisks or optional badges inside the label without semantic confusion.

featureItems.title: Works with All Input Types.
featureItems.description: Pair with Shadcn Input, Shadcn Checkbox, Shadcn Radio Group, and Shadcn Select fields.

faqs.question: Should I use a label or placeholder text to describe a form field?
faqs.answer: Always use a real label paired with htmlFor. Placeholder text disappears when the user types, so it shouldn't be the only description. Treat placeholder as a hint only; the label is always visible and identifies the field.

faqs.question: How do I create a proper label-input association?
faqs.answer: Use the htmlFor prop on the label (matches the input id) or use aria-labelledby. The first is clearer and more compatible. Example: <Label htmlFor="email">Email</Label> <Input id="email" />

faqs.question: Should I display required (*) or optional badges on labels?
faqs.answer: Be explicit about requirements. Add a required asterisk (*) or "required" text for mandatory fields. For optional fields, you can mark them optional or leave it implicit if most fields are optional. Keep the component consistent across your form.

faqs.question: Is Shadcn Label accessible when using htmlFor properly?
faqs.answer: Yes. Screen readers announce the label when the input receives focus. Test with real assistive tech to ensure form structure is clear and error messages are linked correctly with aria-describedby.

faqs.question: Can I use labels with checkboxes and radio buttons?
faqs.answer: Yes. Labels work with any input type. For checkboxes and radio buttons, the label often wraps the input or sits next to it, and the label click toggles the control. Use htmlFor to associate explicitly or let the input be inside the label.

faqs.question: How do ReUI label components differ from a minimal example?
faqs.answer: ReUI shows labels in complete forms with Shadcn Field, Shadcn Input, Shadcn Checkbox, Shadcn Radio Group, and validation components together. You see density, alignment, and error messaging in realistic context.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair labels with Shadcn Input, Shadcn Checkbox, Shadcn Radio Group, and Shadcn Select using the htmlFor association. Wrap everything in Shadcn Field to add descriptions and error messages.
Use labels with Shadcn Number Field and Shadcn Input Group for complete form sections. Add Shadcn Tooltip on the label to explain non-obvious fields.

Combine labels with Shadcn Button (e.g., a submit or clear button) to create standalone form blocks. Use form library integration like React Hook Form to manage validation and display errors near the label and field.

menubar.description: {{count}} free shadcn menubar components for desktop application menus and hierarchical command organization. Build React menubars with Tailwind CSS styling.
menubar.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn menubar components for application menus, desktop navigation, and hierarchical command structure. Shadcn Menubar is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI menu components and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Menubar: Desktop App Menus
summary: Shadcn Menubar is a persistent horizontal menu bar that organizes command groups, common in desktop applications. It maps to Radix UI menubar semantics so keyboard navigation and focus management stay predictable, and aligns with Shadcn Create style options.
It displays File, Edit, View, and other menus in a persistent top bar so users have consistent access to commands and submenus.

ReUI lists menubar components in desktop-style apps and web tools so you evaluate menu nesting, icon usage, and keyboard shortcuts next to a Shadcn Dropdown Menu or Shadcn Navigation Menu. The sections below cover what it is, why teams use it, and integration components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Menubar?
paragraphs: In desktop UI terms, a menubar is a horizontal row of menu triggers that open submenus with commands. Shadcn Menubar follows Radix UI components for menu interaction, keyboard navigation, and focus management.

Components here show typical app menus (File, Edit, View, Help) with submenus and nested actions. They help you decide menu depth, icon usage, and command organization before implementing the same structure in a web-based desktop tool.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Menubar?
paragraphs: Menubars organize commands in a familiar, persistent UI component. Desktop users expect File, Edit, and View menus. Web apps that mimic desktop behavior feel native and discoverable. Keyboard shortcuts and hierarchical nesting help power users work faster.

Searchers often look for "shadcn menubar", "react desktop menu", or "tailwind app menu". This page answers those intents with layouts that show menubars with Shadcn Dropdown Menu submenus, Shadcn Kbd shortcuts, and Shadcn Separator dividers.

featureItems.title: Horizontal Menu Organization.
featureItems.description: Top-level menu triggers sit in a horizontal bar with submenus opening below, matching desktop application conventions.

featureItems.title: Nested Submenus.
featureItems.description: MenubarSub, MenubarSubTrigger, and MenubarSubContent enable multi-level command hierarchies.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Navigation.
featureItems.description: Arrow keys move between menu items, Enter activates commands, Escape closes menus. Full keyboard access without mouse.

featureItems.title: Checkbox and Radio Items.
featureItems.description: MenubarCheckboxItem and MenubarRadioItem for toggles and choices within menus.

featureItems.title: Shortcut Display.
featureItems.description: Render keyboard shortcuts next to menu items using Shadcn Kbd so users learn power commands.

faqs.question: When should I choose a menubar over a dropdown menu or navigation menu?
faqs.answer: Use menubar when you're building a desktop-style app with persistent File, Edit, View, and Help menus. Use Shadcn Dropdown Menu for a button-triggered menu. Use Shadcn Navigation Menu for primary site navigation.

faqs.question: How do I add keyboard shortcuts to menubar items?
faqs.answer: Render Shadcn Kbd inside each menu item label (e.g., "Save Cmd+S"). Wire actual keyboard event listeners to your command handlers so the shortcuts actually work.

faqs.question: Is it okay to have deeply nested submenus in a menubar?
faqs.answer: Avoid deeply nested menus. Aim for one or two levels at most. Beyond that, navigation becomes hard to follow. Consider a different layout (sidebar, tabs) to avoid menu fatigue.

faqs.question: Is Shadcn Menubar accessible for keyboard and screen reader users?
faqs.answer: Yes. Built on Radix UI, it provides keyboard shortcuts and semantic roles. Screen readers announce menus and commands. Ensure actual commands are wired correctly and labels are clear so the menu is functional, not just styled.

faqs.question: How do I show which menu or command is currently active?
faqs.answer: Use conditional CSS classes or the checked state on MenubarCheckboxItem to show which menu is active. This helps users understand context, especially in multi-view applications.

faqs.question: How do ReUI menubar components differ from a minimal storybook example?
faqs.answer: ReUI shows menubars in desktop-style app layouts with full File, Edit, View hierarchies. You see keyboard shortcuts, nested submenus, and context together, which is closer to real application structure than a single menu example.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Use Shadcn Kbd inside menubar items to display keyboard shortcuts. Pair with Shadcn Dropdown Menu for checkbox or radio options within menu sections.
Add Shadcn Separator to group related commands visually. Combine with Shadcn Dialog or Shadcn Drawer when a menu item opens a form or settings panel.

Render menu item icons from lucide-react for visual scanning. Use Shadcn Tooltip on menu items to explain less obvious commands. Combine menubars with a main content area and optional Shadcn Sidebar for full app structure.

native-select.description: {{count}} free shadcn native select components for mobile-optimized native browser dropdowns and form input controls. Style HTML select with Tailwind CSS.
native-select.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn native-select components for native browser dropdowns, mobile-optimized selects, and form choice controls. Shadcn Native Select is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI form components and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Native Select: Browser-Native Dropdowns
summary: Shadcn Native Select is a styled wrapper around the HTML select element. It provides browser-native behavior, better mobile UX, and performance benefits while maintaining Tailwind styling and Radix compatibility.
It renders the native OS picker on mobile and a styled dropdown on desktop, giving users the best interaction component for their device.

ReUI lists native-select components in forms, filters, and settings so you evaluate when native is better than custom Shadcn Select. The sections below cover what it is, why teams use it, and integration components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Native Select?
paragraphs: In form terms, a native select is the HTML select element with light styling. Shadcn Native Select wraps it with Tailwind classes so it fits your design system while keeping browser-native behavior.

Components here show country selects, category choices, and filter dropdowns. They help you decide when native is better than custom Shadcn Select, which offers more flexibility but less mobile optimization.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Native Select?
paragraphs: Native selects are faster, more accessible, and work better on mobile. The browser renders the appropriate picker (iOS, Android) without JavaScript. They reduce bundle size and improve form performance compared to custom Shadcn Select.

Searchers often look for "shadcn native select", "react dropdown", or "html select styling". This page answers those intents with layouts that show native selects in forms with Shadcn Field and Shadcn Button actions.

featureItems.title: Browser-Native Picker.
featureItems.description: Mobile devices render the native OS picker (iOS wheel, Android list) for better UX and faster interaction.

featureItems.title: Zero JavaScript Overhead.
featureItems.description: Uses the HTML select element so no custom logic is needed, reducing bundle size and improving performance.

featureItems.title: Tailwind Styling.
featureItems.description: Border, focus ring, and text colors respect Shadcn Create tokens so the select matches your design system.

featureItems.title: Option Groups.
featureItems.description: NativeSelectOptGroup organizes related options together for clarity in long lists.

featureItems.title: Disabled and Required States.
featureItems.description: Standard HTML attributes and visual feedback so users understand field status and validation.

faqs.question: Should I use native-select or the custom Shadcn Select component?
faqs.answer: Use native-select when you prioritize mobile UX and performance. Native pickers work great on phones. Use Shadcn Select when you need custom styling, search, or async loading. Most mobile-first apps prefer native-select.

faqs.question: Can I add search or filtering inside a native select dropdown?
faqs.answer: No. Native selects don't support searchable content in the dropdown. If you need search, use Shadcn Select instead, which offers custom rendering and filtering.

faqs.question: How do I disable specific options in a native select?
faqs.answer: Use the disabled attribute on individual NativeSelectOption elements. The browser will gray out those options and prevent selection.

faqs.question: Is Shadcn Native Select accessible for all users?
faqs.answer: Yes. Native selects are semantically correct HTML, so screen readers understand them. Pair with a Shadcn Label using htmlFor to create proper association, and test with assistive tech to confirm announcements are clear.

faqs.question: Can I customize the appearance of the native dropdown options?
faqs.answer: No. The browser controls the appearance of the picker and options. You can style the closed select, but not the open dropdown menu. If you need full control, use Shadcn Select instead.

faqs.question: How do ReUI native-select components differ from a minimal example?
faqs.answer: ReUI shows native selects in forms with Shadcn Field, Shadcn Label, and validation components. You see them compared to Shadcn Select so you understand when to choose native vs. custom.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair native selects with Shadcn Field to add labels, descriptions, and error messages. Use Shadcn Label with htmlFor for proper form association.
Combine with Shadcn Button for apply or clear actions next to the select. Use native-select in filter bars and form sections alongside Shadcn Input and other controls.
Place native selects in Shadcn Card sections or Shadcn Dialog for settings and preferences. Compare with Shadcn Select in real forms to decide which fits your performance and UX needs.

navigation-menu.description: {{count}} free shadcn navigation menu components for primary site navigation and rich mega menus. Build React site navigation with Tailwind CSS styling.

Shadcn Navigation Menu is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI menu components and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Navigation Menu: Hierarchical Site Navigation
summary: Shadcn Navigation Menu is a hierarchical menu for primary site navigation and mega menus. It maps to Radix UI menu semantics so keyboard navigation and focus management work reliably, and aligns with Shadcn Create style options.
It displays top-level links with optional submenus so users navigate content hierarchy without losing context.

ReUI lists navigation-menu components in site headers and landing pages so you evaluate trigger placement, submenu depth, and icon usage next to a Shadcn Dropdown Menu or Shadcn Menubar. The sections below cover what it is, why teams use it, and integration components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Navigation Menu?
paragraphs: In site terms, a navigation menu is a list of links for main content categories, often with submenus. Shadcn Navigation Menu uses Radix UI components for keyboard navigation, focus management, and accessibility.
Components here show product category menus, resource link hierarchies, and mega menus with rich content. They help you decide trigger layout, submenu depth, and whether to use text, icons, or both.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Navigation Menu?
paragraphs: Navigation menus organize site structure in a persistent, familiar component. Top-level triggers hint at content below; submenus let users explore without leaving the current page. They reduce clicks and keep users in context.

Searchers often look for "shadcn navigation menu", "react site navigation", or "tailwind mega menu". This page answers those intents with layouts that show navigation menus in headers with Shadcn Button actions and logo placement.

featureItems.title: Top-Level Triggers.
featureItems.description: NavigationMenuItem and NavigationMenuTrigger create main menu categories that open submenus on hover or click.

featureItems.title: Rich Submenu Content.
featureItems.description: NavigationMenuContent renders any React component inside, so submenus can be text links, icons, images, or custom layouts.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Navigation.
featureItems.description: Arrow keys, Enter, and Escape work out of the box. Tab moves through menu items. Full keyboard access without mouse.

featureItems.title: Automatic Viewport Positioning.
featureItems.description: Submenus automatically position themselves to avoid overflow and stay visible.

featureItems.title: Indicator and Icons.
featureItems.description: Render chevrons or icons on triggers so users know there are submenus. Icons improve scannability.

faqs.question: When should I use navigation-menu instead of dropdown menu or menubar?
faqs.answer: Use navigation-menu for primary site structure and mega menus. Use Shadcn Dropdown Menu for contextual, secondary actions. Use Shadcn Menubar for desktop app commands. Each serves a different navigational purpose.

faqs.question: How do I add icons and descriptions to navigation menu links?
faqs.answer: Render custom content inside NavigationMenuContent. Use icons from lucide-react alongside text and descriptions. This creates richer, more scannable navigation than plain text links.

faqs.question: How do I make navigation menus responsive and mobile-friendly?
faqs.answer: Use Tailwind responsive utilities to hide the navigation menu on mobile and show a Shadcn Drawer or Shadcn Sheet instead. This keeps navigation usable on small screens.

faqs.question: Is Shadcn Navigation Menu accessible for keyboard and screen reader users?
faqs.answer: Yes. Built on Radix UI, it provides keyboard shortcuts and semantic roles. Screen readers announce menu structure and links. Ensure triggers are properly labeled and focus order is logical.

faqs.question: How do I highlight the active page or current section?
faqs.answer: Use conditional CSS classes on menu items to highlight the active link. Update this based on the current route (Next.js useRouter or React Router). This helps users know where they are in the site.

faqs.question: How do ReUI navigation-menu components differ from a minimal example?
faqs.answer: ReUI shows navigation menus in full site headers with logo, buttons, and responsive behavior. You see mega menus with icons, rich content, and integration with Shadcn Drawer for mobile, which is closer to production headers.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Combine navigation menus with a logo and Shadcn Button (search, sign in) in a site header. Use Tailwind sticky utilities to keep the navigation visible while scrolling.
On mobile, hide the navigation menu and show a Shadcn Drawer or Shadcn Sheet that opens via a hamburger menu button. This keeps navigation accessible at all screen sizes.
Render rich submenu content using Shadcn Card layouts, CSS Grid, or custom columns. Add Shadcn Badge for "new" or "featured" items. Pair with internal links to detailed resource pages.

number-field.description: {{count}} free shadcn number field components for numeric input with spinners and validation. Accept numbers in React forms with Tailwind CSS styling.
number-field.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn number-field components for numeric entry, stepper controls, and quantity selection. Shadcn Number Field is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI form components and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Number Field: Numeric Entry with Spinners
summary: Shadcn Number Field is a numeric input component with increment/decrement buttons (spinners) and keyboard step support. It maintains semantic HTML and accessibility while providing Tailwind styling and Radix compatibility.
It handles numeric-only input, prevents invalid values, and provides visual spinner controls so users can increment or decrement values without typing.

ReUI lists number-field components in product quantity selectors, price filters, and quantity forms so you evaluate spinner placement, button sizing, and validation next to a Shadcn Input or Shadcn Field. The sections below cover what it is, why teams use it, and integration components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Number Field?
paragraphs: In form terms, a number field is an input constrained to numeric values. Shadcn Number Field provides spinner buttons for increment/decrement, keyboard shortcuts (up/down arrows), and validation so only valid numbers are entered.

Components here show product quantity selectors, price range filters, and age pickers. They help you decide spinner styling, minimum/maximum values, and step size before implementing numeric entry throughout your app.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Number Field?
paragraphs: Number fields reduce errors by constraining input to numeric values. Spinners let users adjust values without typing. They improve mobile UX where on-screen number keyboards can feel slow. Use them for quantities, prices, ratings, and any numeric choice.

Searchers often look for "shadcn number field", "react numeric input", or "tailwind spinner input". This page answers those intents with layouts that show number fields in checkout steps, filter panels, and product pages.

featureItems.title: Increment/Decrement Spinners.
featureItems.description: Up and down buttons for adjusting the value without typing, with keyboard support (arrow keys).

featureItems.title: Min and Max Constraints.
featureItems.description: Set valid number ranges so users can't enter out-of-bounds values. Spinners disable at boundaries.

featureItems.title: Step Control.
featureItems.description: Define step size (1, 5, 10) for keyboard and spinner increments, matching your domain (quantities, prices).

featureItems.title: Keyboard Shortcuts.
featureItems.description: Arrow keys increment/decrement; Page Up/Down for larger steps. Full keyboard control without spinners.

featureItems.title: Numeric-Only Input.
featureItems.description: Validation prevents non-numeric characters, decimals (unless allowed), and invalid formats.

sections.title: ReUI Number Field: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Number Field is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project : following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Number Field is a custom numeric input component built on `@base-ui/react` NumberField, not available in base shadcn/ui. It provides increment and decrement stepper buttons, min/max/step constraints, and keyboard-controlled value changes with full WAI-ARIA spinbutton semantics. Integrates with `react-hook-form` and `zod` for form validation.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling : only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 17 such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version : API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: Can I use a number field to input decimal values like prices?
faqs.answer: Yes. Most number fields support decimals by default. You can set step to 0.1 or 0.01 to allow smaller increments. For currency, set step to 0.01 to match cents.

faqs.question: How do I set minimum and maximum values on a number field?
faqs.answer: Use min and max props. Spinners become disabled when the value reaches the boundary, and validation prevents users from typing out-of-range values.

faqs.question: What is the difference between a number field and a plain input type=number?
faqs.answer: Number field adds spinner buttons, keyboard step support, and clear min/max/step props. Plain input type=number offers less control over styling and behavior. Number field is better for intentional numeric entry workflows.

faqs.question: Is Shadcn Number Field accessible for all users?
faqs.answer: Yes. Keyboard arrow keys, spinner buttons, and text input all work. Pair with a Shadcn Label and Shadcn Field for proper form association. Test focus states and validation announcements with screen readers.

faqs.question: Can I integrate number field with a form library like React Hook Form?
faqs.answer: Yes. Treat it like a controlled input: wire value and onChange to your form state. Libraries like React Hook Form and TanStack Form work seamlessly with number fields for validation and error display.

faqs.question: How do ReUI number-field components differ from a minimal example?
faqs.answer: ReUI shows number fields in checkout flows, filter panels, and quantity selectors. You see spinner styling, validation states, and integration with Shadcn Field, Shadcn Button, and form contexts together.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair number fields with Shadcn Field to add labels and descriptions. Use Shadcn Label with htmlFor for proper form association so screen readers understand the field.
Combine number fields with Shadcn Input Group to add currency symbols or unit labels. Use Shadcn Button next to or around the spinner for additional actions like remove or add-to-cart.
Render number fields in Shadcn Card sections for product ordering or Shadcn Dialog for bulk quantity entry. Pair with Shadcn Slider as an alternative for value selection in a range.
