Shadcn Pagination is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI link components and is fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options so your previews match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Pagination: Page Navigation Controls
summary: Shadcn Pagination is a navigation component for breaking large datasets into pages. It provides previous/next buttons, numbered pages, and ellipsis for large page ranges. It aligns with Radix UI and Base UI link components, and is fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options.
It helps users navigate long lists, tables, and search results without loading all data at once.

ReUI lists pagination components alongside Shadcn Table and Shadcn Data Grid so you evaluate button placement, page number display, and responsive behavior. The sections below cover what it is, why teams use it, and integration components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Pagination?
paragraphs: In UI terms, pagination is a navigation control for moving between pages of results. Shadcn Pagination provides previous/next buttons, clickable page numbers, and ellipsis to skip ranges so users jump to any page quickly.

Components here show table pagination, search result navigation, and list pagination. They help you decide page number visibility, button size, and responsive behavior before integrating pagination into data-heavy pages.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Pagination?
paragraphs: Pagination improves performance and UX by breaking large datasets into manageable chunks. Users see only what fits on one page, pages load faster, and users control how much data they consume. They provide clear progress (page 3 of 47) so users understand scope.

Searchers often look for "shadcn pagination", "react page navigation", or "table pagination". This page answers those intents with layouts that show pagination below Shadcn Table and Shadcn Data Grid in real dashboards.

featureItems.title: Previous and Next Buttons.
featureItems.description: Move one page forward or back, with disabled state at boundaries so users know they've reached the end.

featureItems.title: Numbered Page Links.
featureItems.description: Clickable page numbers so users jump directly to a specific page instead of clicking next repeatedly.

featureItems.title: Ellipsis for Large Ranges.
featureItems.description: PaginationEllipsis indicates skipped pages ("...") so long page lists stay compact.

featureItems.title: Next.js Link Integration.
featureItems.description: Use asChild to compose Next.js Link so pagination links work with client-side routing.

featureItems.title: Active Page Indication.
featureItems.description: Highlight the current page number so users know their location in the result set.

faqs.question: When is pagination better than infinite scroll for a list?
faqs.answer: Use pagination when users need to jump to a specific page, understand total results, or when performance matters (showing all data is slow). Use infinite scroll for discovery-driven browsing (social media, feeds) where continuous exploration is the goal.

faqs.question: How many page numbers should I show in the controls?
faqs.answer: Show 5-7 page numbers around the current page for clarity. Use ellipsis (...) to skip ranges. On mobile, show fewer numbers to save space. Always show prev/next and first/last page indicators.

faqs.question: How do I update pagination when filters change?
faqs.answer: Pagination is independent of filters. When a user applies a filter, reset to page 1. Use URL query params (page=2&filter=category) so bookmarking and sharing work correctly.

faqs.question: Is Shadcn Pagination accessible for keyboard and screen reader users?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each page link is a proper link element so keyboard Tab navigation works. Use meaningful labels (Page 3) and aria-current="page" on the active link so screen readers announce which page the user is on.

faqs.question: Where should I store pagination state: in URL query params or component state?
faqs.answer: Store it in URL query params (preferred). When a user clicks a page link, update the URL and fetch new data. This way, users can bookmark, share links, and navigate back correctly.

faqs.question: How do ReUI pagination components differ from a minimal example?
faqs.answer: ReUI shows pagination below Shadcn Table and Shadcn Data Grid in real dashboards. You see responsive behavior, integration with filters and search, and combination with data display together, which is closer to production than a single control.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Place pagination below Shadcn Table or Shadcn Data Grid for managing result sets. Add a rows-per-page selector next to pagination to let users control density.
Combine pagination with Shadcn Input search above the table. Use both together so users can search results and then paginate within them.
Pair pagination with Shadcn Card layouts for paginated content feeds or product galleries. Use Shadcn Button for load-more components as an alternative to traditional numbered pagination.

phone-input.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn phone input components for international phone number entry with country code flag dropdown. Covers basic, small, large, disabled, preset value, error state, specific default country, and read-only modes : a custom ReUI component built on Input Group primitives.

content.title: Shadcn Phone Input: International Numbers with Country Flags
summary: ReUI Phone Input is a custom shadcn component for international phone number entry. It renders a country code selector with flag icons alongside a formatted phone number input : composing shadcn Input Group, Select, and Field primitives with a country data set.

Eight components cover basic phone input, small and large size variants, disabled state, preset value initialization, error state with validation feedback, specific default country selection, and read-only display mode.
The sections below cover the component architecture, real use cases in global SaaS products, features, and form integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Phone Input?
paragraphs: ReUI Phone Input is a custom shadcn component : not in base shadcn/ui for collecting international phone numbers. It renders a flag icon and country code as a dropdown trigger alongside a number input that formats the local part. The country selector uses shadcn's native Select or custom dropdown built on Popover, and the number input wraps the shadcn Input primitive.

The component manages country code prefix and local number separately, combining them into an E.164-formatted value for form submission. Input masking applies the correct local number format for the selected country : US numbers format as (555) 555-5555, UK numbers as 07700 900000.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Phone Input?
paragraphs: Global SaaS products, e-commerce checkouts, and B2B registration flows all collect international phone numbers. A plain text input forces users to know their country code; a phone input with a country selector reduces friction and improves data quality by separating country code from local number.

Error state handling communicates invalid format (too few digits, invalid area code) and required field validation inline with the shadcn Field error slot : the same component used across all ReUI form components. Pair with Shadcn Field and Shadcn Label for consistent form layout.

featureItems.title: Country Flag and Code Selector.
featureItems.description: Country selector renders a flag emoji or SVG flag icon alongside the dial code. Users scroll or search the country list to switch dial codes. The selected country updates the input placeholder format.

featureItems.title: E.164 Format Output.
featureItems.description: The component combines the selected country dial code and local number into an E.164 international format string (+14155552671) for API submission : the standard format for Twilio, Vonage, and other telephony APIs.

featureItems.title: Default Country Preset.
featureItems.description: Initialize the selector to a specific country based on user locale, billing address, or IP geolocation : the default country component shows how to pass an ISO country code as the initial value.

featureItems.title: Preset Value and Controlled Mode.
featureItems.description: Pass an existing E.164 phone number as the value prop and the component parses it into country code and local number : useful for edit forms where an existing phone number needs to display correctly.

featureItems.title: Error State and Validation.
featureItems.description: Error state renders a red border on the input and country selector with an inline error message via shadcn Field : consistent with the validation UI of all other ReUI form inputs.

featureItems.title: Size Variants.
featureItems.description: Small, default, and large size variants scale the flag selector and number input together : matching the size of adjacent form fields in compact or spacious form layouts.

featureItems.title: Disabled and Read-Only Modes.
featureItems.description: Disabled mode prevents interaction while retaining the displayed value. Read-only mode displays the formatted number without edit controls : useful in profile summary and order confirmation screens.

sections.title: ReUI Phone Input: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Phone Input is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project : following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Phone Input is a custom international phone number component not available in base shadcn/ui. It is built on `react-phone-number-input` for country code management and E.164 formatting, wrapping the country selector and number input in shadcn Input Group primitives. Supports preset value, default country, error state, and disabled and read-only modes.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling : only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 17 such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version : API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version : API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: What is the output format for the phone number?
faqs.answer: E.164 international format : a plus sign, country dial code, and local number with no spaces or punctuation (e.g., +14155552671). This is the standard format for telephony APIs like Twilio, Vonage, and AWS SNS.

faqs.question: How do I set a default country for the selector?
faqs.answer: Pass a defaultCountry prop with an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., 'US', 'GB', 'DE'). The component initializes the flag and dial code to that country. Detect the user's country from their IP, browser locale, or billing address to pre-fill.

faqs.question: Can I validate phone numbers with Zod?
faqs.answer: Yes. After the component outputs the E.164 string, validate it with a Zod regex: z.string().regex(/^\+[1-9]\d{1,14}$/, 'Invalid phone number'). For stricter validation, integrate a library like libphonenumber-js for per-country format checking.

faqs.question: How do I use preset value to populate an existing phone number?
faqs.answer: Pass the existing E.164 phone number as the value prop. The component parses the dial code prefix to select the correct country and formats the remaining digits as the local number. The user sees the flag, country code, and formatted local number in the edit state.

faqs.question: Does the phone input work with React Hook Form?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use Controller from `react-hook-form` with the phone input as the controlled element. Pass value and onChange from the render prop. The E.164 output string is the field value submitted with the form.

faqs.question: Is ReUI Phone Input different from a standard shadcn component?
faqs.answer: Yes. ReUI Phone Input is a custom in-house component, not part of base shadcn/ui. It composes shadcn Input Group, Field, and Label primitives with international phone number formatting and country selection specific to ReUI.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Always wrap Phone Input in Shadcn Field with Shadcn Label for accessible form labeling and error message display. Use the same error slot component as other ReUI form inputs for consistent validation UX.
Pair with Shadcn Input fields for adjacent email and name fields in registration or checkout forms, maintaining consistent size variants across the form.
Combine with Shadcn Select for country selection in shipping address forms where the phone input's country and the address country should stay in sync.

popover.description: {{count}} free shadcn popover components for contextual overlays, quick edits, and interactive context menus. Float React panels with Tailwind CSS styling.
popover.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn popover components for inline menus, quick edits, and interactive overlays. Popover is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and fully aligned with Shadcn Create so radius, colors, and typography match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Popover: Contextual Overlays and Rich Content
summary: Shadcn Popover is an overlay component that displays content triggered by a button or interaction, allowing rich text, forms, or interactive elements in a floating panel. Built on Radix UI Popover with full keyboard and focus management, it aligns with Base UI-style composition conventions and is fully compatible with Shadcn Create styling.

Unlike a tooltip (which shows only read-only text on hover), a popover accepts clicks, keyboard navigation, and interactive content like buttons and inputs, making it ideal for quick edits, filters, and contextual menus.

ReUI lists popover components inside dashboards, tables, and forms so you evaluate focus traps, positioning, dismissal, and how popovers pair with Shadcn Button, Shadcn Input, and Shadcn Field. The sections below cover what the component is, why teams use it, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Popover?
paragraphs: A popover is a floating panel that appears when a user clicks a trigger element. It can contain text, buttons, forms, or any interactive content. Shadcn Popover follows Radix UI focus and dismissal components so users can tab through interactive elements and close with Escape or by clicking outside.

Components here show simple text popovers, edit forms, filter menus, and responsive positioning. They help you decide when a popover is lighter than a dialog, how to manage focus when the popover closes, and whether to include a confirmation button or auto-save the change.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Popover?
paragraphs: Teams use popovers when they need to show interactive content without opening a full dialog or page. Quick edits to a table row, filter selections, date pickers, and help text are all tasks suited to popovers because they stay contextual and let users return focus to the original action.

Searchers look for 'react popover', 'tooltip with form', and 'popover vs dialog'. This page answers with real examples showing how popovers differ from tooltips (which are read-only), how they compare to dialogs (which are modal and block interaction), and when to use each component in your product.

featureItems.title: Click-Triggered Overlay.
featureItems.description: Appears when a user clicks the trigger, persists until dismissed via Escape, clicking outside, or a cancel action. Ideal for non-blocking, lightweight tasks.

featureItems.title: Rich Content Support.
featureItems.description: Holds text, buttons, forms, and any interactive element, unlike tooltips which display read-only hints on hover.

featureItems.title: Focus Management.
featureItems.description: Focus automatically moves into the popover when it opens and returns to the trigger when dismissed, following Radix UI conventions for accessible overlays.

featureItems.title: Smart Positioning.
featureItems.description: Automatically adjusts position to stay within viewport; use side and align props to prefer top, bottom, left, or right placement.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Navigation.
featureItems.description: Tab cycles through interactive elements inside the popover; Escape dismisses it. Pair with Shadcn Button and Shadcn Input for full keyboard support.

faqs.question: What is the difference between a popover and a tooltip?
faqs.answer: A tooltip displays read-only text on hover and hides when the mouse moves away. A popover shows on click, can contain interactive content (forms, buttons), and requires explicit dismissal. Use tooltips for lightweight hints; use popovers for tasks that need user input or confirmation.

faqs.question: When should I use a popover instead of a dialog or modal?
faqs.answer: Use a popover for lightweight, non-blocking tasks like quick edits, filters, or help text. Dialogs are better for important decisions or multi-step flows that need the user's full attention and should block page interaction until resolved.

faqs.question: How do I handle form submission inside a popover?
faqs.answer: Place form inputs inside the popover body. When the user clicks a submit button, validate and either close the popover (with optional confirmation toast) or show an error Shadcn Alert inside the popover so they can correct it.

faqs.question: How do I position a popover to appear at specific corners?
faqs.answer: Use the side prop to set 'top', 'bottom', 'left', or 'right', and align prop to set 'start', 'center', or 'end'. Test on mobile to ensure the popover stays within the viewport.

faqs.question: Can I open a popover on page load or by default?
faqs.answer: Yes. Manage open state with a React hook and pass it to PopoverContent via the open prop. Useful for onboarding overlays or guided tours, but avoid opening multiple popovers at once to prevent focus confusion.

faqs.question: Is popover accessible for keyboard and screen reader users?
faqs.answer: Yes. Radix UI Popover follows WAI-ARIA guidance: Tab navigates content, Escape closes, and roles are announced. Test with a screen reader to confirm the trigger is labeled and the popover relationship is clear.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Trigger popovers with a Shadcn Button or icon button. Use a Shadcn Tooltip for lightweight hints on icons, and a popover when the icon opens an interactive menu or filter panel.
Place Shadcn Field, Shadcn Input, or Shadcn Select inside the popover for quick-edit workflows. Combine with Shadcn Button (primary) and a close/cancel option for clear submission flow.

Pair popovers with Shadcn Hover Card for richer preview content. Use Shadcn Date Selector inside a popover when date picking needs context-sensitive positioning, and Shadcn Command for searchable option lists.

progress.description: {{count}} free shadcn progress components for progress bars, upload tracking, and task completion indicators. Visualize React progress with Tailwind CSS.
progress.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn progress components for file uploads, long operations, and task completion tracking. Progress is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI stacks and fully aligned with Shadcn Create so radius, colors, and typography match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Progress: Visualize Task Completion and Status
summary: Shadcn Progress is a visual indicator that shows the completion percentage of a task: file uploads, downloads, form validation, or long-running operations. Built on Radix UI Progress with full WAI-ARIA support, it fits seamlessly into Radix and Base UI stacks while maintaining full compatibility with Shadcn Create style tokens.

It displays a horizontal or vertical bar that fills from 0% to 100%, optionally with animated indeterminate states when the total duration is unknown, and clear labels so users understand what is being tracked.

ReUI lists progress components inside file upload flows, dashboards, and checkout steps so you evaluate how progress pairs with Shadcn Spinner, Shadcn Alert for status updates, and Shadcn Button actions. The sections below explain what the component is, why it matters, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Progress?
paragraphs: A progress bar is a visual indicator showing how much of a task is complete. Shadcn Progress wraps Radix UI primitives to provide accessible, themeable progress indicators that work with Tailwind styling and render clear roles for assistive technologies.

Components here show determinate progress (0–100% with a known duration), indeterminate progress (animated, duration unknown), color-coded status (success, warning, danger), and stacked progress for multi-step workflows. They help you choose a component, decide when to show a spinner vs. a progress bar, and integrate progress with form submission feedback.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Progress?
paragraphs: Progress bars reduce anxiety during long operations by showing users that something is happening. File uploads, API calls, and batch processes all benefit from visible feedback so users don't think the app has frozen.

Searchers look for 'react progress bar', 'file upload progress', and 'loading indicator'. This page answers with realistic components showing how progress bars pair with Shadcn Field labels, Shadcn Button actions (pause, cancel), and status messages so users stay informed and confident.

featureItems.title: Determinate and Indeterminate.
featureItems.description: Set a value prop (0–100) to show known progress, or omit it for animated indeterminate state when duration is unknown.

featureItems.title: Accessible Roles.
featureItems.description: Uses progressbar role and aria-valuenow/aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax so screen readers announce percentage and completeness.

featureItems.title: Color Variants.
featureItems.description: Success (green), warning (yellow), and destructive (red) variants communicate status beyond the percentage, important for at-a-glance understanding.

featureItems.title: Label Support.
featureItems.description: Pair with Shadcn Field label or helper text to describe what is being tracked, e.g., 'Uploading: 3 of 12 files (25%)'.

featureItems.title: Smooth Animations.
featureItems.description: Transitions between percentage changes are smooth and perceptible, respecting prefers-reduced-motion for users with motion sensitivity.

faqs.question: Should I show a spinner or progress bar for an async operation?
faqs.answer: Use a progress bar when you know or can estimate the task duration and want to show how much is complete (determinate). Use a spinner (or indeterminate progress) when duration is unknown. Pair progress bars with estimated time remaining text for best UX.

faqs.question: How do I update progress in real time as an API call completes?
faqs.answer: Maintain a React state (e.g., setProgress) and update it as events arrive: start at 0%, increment on chunk upload, reach 100% when the API call completes. Smooth transitions are automatic in CSS; avoid jumping from 0% to 100% in one step.

faqs.question: Can I show progress for multiple items or operations at once?
faqs.answer: Yes. Stack multiple progress bars inside a list or grid, each tracking one upload. Show individual progress and an overall summary bar so users see both granular and aggregate completion.

faqs.question: How do I make a progress bar accessible?
faqs.answer: Shadcn Progress includes proper roles (progressbar) and ARIA attributes (aria-valuenow, aria-valuemax). Always pair with a visible label describing what is being tracked, and consider adding aria-label if the purpose is unclear from context.

faqs.question: Should I display the percentage number on the progress bar?
faqs.answer: Show a percentage if it's meaningful and space permits. For tight layouts, place the text next to the bar or in a Shadcn Field helper. Always show a human-readable description (e.g., '3 of 10 files uploaded') alongside the percentage.

faqs.question: Can the user cancel or pause a task shown in a progress bar?
faqs.answer: Yes. Place a Shadcn Button next to the progress bar for cancel or pause actions. When cancelled, show a dismissal option or move to an error state with a Shadcn Alert explaining what happened.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair progress with Shadcn Spinner when you want to show both indeterminate motion (early stages) and a deterministic percentage (later stages). Combine with Shadcn Button for cancel or pause actions that users can trigger if the operation takes too long.
Show progress inside a Shadcn Card or modal for prominent uploads. Add a Shadcn Field label above the bar ('Uploading file.pdf...') and helper text below ('3 of 5 files done, 45 seconds remaining').

When the operation completes, replace the progress bar with a Shadcn Alert showing success or failure. Use Shadcn Badge to mark completed tasks in a list, and Shadcn Skeleton to preview final content while previous uploads are finishing.

radio-group.description: {{count}} production-ready shadcn radio components for single-select forms, preference settings, and option choices. Build accessible radio buttons.
radio-group.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn radio-group components for single-select forms, preference settings, and option lists. Radio Group is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and fully aligned with Shadcn Create so radius, colors, and typography match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Radio Group: Single-Select Form Fields
summary: Shadcn Radio Group is a set of radio buttons for single-choice form inputs: shipping method, account tier, or notification frequency. Built on Radix UI with full keyboard navigation and focus management, it integrates seamlessly with Base UI-style composition while maintaining full Shadcn Create style compatibility.
It enforces one-at-a-time selection and provides clear visual feedback for checked, hovered, and focused states, with automatic label association so clicking text toggles the radio.

ReUI lists radio-group components inside forms, settings panels, and checkout flows so you evaluate layout (vertical vs. inline), icon pairing, disabled states, and error messaging alongside [Shadcn Field](/components/field). The sections below explain what the component is, why it matters, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Radio Group?
paragraphs: A radio group is a set of mutually exclusive options where selecting one automatically deselects the others. Shadcn Radio Group wraps Radix UI semantics to ensure one option is always selected and keyboard users can navigate with arrow keys.

Components here show vertical stacks (common in forms), horizontal layouts (tight spaces), icon-paired options, and disabled radio items. They help you decide between radio groups and checkboxes (multi-select), when to disable options, and how to integrate error messages from form validation.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Radio Group?
paragraphs: Teams use radio groups when users must choose exactly one option from a set. Payment methods, subscription tiers, delivery speeds, and notification preferences are common cases where one-at-a-time selection is required.

Searchers look for 'radio button react', 'form radio component', and 'single-select form field'. This page answers with real components showing when radio groups are better than dropdowns (fewer options, all visible), and how to pair them with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) labels and validation feedback.

featureItems.title: Mutually Exclusive Selection.
featureItems.description: Only one radio can be checked at a time; selecting a new option automatically unchecks the previous one.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Navigation.
featureItems.description: Arrow keys move focus between radios, Space or Enter toggles selection, Tab enters/exits the group. Full WCAG 2.1 AA keyboard support out of the box.

featureItems.title: Label Association.
featureItems.description: Clicking a label toggles the radio; implicit association via htmlFor, or wrap the radio and label together in a clickable container for larger touch targets.

featureItems.title: Disabled and Read-Only States.
featureItems.description: Disable individual radios or the entire group; show clear visual feedback so users understand why an option cannot be selected.

featureItems.title: Icon and Badge Support.
featureItems.description: Pair radios with icons or [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) labels (e.g., 'Free', 'Pro') to add context; Lucide icons work well for visual clarity.

faqs.question: Should I use a radio group or a dropdown for single-select options?
faqs.answer: Use radio groups when there are fewer than 5–7 options and you want all choices visible at once. Use a [Shadcn Select](/components/select) or [Shadcn Combobox](/components/combobox) when there are many options and space is limited, or when users might not need to see all choices.

faqs.question: How do I make a radio group field required in a form?
faqs.answer: Validate the radio group state on form submission: check that a value is selected before allowing submit. Use aria-required on the group and show a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) error message below the radios if validation fails.

faqs.question: Can I add helper text or descriptions to radio options?
faqs.answer: Yes. Wrap each radio and label in a container with additional description text below. Pair with aria-describedby to associate the description with the radio for screen readers, improving clarity especially for pricing or feature comparisons.

faqs.question: Is a radio group keyboard accessible out of the box?
faqs.answer: Yes. Radix UI Radio Group includes arrow key navigation, Space/Enter selection, and Tab to enter/exit the group. Always test with a screen reader to confirm labels and descriptions are announced clearly.

faqs.question: How do I validate a radio group in React Hook Form?
faqs.answer: Register the field as a controlled input with defaultValue set to the initial selection. Use Zod or Yup to validate that a value is selected, and display errors via [Shadcn Field](/components/field) below the group.

faqs.question: Can I disable specific radio options based on a condition?
faqs.answer: Yes. Apply the disabled prop to individual RadioGroupItem components based on your logic (e.g., show 'Pro' as disabled if the user's plan doesn't support upgrades). Always explain why an option is unavailable with tooltip or helper text.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap radio-group in a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) to add a label, description, and validation errors. Use [Shadcn Fieldset](/components/fieldset) and [Shadcn Legend](/components/legend) to group radios with an overall title, following WAI-ARIA fieldset semantics.

Pair radios with [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) or [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) to add clarifying context (e.g., 'Free', 'Pro', 'Limited time'). Show [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for form submission after the radio group.

Combine radio-group with [Shadcn Card](/components/card) when presenting pricing tiers or plan options so each radio has clear visual boundaries. Use [Shadcn Alert](/docs/components/base/alert) above the group to highlight a recommended or limited option, guiding user choice.

rating.description: {{count}} rating components with star icons, half-stars, and read-only modes. Interactive, accessible, and `react-hook-form` compatible.
rating.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn rating components for star ratings, decimal display, editable and read-only modes, emoji reaction ratings, interactive half-star, review summaries with progress bars, and text input review forms : a custom ReUI component built on accessible input primitives.

content.title: Shadcn Rating: Stars, Emojis, and Review Summaries
summary: ReUI Rating is a custom shadcn component for collecting and displaying user ratings. It renders star icons as radio inputs, making the selection accessible to keyboard users and screen readers via standard radio group semantics without custom ARIA roles.

Nine components cover basic star rating, decimal display (3.7 stars), show-value labels, editable mode, size variants, full review summary with rating breakdown bars, interactive half-star rating, emoji reaction rating, and a combined rating with review text input.
The sections below explain the accessible radio input foundation, real use cases in e-commerce and SaaS, features, and composition with Progress, Textarea, and Button for complete review flows.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Rating?
paragraphs: ReUI Rating is a custom shadcn component : not in the base shadcn/ui library. It renders a set of star icons backed by hidden radio inputs, giving native keyboard navigation (arrow keys within the group), correct labeling for screen readers, and standard form submission behavior without any custom ARIA management.

Read-only mode renders filled and unfilled star icons from a numeric value, supporting decimal display (a 3.7 score renders as 3 full stars and a 70% partial star). Editable mode adds hover preview : stars fill as the user moves the cursor : and fires onChange on click or keyboard selection.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Rating?
paragraphs: Star ratings appear in product reviews, app store feedback, course quality scores, restaurant ratings, and service satisfaction surveys. An accessible implementation requires radio group semantics, hover state management, decimal rendering, and half-star support : all common requirements that custom span-based implementations get wrong. ReUI Rating handles all of these correctly.

The review summary component combines a large display rating with a breakdown of 5-star through 1-star counts as Progress bars : the standard component for product pages and app store listings. Pair with [Shadcn Textarea](/components/textarea) for written review input and [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for form submission.

featureItems.title: Radio Input Accessibility.
featureItems.description: Stars render as radio inputs in a fieldset. Screen readers announce the rating value and scale. Arrow keys navigate between star values. No custom ARIA roles are needed : native radio semantics handle all interaction.

featureItems.title: Decimal Display.
featureItems.description: Read-only mode renders partial stars for decimal values using CSS clip-path. A 3.7 score shows 3 full stars and a 70% partial fourth star : matching the display convention of Amazon, Google, and App Store listings.

featureItems.title: Editable Mode with Hover Preview.
featureItems.description: In editable mode, stars fill progressively as the user hovers. The hover value displays as a number label when show-value is enabled. Click or keyboard selection commits the rating.

featureItems.title: Half-Star Rating.
featureItems.description: Interactive half-star mode divides each star icon into two click targets : the left half for .5 and the right half for the full integer value. Supports both display and editable modes.

featureItems.title: Emoji Reaction Rating.
featureItems.description: Emoji variant replaces star icons with reaction emojis (😞 😐 😊 😁 🤩) for informal feedback, satisfaction surveys, and NPS-style quick polls.

featureItems.title: Review Summary with Progress Bars.
featureItems.description: Review summary component combines an aggregate score, total review count, and per-star breakdown using shadcn Progress bars : the standard layout for product detail pages and app listings.

featureItems.title: Size Variants.
featureItems.description: Small, default, and large size variants scale the star icon and touch target. Small fits inside table cells or card footers; large suits prominent hero review sections.

featureItems.title: Rating with Review Text Input.
featureItems.description: Combined component pairs the rating input with a shadcn Textarea for written review content and a submit Button : a complete review form in a single composable component.

sections.title: ReUI Rating: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Rating is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project : following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Rating is a custom accessible rating component not available in base shadcn/ui. Stars render as radio inputs in a fieldset for native keyboard navigation and screen reader support. Supports decimal display with CSS clip-path, editable hover preview, half-star interactive mode, emoji reaction variant, and a review summary with per-star Progress breakdown bars.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling : only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version : API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version : API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: What is the difference between ReUI Rating and a standard Radix UI rating component?
faqs.answer: ReUI Rating provides out-of-the-box support for half-star selections, smooth animations, and integrated form validation with `react-hook-form`. It also includes customizable icons and better accessibility defaults.

faqs.question: Can I use the rating component in read-only mode?
faqs.answer: Yes. Set the disabled prop or readonly state to show a read-only rating display without interaction. Useful for displaying product reviews or user feedback.

faqs.question: How do I implement half-star ratings?
faqs.answer: Pass step='0.5' to allow selection in 0.5 increments. Combine with custom icon rendering to show filled, half-filled, and empty star states.

faqs.question: How is the ReUI Rating component different from Radix vs Base UI versions?
faqs.answer: The Radix version uses Radix primitives; the Base UI version is built on Base UI components. Both feature the same API and accessibility guarantees but differ in their underlying dependencies.

faqs.question: How do I validate a rating selection with react-hook-form?
faqs.answer: Register the rating field and use Zod or Yup to ensure a value is selected. Show validation errors below the rating component using the Field component.

faqs.question: What ARIA attributes are included for screen reader support?
faqs.answer: The component includes role='radio' on each star, aria-label describing the rating value, aria-describedby for additional instructions, and aria-required for mandatory fields.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Pair Rating with [Shadcn Textarea](/components/textarea) and [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for a complete review submission form. Use [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for accessible labeling and validation error display.

Use [Shadcn Progress](/components/progress) for the per-star count breakdown bars in a review summary panel, and [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for verified purchase or review category labels.

Embed rating stars inside [Shadcn Card](/components/card) for product listing grids, or in [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid) cells for rating columns in admin review management tables.

inHouseSection.title: ReUI Rating: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Rating is a custom implementation built on Radix UI primitives, offering both interactive and read-only modes with smooth animations and full keyboard accessibility. It supports half-star selections, custom icon substitution, and seamless integration with form validation libraries.

ReUI maintains 17 in-house components that enhance the base shadcn/ui library with production-grade features for Next.js applications. The Rating component includes built-in screen reader support for star announcements and ARIA attributes.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version: API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version: API, Props and Usage

resizable.description: {{count}} resizable panel components with drag handles, min/max constraints, and localStorage persistence. Responsive splits with collapsible sections.
resizable.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn resizable components for multi-panel layouts, dashboard dashboards, and flexible content grids. Resizable is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI composition models and fully aligned with Shadcn Create so radius, colors, and typography match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Resizable: Flexible Multi-Panel Layouts
summary: Shadcn Resizable is a layout component for building adjustable multi-panel interfaces: code editors, IDE-style dashboards, and canvas tools where users control panel sizes. Built on react-resizable-panels by bvaughn, it supports mouse, touch, and keyboard dragging while integrating seamlessly with Radix UI components and full Shadcn Create style compatibility.
It provides horizontal and vertical draggable dividers between panels, with snapping, collapsible behavior, and persistent size state so users' layout preferences are remembered.

ReUI lists resizable components inside dashboards, editors, and split-view layouts so you evaluate divider affordance, collapse triggers, and how panels pair with [Shadcn Card](/components/card) content. The sections below explain what the component is, why teams use it, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Resizable?
paragraphs: A resizable panel group is a container that holds one or more draggable dividers and panels, allowing users to adjust panel widths or heights. Shadcn Resizable wraps react-resizable-panels to provide keyboard-accessible dragging, touch support, and collapsible panel logic.

Components here show horizontal splits (sidebar + content), vertical splits (editor + preview), and nested splits for complex UIs. They help you decide panel defaults, min/max sizes, and whether to persist layout state to localStorage so users' preferences are remembered on return visits.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Resizable?
paragraphs: Teams use resizable panels when users benefit from controlling layout. Code editors, data dashboards, and design tools all let power users customize how much space each view occupies, improving focus and productivity.

Searchers look for 'react split pane', 'draggable panel layout', and 'resizable dashboard'. This page answers with realistic components showing how to build editor and dashboard layouts where panels adjust dynamically, and how to pair resizable with [Shadcn Card](/components/card) content containers.

featureItems.title: Horizontal and Vertical Splits.
featureItems.description: Set orientation to 'horizontal' (side-by-side) or 'vertical' (stacked); nest groups for complex multi-pane layouts.

featureItems.title: Draggable Dividers.
featureItems.description: Mouse, touch, and keyboard users can drag dividers to resize panels. Cursor changes to col-resize or row-resize for affordance.

featureItems.title: Min and Max Constraints.
featureItems.description: Set minSize and maxSize on each panel to prevent collapse or excessive expansion; useful for sidebars and fixed-width components.

featureItems.title: Collapsible Panels.
featureItems.description: Add collapse triggers or double-click dividers to collapse/expand panels; great for hiding sidebar navigation on mobile.

featureItems.title: State Persistence.
featureItems.description: Pair with localStorage to save user's layout preferences so resizing is remembered across page reloads.

faqs.question: How do I build an editor-style two-pane layout with resizable?
faqs.answer: Use ResizablePanelGroup with orientation='horizontal'. Add two ResizablePanels with content inside, and a ResizableHandle divider between them. Set minSize on each panel to prevent complete collapse.

faqs.question: Can I save the user's panel sizes and restore them on page reload?
faqs.answer: Yes. Listen to ResizablePanelGroup onLayout changes, store sizes in localStorage, and use defaultSizes to restore on mount. This creates a seamless experience where users' customizations persist.

faqs.question: How do I collapse or expand a panel programmatically?
faqs.answer: Use the collapse() and expand() methods available on ResizablePanel refs. Pair with a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to allow users to toggle sidebar visibility with keyboard shortcuts or menu items.

faqs.question: Can resizable panels work on mobile or touch devices?
faqs.answer: Yes. react-resizable-panels supports touch dragging out of the box. On narrow screens, consider collapsing one panel by default so the primary content is full-width, with a menu button to reveal the sidebar.

faqs.question: What is the divider (ResizableHandle) and how do I customize it?
faqs.answer: The handle is the draggable zone between panels. Customize its width/height with className (e.g., 'w-1'), color, and hover effects. Add a visual indicator (vertical or horizontal line) so users understand they can drag.

faqs.question: How do I add keyboard shortcuts for resizing or collapsing?
faqs.answer: Capture keyboard events (e.g., Cmd+B to toggle sidebar) and call collapse()/expand() on panel refs. Always show keyboard shortcuts in a help menu so power users discover them.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Place [Shadcn Card](/components/card) or custom content inside each ResizablePanel to create modular, resizable sections. Use [Shadcn Button](/components/button) on panel headers for collapsing, full-screen, or other panel-specific actions.

Pair resizable with [Shadcn Navigation Menu](/components/navigation-menu) or [Shadcn Sidebar](/components/sidebar) in the left panel, and content views in the center/right panels. Add [Shadcn Tabs](/components/tabs) inside panels for multi-view switching within a single panel.

Use [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) on the resize handle to hint 'Drag to resize' on hover. Combine with [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) or [Shadcn Sheet](/components/sheet) for modal actions that don't permanently change the layout.

scroll-area.description: {{count}} scroll-area components for custom scrollbars, overflow content, and virtualized lists. Styled with Tailwind CSS and Shadcn Create design tokens.
scroll-area.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn scroll-area components for custom scroll styling, long lists, and bounded content. Scroll Area is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI composition and fully aligned with Shadcn Create so radius, colors, and typography match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Scroll Area: Custom Scrollbars and Bounded Content
summary: Shadcn Scroll Area is a bounded scrollable container that wraps Radix UI Scroll Area for styling native browser scrolling with custom scrollbars and tracks. It maintains full accessibility while allowing designers to hide native scrollbars and substitute themed alternatives that match your design system.
It renders a container with overflow-hidden and uses JavaScript-driven scrolling to display custom thumb and track elements styled with Tailwind, keeping the interface cohesive.

ReUI lists scroll-area components inside data tables, long lists, modals, and dashboards so you evaluate scrollbar size, thumb styling, and how scroll-area pairs with [Shadcn Card](/components/card) and [Shadcn Table](/components/table). The sections below explain what the component is, why it matters, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Scroll Area?
paragraphs: A scroll area is a container that overflows and requires scrolling, but displays a custom scrollbar instead of the browser default. Shadcn Scroll Area wraps Radix UI's Scroll Area primitive to provide themed, accessible custom scrollbars that match your design tokens.

Components here show vertical scrollbars in lists and tables, horizontal scrollbars for wide data, and combined vertical + horizontal for spreadsheets. They help you decide scrollbar size, thumb visibility, and context where a custom scrollbar adds to the visual coherence of your product.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Scroll Area?
paragraphs: Teams use scroll area when the default browser scrollbar doesn't match the design system or when fine-tuned scrolling behavior improves the product experience. Long lists, data tables, and code blocks all benefit from visible, styled scrollbars that hint at content flow.

Searchers look for 'custom scrollbar react', 'hide scrollbar tailwind', and 'scroll area component'. This page answers with components showing how scroll-area works with [Shadcn Table](/components/table) for scrollable data, and how to style scrollbars to match your Shadcn Create tokens.

featureItems.title: Custom Scrollbar Styling.
featureItems.description: Replaces native scrollbar with themed thumb and track styled via Tailwind; color and size are fully customizable to match design tokens.

featureItems.title: Wraps Radix UI Scroll Area.
featureItems.description: Built on Radix Scroll Area for accessibility; maintains keyboard focus management and mouse wheel / trackpad scroll behavior.

featureItems.title: Vertical and Horizontal.
featureItems.description: Supports vertical (default) and horizontal scrolling, or both. Useful for wide data tables and responsive content.

featureItems.title: Bounded Container.
featureItems.description: Set a fixed height or max-height on the scroll area so content overflows in a predictable, contained zone, preventing layout shift.

featureItems.title: Smooth Scroll and Lazy Loading.
featureItems.description: Pair with virtualization libraries (react-window, react-virtual) for smooth scrolling of thousands of items without DOM bloat.

faqs.question: How do I use custom scrollbars in my React app with Shadcn Scroll Area?
faqs.answer: Import ScrollArea and wrap your overflow content. The custom scrollbar is styled automatically via Tailwind classes in the component source. Override the classes in your component to customize colors and size.

faqs.question: Can I use scroll-area with a large data table?
faqs.answer: Yes. Place a [Shadcn Table](/components/table) inside a ScrollArea to bound height and add custom scrollbars. For thousands of rows, combine with virtualization (react-window or TanStack React Table) to avoid rendering all rows at once.

faqs.question: How do I hide the scrollbar but keep scrolling enabled?
faqs.answer: Add scrollbar-hide class or set overflow-hidden with custom scroll logic. However, hidden scrollbars harm UX; only hide them if you provide an alternative scroll indicator or if scrolling is optional (most content is visible).

faqs.question: Is scroll-area accessible for keyboard and screen-reader users?
faqs.answer: Yes. Radix UI Scroll Area maintains keyboard focus management. Users can tab to content and scroll with arrow keys or mouse wheel. Test with a screen reader to confirm.

faqs.question: Can I customize scrollbar thickness and color?
faqs.answer: Yes. Edit the Tailwind classes in your scroll-area component file: adjust w-2 or h-2 for thumb width, and bg-* utilities for color. Keep the scrollbar thin enough to not distract from content but visible enough to indicate scrollable area.

faqs.question: How do I make scroll-area responsive on mobile?
faqs.answer: Use conditional logic to show the native scrollbar on mobile (overflow-y-auto) and custom scroll-area on desktop. Or let native scroll work on touch devices:custom scrollbars are less critical on mobile where finger scrolling is natural.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Place a [Shadcn Table](/components/table) or long list inside ScrollArea to bound the height and add custom scrollbars. Pair with [Shadcn Pagination](/components/pagination) to split large datasets and avoid excessive scrolling.

Use ScrollArea inside a [Shadcn Card](/components/card) to contain scrollable content with clear boundaries. Combine with [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) inside the scroll area to show loading states for items as they're fetched.

For chat or log interfaces, use ScrollArea with auto-scroll-to-bottom logic so new messages appear at the bottom without manual scrolling. Add [Shadcn Input](/components/input) below the scroll area for message entry.

scrollspy.description: {{count}} scrollspy components for sticky navigation, table of contents, and offset detection. Smooth scroll and dynamic content support.
scrollspy.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn scrollspy components for highlighting the active section as users scroll. Covers basic scrollspy with sticky navigation and scrollspy with scroll area : a custom ReUI component using IntersectionObserver for accurate section tracking.

content.title: Shadcn Scrollspy: Active Section Navigation Highlight
summary: ReUI Scrollspy is a custom shadcn component that highlights the active navigation link as users scroll through page sections. It uses the Intersection Observer API to detect which section is in the viewport and updates the active link state without scroll event listeners or manual offset calculations.

Two components cover basic scrollspy with sticky navigation links and scrollspy inside a shadcn Scroll Area for constrained-height containers. Both components are suitable for documentation sidebars, long-form content pages, and settings panels with section anchors.
The sections below explain the Intersection Observer approach, use cases, features, and composition guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Scrollspy?
paragraphs: ReUI Scrollspy is a custom component that tracks scroll position relative to named sections on a page and highlights the corresponding navigation link. It uses the browser's native IntersectionObserver API : a more accurate and performance-efficient approach than calculating scroll position with getBoundingClientRect or scroll event listeners.

The sticky navigation component renders a left or right sidebar of section links that stick in the viewport as the user scrolls. The scroll area component embeds the scrollable content inside a shadcn ScrollArea, with navigation links in the same panel : useful for settings pages, doc viewers, and constrained panels.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Scrollspy?
paragraphs: Long-form documentation pages, settings panels with many sections, privacy policies, pricing pages, and onboarding guides all benefit from in-page navigation with visual feedback about which section the user is viewing. Scrollspy removes the cognitive overhead of mapping scroll position to menu items manually.

IntersectionObserver fires when a section enters or exits the viewport : no scroll events that run on every pixel of movement. This makes scrollspy components performant even on pages with many observed sections. Pair with [Shadcn Navigation Menu](/components/navigation-menu) or a custom link list for the navigation side.

featureItems.title: IntersectionObserver Tracking.
featureItems.description: Uses the browser's native IntersectionObserver to detect which section is in the viewport. No scroll event listeners, no getBoundingClientRect polling, no performance overhead on scroll.

featureItems.title: Active Link Highlighting.
featureItems.description: The navigation link corresponding to the current visible section receives an active style : bold text, colored border, or background : that updates smoothly as sections enter and exit the viewport.

featureItems.title: Sticky Navigation Sidebar.
featureItems.description: Navigation links use CSS position: sticky to remain visible as the user scrolls the main content. The active highlight updates in real time so users always know their position in the document.

featureItems.title: Scroll Area Variant.
featureItems.description: Scrollspy inside a shadcn ScrollArea confines the scrollable content to a fixed-height panel. Useful for settings pages, doc viewers, and constrained panels in dashboards.

featureItems.title: Smooth Scroll on Link Click.
featureItems.description: Clicking a navigation link smoothly scrolls the page or scroll container to the target section using scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' }) : matching the UX of well-known documentation sites.

featureItems.title: Configurable Threshold and Root Margin.
featureItems.description: IntersectionObserver threshold and rootMargin are configurable. Adjust rootMargin to trigger the active state earlier or later relative to the viewport edge, matching your layout's sticky header height.

sections.title: ReUI Scrollspy: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Scrollspy is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project : following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Scrollspy is a custom in-page navigation component not available in base shadcn/ui. It uses the browser's native IntersectionObserver API to track which content section is in the viewport and highlights the corresponding navigation link. No scroll event listeners : only intersection callbacks fire, keeping performance overhead minimal on long pages.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling : only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version : API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version : API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: What is the difference between ReUI Scrollspy and a standard scrollspy library?
faqs.answer: ReUI Scrollspy integrates seamlessly with Shadcn Create design tokens and provides optimized performance for many sections. It includes built-in support for offset configuration and smooth scroll behavior.

faqs.question: How do I configure offset for fixed headers?
faqs.answer: Pass the offset prop to account for fixed headers. If your header is 80px tall, set offset={80} so scrollspy highlights trigger at the correct scroll position.

faqs.question: How do I use scrollspy with dynamically loaded content?
faqs.answer: Scrollspy updates section detection when content changes. Wrap dynamic sections in data-attributes and let scrollspy observe the DOM. Re-initialize if needed after major content updates.

faqs.question: Can I have multiple scrollspy instances on one page?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each instance watches a specific container and nav element. Assign unique IDs to containers and nav menus to avoid conflicts between multiple scrollspy components.

faqs.question: How does scrollspy perform with 50+ sections?
faqs.answer: Scrollspy uses Intersection Observer for efficient section detection. Performance is excellent even with many sections; consider using pagination or lazy loading for very large documents.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Pair Scrollspy with [Shadcn Scroll Area](/components/scroll-area) for constrained-height scrollable panels where the navigation and content both sit inside a bounded container.

Use [Shadcn Navigation Menu](/components/navigation-menu) or a simple link list for the navigation side. Apply active styles with Tailwind variants : border-l-2 border-primary for a left-rule active indicator that matches documentation site conventions.
Combine Scrollspy with [Shadcn Separator](/components/separator) between sections and [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for 'Back to top' actions at the bottom of long content pages.

inHouseSection.title: ReUI Scrollspy: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Scrollspy is a custom component that automatically highlights navigation items as the user scrolls through page sections. It handles fixed header offsets, dynamic content, and multiple scrollspy instances with minimal configuration.
ReUI maintains 17 in-house components for production Next.js apps. The Scrollspy component provides smooth scroll anchoring, performance optimization for many sections, and accessibility features.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version: API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version: API, Props and Usage

select.description: {{count}} select components with async options, virtualization, and multi-select support. Accessible dropdowns built on Radix UI with form validation.
select.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn select components for customizable dropdowns, multi-select, and searchable option lists. Select is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI composition and fully aligned with Shadcn Create so radius, colors, and typography match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Select: Customizable Dropdowns and Option Lists
summary: Shadcn Select is a styleable dropdown component built on Radix UI for single and multi-option selection. Unlike native <select>, it allows custom styling via Tailwind, animations, and rich option content (icons, badges) while maintaining full keyboard and accessibility support.
It displays a trigger button that opens a dropdown menu, with typeahead search, filtering, and disabled state support. Fully compatible with Shadcn Create style tokens for consistent theming.

ReUI lists select components inside forms, filters, and settings panels so you evaluate when to use Select vs. Radio Group (fewer options), Combobox (searchable large lists), and Native Select (mobile-friendly). The sections below explain what the component is, why it matters, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Select?
paragraphs: A select component is a dropdown menu for choosing one option from a list. Shadcn Select wraps Radix UI Select to provide custom styling, animations, and rich content support (icons, labels, groups) while maintaining WCAG 2.1 AA keyboard and screen-reader accessibility.

Components here show basic single-select, optionally searchable with icons, grouped options (e.g., by category), disabled options, and multi-select variations. They help you decide between Select, native <select>, and Combobox based on option count and whether searching is needed.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Select?
paragraphs: Teams use Select when they need a dropdown that looks and feels consistent with their design system. Radix UI Select provides keyboard accessibility and mobile support out of the box, while custom styling makes it a strong alternative to native <select>.

Searchers look for 'react dropdown', 'custom select component', and 'select vs combobox'. This page answers with components showing the trade-offs: native <select> is fastest and mobile-optimized; Shadcn Select offers design flexibility and animations; Combobox adds search for large lists.

featureItems.title: Customizable Styling.
featureItems.description: Fully styled with Tailwind CSS; set colors, sizes, and animations to match your design tokens. No CSS-in-JS or theme overrides needed.

featureItems.title: Radix UI Foundation.
featureItems.description: Built on Radix UI Select for proven keyboard behavior, focus management, and screen-reader support that matches WAI-ARIA guidance.

featureItems.title: Grouped Options.
featureItems.description: Organize options into logical groups (e.g., 'Colors', 'Sizes') with SelectGroup and SelectLabel for better scannability in long lists.

featureItems.title: Rich Option Content.
featureItems.description: Add icons (Lucide), badges, or custom markup to options for visual clarity; pair with [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for status or counts.

featureItems.title: Disabled and Read-Only.
featureItems.description: Disable individual options or the entire select; show visual feedback so users understand why an option cannot be chosen.

faqs.question: Should I use Select or native <select>?
faqs.answer: Use Shadcn Select when design consistency and custom styling matter. Use Native Select (or [Shadcn Native Select](/components/native-select)) when you prioritize performance, minimal JS, and perfect mobile UX (iOS picker). Combobox is best for large searchable lists.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Select and Combobox?
faqs.answer: Select displays a dropdown menu of options when clicked; users pick one. Combobox combines a text input with a dropdown; users can type to filter options or click to see all. Use Select for short lists (5–20 items); Combobox for large lists where searching saves time.

faqs.question: How do I add search or filtering to a Select?
faqs.answer: For simple filtering, use [Shadcn Combobox](/components/combobox) instead, which includes search. For Select, if you need to filter options, a separate search input field above the select is clearer than hiding search inside the dropdown.

faqs.question: Can I use Select with React Hook Form?
faqs.answer: Yes. Register the select field and manage value with form state. Use Zod or Yup to validate that a selection is made. Show validation errors via [Shadcn Field](/components/field) below the select.

faqs.question: How do I handle multi-select with Shadcn Select?
faqs.answer: Shadcn Select supports single selection. For multi-select, use a custom component with checkboxes or consider [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) groups. Alternatively, use a third-party multi-select library and style it to match Shadcn tokens.

faqs.question: Is Select keyboard and screen-reader accessible?
faqs.answer: Yes. Radix UI Select includes arrow key navigation, Space/Enter selection, and Tab to enter/exit. Screen readers announce the current selection and option count. Always pair with a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) label.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Wrap select with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) to add a label, description, and validation error messages. Use [Shadcn Fieldset](/components/fieldset) and [Shadcn Legend](/components/legend) to group multiple select fields under a common title.

For large searchable lists, use [Shadcn Combobox](/components/combobox) instead. For simple single-choice forms with few options, use [Shadcn Radio Group](/components/radio-group) so all options are visible without opening a dropdown.

Pair select with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for form submission. Add [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) to provide help text or examples if users are unsure which option to pick. For mobile optimization, offer [Shadcn Native Select](/components/native-select) as an alternative that uses OS-level pickers.

separator.description: {{count}} separator components for dividing content sections. Semantic HTML dividers, vertical layouts, decorative lines, and full WCAG accessibility.
separator.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn separator components for content dividers, list grouping, and visual hierarchy. Separator is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI composition and fully aligned with Shadcn Create so radius, colors, and typography match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Separator: Visual Dividers and Content Grouping
summary: Shadcn Separator is a thin, semantic divider for grouping content and reinforcing visual hierarchy. Built on Radix UI Separator with accessible roles, it supports horizontal and vertical orientations and works seamlessly with Shadcn Create styling.
It is a lightweight, purely presentational component that helps users scan layouts by clearly delineating sections without requiring extensive CSS or custom styling.

ReUI lists separator components inside card layouts, list sections, and multi-column grids so you evaluate spacing, color contrast, and context where a subtle divider improves readability without visual clutter. The sections below explain what the component is, why it matters, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Separator?
paragraphs: A separator (or divider) is a thin line or visual break between content sections. Shadcn Separator renders a semantic <hr> or <div> element with Radix UI roles so assistive technologies recognize it as a structural divider, not decorative.

Components here show simple horizontal dividers between card sections, vertical separators in button groups, and nested separators inside complex layouts. They help you decide when a separator improves readability versus adding clutter, and how to pair separators with whitespace and typography.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Separator?
paragraphs: Teams use separators to group related content and improve visual hierarchy without opening a new card or section. List items, form sections, and menu items all benefit from clear dividers that guide the eye without distraction.

Searchers look for 'react divider', 'horizontal line component', and 'content separator'. This page answers with components showing when and where separators improve layouts, and how to style them to match your Shadcn Create tokens for cohesive design.

featureItems.title: Horizontal and Vertical.
featureItems.description: Set orientation prop to 'horizontal' (default) for vertical dividers or 'vertical' for inline separators between elements.

featureItems.title: Semantic HTML.
featureItems.description: Uses <hr> or proper <div> roles so screen readers recognize it as a structural divider, not decorative noise.

featureItems.title: Customizable Color and Width.
featureItems.description: Style with Tailwind utilities (bg-border, h-px) to match your theme. Adjust opacity for subtle vs. prominent dividers.

featureItems.title: Margin Control.
featureItems.description: Add Tailwind margin utilities (my-4, my-8) to control spacing around the divider so it doesn't feel cramped or isolated.

featureItems.title: Lightweight.
featureItems.description: No JavaScript, no state, no event handlers:purely presentational. Pair with other components for interactive layouts.

faqs.question: When should I use a separator vs. whitespace to group content?
faqs.answer: Use a separator when visually distinct sections need a clear boundary (e.g., different card sections, menu groups). Use whitespace alone when sections are already visually distinct or when the divider would feel like clutter. Test with users to ensure the divider adds clarity.

faqs.question: How do I make a vertical separator between inline elements?
faqs.answer: Use orientation='vertical' and set a fixed height (e.g., h-4) and width (e.g., w-px). Place it between buttons or text elements to create a light divider; pair with small margins (mx-2) so it doesn't feel crowded.

faqs.question: Can I use a separator inside a list?
faqs.answer: Yes. Wrap separator in an <li> element so it's a valid list item. This keeps HTML structure clean and semantically correct, important for screen reader users scanning lists.

faqs.question: What color should my separator be?
faqs.answer: Use a subtle contrast: bg-border or bg-muted from your Shadcn theme. Avoid black or strong colors unless you want the divider to be prominent. Test contrast ratios to ensure readability without overwhelming content.

faqs.question: How much margin should I add around a separator?
faqs.answer: Add consistent spacing (my-4, my-6, or my-8) based on your design system. The separator should feel like it belongs between sections, not crammed or floating. Match spacing to other components like [Shadcn Card](/components/card) for visual consistency.

faqs.question: Is a separator component necessary, or can I use CSS borders?
faqs.answer: A separator is useful for consistency across your product and pairs naturally with Shadcn Create theming. For simple cases, a CSS border (border-top) works fine. Use the component when you want semantic HTML, theme alignment, and reduced CSS boilerplate.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Use separators inside [Shadcn Card](/components/card) to divide sections (header, body, footer). Add separators between [Shadcn Field](/components/field) groups in long forms to signal logical groupings without full section breaks.

Pair separators with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) groups (e.g., 'Save | Cancel | Delete') using vertical orientation. Use horizontal separators in [Shadcn Accordion](/components/accordion) panels or [Shadcn Table](/components/table) rows for visual clarity.

Add separators to [Shadcn Navigation Menu](/components/menu) or [Shadcn Dropdown Menu](/components/dropdown-menu) to group related actions. For layouts with [Shadcn Sidebar](/components/sidebar), use horizontal separators to divide navigation sections, improving scannability.

sheet.description: {{count}} sheet components for side drawers, bottom sheets, and modals. Customizable positioning and fully accessible slide-out panels.
sheet.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn sheet components for navigation drawers, filtering panels, and detail views. Sheet is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and fully aligned with Shadcn Create so radius, colors, and typography match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Sheet: Side Panels, Drawers, and Modal Overlays
summary: Shadcn Sheet is a modal overlay that slides in from the edge (top, bottom, left, right), extending the Dialog component for flexible side-panel layouts. Built on Radix UI Dialog and compatible with full focus management and keyboard navigation, it integrates seamlessly with Shadcn Create theming.
It darkens the page backdrop and displays content at the screen edge, useful for navigation menus, filtering panels, and detail views that shouldn't scroll the main page.

ReUI lists sheet components inside dashboards, e-commerce filters, and mobile navigation so you evaluate edge positioning, width constraints, and how sheets pair with [Shadcn Button](/components/button), [Shadcn Input](/components/input), and [Shadcn Field](/components/field). The sections below explain what the component is, why teams use it, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Sheet?
paragraphs: A sheet is a modal overlay that extends from the page edge, similar to a drawer. Shadcn Sheet extends Radix UI Dialog to provide positioning (top, bottom, left, right) via the side prop, making it ideal for navigation, filters, and detail panels.

Components here show left-side navigation drawers, bottom sheets for mobile actions, right-side filter panels, and responsive sheets that change sides on mobile. They help you decide edge positioning, width/height constraints, and whether content should scroll inside the sheet or the page remains fixed.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Sheet?
paragraphs: Teams use sheets when they need modal content without overlaying the entire page center (where a full dialog would sit). Mobile navigation, filter menus, and product detail panels all benefit from edge-positioned overlays that feel lightweight and responsive.

Searchers look for 'react drawer', 'side panel modal', and 'mobile navigation overlay'. This page answers with components showing how sheets differ from dialogs (which center on screen), how to make sheets responsive, and when to use sheets vs. full-page navigation.

featureItems.title: Edge Positioning.
featureItems.description: Set side prop to 'top', 'bottom', 'left', or 'right' to position the sheet at the screen edge. Useful for navigation, filters, and detail views.

featureItems.title: Modal Behavior.
featureItems.description: Darkens the page backdrop and traps focus inside the sheet. Clicking outside or pressing Escape dismisses the sheet, following [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) semantics.

featureItems.title: Scrollable Content.
featureItems.description: Sheet content can overflow and scroll independently, keeping the main page fixed. Useful for long filter lists or navigation menus.

featureItems.title: Responsive Positioning.
featureItems.description: Use conditional logic to position sheets on the left on desktop and bottom on mobile, adapting to screen size. Pair with [Shadcn Drawer](/components/drawer) for similar functionality.

featureItems.title: Custom Width/Height.
featureItems.description: Set max-width on the SheetContent wrapper to control sheet size; left/right sheets often take 50% of viewport width on desktop.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Sheet and Drawer?
faqs.answer: Sheet extends [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) for side-positioned modals. Drawer is built on Vaul (by Emil Kowalski) and focuses on gesture-driven mobile UX with swipe-to-dismiss. Use Sheet for responsive, keyboard-first overlays; use Drawer for mobile-first, gesture-friendly experiences.

faqs.question: When should I use a Sheet vs. a full Dialog?
faqs.answer: Use Sheet for lightweight, non-blocking overlays at screen edges (navigation, filters). Use [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) for important decisions or full-page tasks that need center focus. Sheet feels lighter and leaves more of the page visible.

faqs.question: How do I make a Sheet responsive so it changes position on mobile?
faqs.answer: Use React hooks or CSS media queries to detect viewport size. Render Sheet with side='left' on desktop and side='bottom' on mobile. Alternatively, use a Drawer on mobile and Sheet on desktop for gesture support.

faqs.question: Can I scroll content inside a Sheet without scrolling the page?
faqs.answer: Yes. The page is automatically locked (overflow: hidden) when the Sheet opens. Content inside SheetContent overflows and scrolls independently. Use [Shadcn Scroll Area](/components/scroll-area) inside the sheet for custom scrollbar styling.

faqs.question: How do I style the Sheet width or height?
faqs.answer: Add className to SheetContent to set max-width or max-height. For left/right sheets, use max-w-sm, max-w-md, or max-w-full. For top/bottom sheets, use h-1/2 or h-2/3. Test on mobile to ensure the sheet doesn't exceed the viewport.

faqs.question: Is Sheet keyboard and screen-reader accessible?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sheet inherits Radix Dialog accessibility: Tab cycles through interactive elements, Escape closes, and roles are announced. Test with a screen reader to confirm the sheet title and close button are clear.
