relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Use Sheet for mobile navigation driven by a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) menu icon in the header. Place a [Shadcn Navigation Menu](/components/navigation-menu) or custom link list inside the sheet so tap targets are large and touch-friendly.

Pair Sheet with [Shadcn Input](/components/input) and [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for filter panels or search overlays. Add a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to apply filters or close the sheet when done editing.

Combine Sheet with [Shadcn Scroll Area](/components/scroll-area) when content overflows. Use [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) or [Shadcn Tag](/components/tag) inside the sheet to show active filters or selections. For mobile, use [Shadcn Drawer](/components/drawer) for swipe-dismiss gestures.

skeleton.description: {{count}} skeleton loader components for loading states, placeholder content, and animated shimmer effects. Match skeleton dimensions to real content dimensions.
skeleton.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn skeleton components for loading states, content placeholders, and progressive content revelation. Skeleton is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI composition and fully aligned with Shadcn Create so radius, colors, and typography match your design tokens.

content.title: Shadcn Skeleton: Loading Placeholders and Perceived Performance
summary: Shadcn Skeleton is a placeholder component that mimics the shape of final content while data is loading, improving perceived performance and reducing cognitive load. Built for the React and Tailwind ecosystem with full compatibility for Shadcn Create styling, it displays neutral gray boxes with optional shimmer animations.
It maintains layout stability during loading, preventing layout shift (Cumulative Layout Shift, a Core Web Vital) and giving users confidence that content is on the way.

ReUI lists skeleton components inside dashboards, cards, tables, and media galleries so you evaluate proportions, animation styles, and how skeletons pair with [Shadcn Card](/components/card), [Shadcn Table](/components/table), and [Shadcn Progress](/components/progress). The sections below explain what the component is, why it matters, key features, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Skeleton?
paragraphs: A skeleton screen is a placeholder UI that mimics the layout of real content while data loads. Shadcn Skeleton provides gray, rounded rectangles in various sizes for text lines, avatars, and images. When content arrives, the skeleton is replaced with real data.

Components here show text line skeletons (for blog post summaries), avatar skeletons, card skeletons (title + image + description), and table row skeletons. They help you decide skeleton sizes, animation style (wave or pulse), and how many placeholder items to show while data loads.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Skeleton?
paragraphs: Skeleton screens reduce the perception of load time. Instead of blank space or spinners, users see a preview of the final layout, creating anticipation rather than frustration.

Searchers look for 'react loading placeholder', 'skeleton loader', and 'shimmer effect'. This page answers with components showing how to craft skeletons that match final content dimensions, when to use skeletons vs. spinners, and how skeletons improve Core Web Vitals metrics.

featureItems.title: Customizable Size and Shape.
featureItems.description: Use Tailwind classes (h-12, w-full) to set skeleton dimensions. Pair with rounded-md for corners matching your final components.

featureItems.title: Shimmer Animation.
featureItems.description: Built-in pulse or wave animation that cycles smoothly, signaling that loading is in progress without being distracting.

featureItems.title: Multiple Placeholders.
featureItems.description: Stack skeletons to represent text lines, images, or full cards. Repeat the skeleton component to match expected content (e.g., 5 text lines + 1 image for a post preview).

featureItems.title: Layout Preservation.
featureItems.description: Skeleton maintains the space of final content so layout doesn't jump when data arrives, improving CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift).

featureItems.title: Theme Integration.
featureItems.description: Skeleton uses your Shadcn theme colors (muted background), so it looks cohesive with final content and respects dark mode automatically.

faqs.question: How long should a skeleton display before showing real content?
faqs.answer: Show skeletons whenever content takes longer than ~300ms to load. For very short delays, a skeleton may feel unnecessary. For long waits (5+ seconds), combine skeletons with a [Shadcn Progress](/components/progress) bar and estimated time so users know to expect a delay.

faqs.question: How do I size a skeleton to match my final content?
faqs.answer: Set skeleton height and width to match the actual text line height, image size, or component dimensions. Test by comparing skeleton layout to real content. Use a CSS grid or flexbox to position skeletons in the same layout as final data.

faqs.question: Should I use a skeleton or a spinner for loading?
faqs.answer: Use skeletons to preserve layout and set expectations for content shape. Use a [Shadcn Spinner](/components/spinner) for operations where you can't predict the final shape (bulk uploads, video processing). Combine both for best UX: show skeleton layout + central spinner for unknown-duration tasks.

faqs.question: How do I animate a skeleton?
faqs.answer: Shadcn Skeleton includes pulse (fade in/out) and wave (moving shimmer) animations. Wave feels faster and more polished for modern products. Customize animation speed via Tailwind theme or CSS keyframes if your product has a distinct loading feel.

faqs.question: Can I use skeleton inside a card or table?
faqs.answer: Yes. Create a skeleton version of your [Shadcn Card](/components/card) by replacing content with skeleton elements at matching dimensions. For [Shadcn Table](/components/table), create skeleton rows with skeleton cells so the table structure is visible while data loads.

faqs.question: Does skeleton improve Core Web Vitals?
faqs.answer: Yes. By preserving layout during loading, skeletons reduce Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS). They don't directly improve LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) or FID, but they create the perception of faster loading, improving user satisfaction and engagement metrics.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Place skeletons inside [Shadcn Card](/components/card) to load card content. When data arrives, replace skeleton elements with real components like [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge), [Shadcn Button](/components/button), and text. Test that final layout matches skeleton dimensions to avoid layout shift.
Use skeletons in [Shadcn Table](/components/table) rows while fetching data. Create skeleton rows with empty cells matching column widths. When data arrives, replace skeleton rows with real rows.

Combine skeletons with [Shadcn Scroll Area](/components/scroll-area) for long lists or [Shadcn Pagination](/components/pagination) for paginated content. Add [Shadcn Progress](/components/progress) to show upload or processing progress alongside skeletons for perceived performance.

slider.description: {{count}} range slider components with single and dual thumbs, step configuration, keyboard support, and form validation. Accessible slider controls.
slider.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn slider components for numeric range selection, price filtering, and continuous value adjustment. Shadcn Slider is compatible with Radix UI and Base UI interaction models and fully aligned with Shadcn Create style options.

content.title: Shadcn Slider: Numeric Range Input for React Apps
summary: Shadcn Slider is the continuous range input primitive for selecting values within a bounded interval. Built on Radix UI and Base UI interaction models, it maps keyboard and mouse input to a numeric scale so users can adjust settings like volume, brightness, or filter thresholds without typing.

It follows WAI-ARIA slider semantics and keyboard navigation so assistive technologies and keyboard-only users can control the range. Shadcn Create style options apply to slider tracks and thumbs so your design tokens flow into exported code.

ReUI lists slider components inside forms, price filters, and settings panels so you evaluate density, labels, and step increments next to real workflows. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how it pairs with other components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Slider?
paragraphs: In product UI, a slider is a control that lets users select a value or range by dragging a thumb along a track. Shadcn Slider follows Radix UI keyboard and focus semantics so screen readers announce the current value and range, and users can change values with arrow keys.

Components here show single and range sliders, labeled with units, paired with numeric input fields, and inside price filters or preference panels. They help you decide spacing, step behavior, and label placement before you wire the same component into Next.js or a React form.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Slider?
paragraphs: Teams use sliders when continuous numeric input fits better than dropdowns or discrete buttons. Price ranges, volume controls, zoom levels, and date ranges all benefit from a visual, draggable interface. Sliders reduce interaction cost when the user's goal is to explore or fine-tune a value.

Searchers look for 'shadcn slider', 'react range input', and 'tailwind price filter'. This page answers those intents with realistic layouts showing how to combine slider elements, labels, and helper text to guide users toward the right range.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Accessible.
featureItems.description: Arrow keys, Page Up/Down, and Home/End adjust the slider value; focus is clearly indicated for screen reader users.

featureItems.title: Range or Single Value.
featureItems.description: Choose a single thumb to select one value or multiple thumbs to select a range, depending on your filter or preference use case.

featureItems.title: Customizable Steps.
featureItems.description: Define step increments (e.g., $5 for price, 1 dB for volume) so users land on meaningful values instead of arbitrary fractional steps.

featureItems.title: Radix UI and Base UI Compatible.
featureItems.description: Styling uses tokens and composition that work with Radix UI projects and Base UI-style unstyled components when you manage theme consistency.

featureItems.title: Label and Helper Text.
featureItems.description: Pair slider with a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) label, description, and optional current-value display so users understand the range and its real-world meaning.

faqs.question: Should I use a slider or a numeric input for range selection?
faqs.answer: Use a slider when the user's goal is to explore or adjust a continuous value without typing. Combine slider with a [Shadcn Input](/components/input) field so users can type exact values when precision matters, and the two stay in sync.

faqs.question: How do I set up a range slider with two thumbs?
faqs.answer: Shadcn Slider supports multiple values in the state array. Define min, max, and step; provide an onChange handler to update both values together. Style each thumb independently if your design requires it.

faqs.question: Is a slider keyboard accessible?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arrow keys move the slider one step at a time, and Page Up/Down jump by larger increments. Home and End move to the min and max. Make sure your label is associated via a <label htmlFor> so assistive tech announces the purpose.

faqs.question: Can I add labels or currency symbols to a slider?
faqs.answer: Yes. Place a [Shadcn Label](/components/label) above or beside the slider and a text span showing the current value or range. Use a unit suffix (e.g., '$100') to clarify meaning without cluttering the track.

faqs.question: How does a slider integrate with React Hook Form?
faqs.answer: Use useController or register to bind slider value to form state. On change, update the field value. On form submit, the slider value is included in the payload like any other field.

faqs.question: Do sliders perform well on mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes, sliders work well on touch. Ensure the thumb hit target is 44px+ and there is space around the slider so users don't accidentally touch nearby elements. Test with your actual touch device to confirm comfort.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Place a [Shadcn Label](/components/label) above the slider to describe its purpose and a [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for optional helper text or error states when validation applies.
Pair the slider with an [Shadcn Input](/components/input) field so users can toggle between dragging and typing exact values. Keep their state synchronized in React.

Use sliders inside a [Shadcn Card](/components/card) or settings panel when the product has many adjustable parameters. Combine with a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to apply or reset all filters at once.

sonner.description: {{count}} sonner toast components for success, error, and loading states. Customizable positions, promise-based async, and programmatic dismissal.
sonner.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn `sonner` toast components for success notifications, error alerts, and loading feedback. Sonner wraps the Sonner toast library with Radix UI accessibility and Shadcn Create style compatibility.

content.title: Shadcn Sonner: Toast Notifications for React
summary: Shadcn Sonner wraps the opinionated Sonner toast library, a modern notification primitive for React that auto-dismisses after a timeout or user interaction. It follows Radix UI accessibility conventions so screen readers announce toasts and users can interact with actions before the notification disappears.

Import the Toaster once in your root layout and use `import { toast } from '`sonner`'` in your client components to trigger notifications. Sonner Shadcn Create style options ensure toasts respect your design tokens for color, radius, and typography.

ReUI lists `sonner` components inside forms, data tables, and workflow dashboards so you evaluate timing, action placement, and variant usage next to real product layouts. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how it integrates with other components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Sonner?
paragraphs: A toast is a transient notification that appears briefly in a fixed corner (usually bottom-right), delivers a status or action, and auto-dismisses or lets the user close it. Shadcn Sonner is built on the Sonner library and manages focus, keyboard navigation, and live region announcements so toasts feel native to the page.

Components here show success, error, loading, and info toasts with optional actions (undo, retry, view details). They help you decide which toast variant fits your feedback need and how to pair toasts with forms, buttons, and async operations without notification fatigue.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Sonner?
paragraphs: Teams use toasts for non-blocking feedback: form submissions, file uploads, bulk operations, and temporary errors that don't need a modal or overlay. Toasts keep users on task by delivering feedback without stealing focus or requiring a button click to dismiss.

Searchers look for 'shadcn toast', '`sonner` react', and 'next.js notification'. This page answers those intents with realistic workflows showing when to use toasts versus [Shadcn Alert](/docs/components/base/alert) (persistent) or [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) (blocking).

featureItems.title: Sonner Library Foundation.
featureItems.description: Built on Sonner (import { toast } from '`sonner`'), a battle-tested toast system for React and Next.js with great TypeScript support.

featureItems.title: Success, Error, Loading, and Info Variants.
featureItems.description: Four semantic types so users instantly recognize outcome. Error toasts may auto-dismiss slower to let users read the message.

featureItems.title: Actions and Links.
featureItems.description: Add an action button ('Undo', 'Retry') or link inside the toast so users can respond without losing context or navigating away.

featureItems.title: Auto-dismiss and Manual Close.
featureItems.description: Toasts auto-dismiss after a timeout or let users dismiss on demand. Control duration and whether users can close or only actions trigger dismissal.

featureItems.title: Accessible Announcements.
featureItems.description: Live regions and ARIA attributes announce toasts to screen readers; keyboard users can tab to actions and close buttons.

faqs.question: How do I use `sonner` toast in my React component?
faqs.answer: Install with shadcn CLI, add the Toaster to your root layout, then import and call `toast.success('Message')`, `toast.error()`, or other variants from your client component. Pass a message string and optional action object with label and onClick handler.

faqs.question: Should I use a toast or a persistent alert?
faqs.answer: Use toasts for transient feedback: form submissions, file uploads, quick confirmations. Use [Shadcn Alert](/docs/components/base/alert) for persistent warnings that users should see on every visit (e.g., billing problems). Use [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) for decisions that require user action before continuing.

faqs.question: How do I add an action button to a Sonner toast?
faqs.answer: Pass an `action` prop (object with `label` and `onClick`) to the toast call. For example, `toast.success('Deleted', { action: { label: 'Undo', onClick: () => restore() } })`.

faqs.question: Can I customize the toast duration or auto-dismiss behavior?
faqs.answer: Yes. Pass `duration` (milliseconds) to control how long before auto-dismiss. Set `duration: Infinity` to disable auto-dismiss and let the user close only via the button or action.

faqs.question: Is Sonner toast accessible to screen readers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sonner uses live regions and semantic markup so screen readers announce the toast message and any available actions. Test with a screen reader to confirm your toast message is clear.

faqs.question: How do I trigger a toast from a form submission or API call?
faqs.answer: Call the toast function in your onSuccess or onError callback (from React Query, SWR, or a try/catch). For example, after a successful mutation, call `toast.success('Saved!')`. Pair toasts with form validation and [Shadcn Field](/components/field) errors for complete feedback.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair toasts with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) form submissions so the click triggers an async action and a toast confirms success or shows an error.
Use toasts with [Shadcn Data Table](/components/data-table) or [Shadcn Card](/components/card) for bulk operations (delete rows, archive items) so feedback is instant and non-blocking.
Combine toasts with [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) for workflows where the dialog completes a task and the toast confirms the outcome (e.g., save dialog, then success toast).

sortable.description: {{count}} free shadcn sortable list components built with DnD Kit. Reorder items in lists, grids, nested layouts, playlists, settings, and image galleries. Copy-ready for React and Next.js. Reorder React list and grid items with Tailwind CSS-styled drag-and-drop sortables.
sortable.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn sortable components using `@dnd-kit/sortable`. Covers vertical lists, grid galleries, nested layouts, playlist ordering, settings priority, sidebar navigation, and image gallery sorting: all keyboard accessible and composable with shadcn primitives.

content.title: Shadcn Sortable: Reorder Lists, Grids, and Galleries
summary: ReUI Sortable is a custom shadcn component built on `@dnd-kit/sortable`: the same engine powering ReUI's Kanban and Data Grid column reordering. It wraps any list or grid of items in a SortableContext and exposes a useSortable hook-based API for individual items, giving you full control over drag handles, visual feedback, and drop animation.
Seven components cover vertical drag-and-drop lists, grid layouts, nested structures, playlist ordering, settings priority queues, sidebar navigation reordering, and image gallery grids.
The sections below explain the component architecture, practical use cases, and how Sortable composes with Kanban, File Upload, and other ReUI primitives.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Sortable?
paragraphs: ReUI Sortable is a custom drag-and-drop reordering component, distinct from the base shadcn/ui library. It uses `@dnd-kit/sortable`'s SortableContext and useSortable hook to make any array of React elements reorderable. Items receive a transform and transition during drag, a drag handle activates the gesture, and arrayMove updates the order in onDragEnd.

Components show sortable items in vertical lists, responsive grids, and nested layouts. Frame wrappers pair sortable lists with toolbars and Sonner feedback for async save: the same composed layout used in ReUI's sidebar navigation and settings priority examples.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Sortable?
paragraphs: Reordering is a common interaction: playlist tracks, dashboard widget order, sidebar navigation priority, settings queue, image gallery arrangement, and task priority all benefit from drag-and-drop reordering with a save action. DnD Kit provides the most accessible, touch-friendly, and React-idiomatic implementation available.

ReUI Sortable components go beyond a bare list: they show real content (cards with avatars and badges, images with overlays, nav items with icons) inside Frame containers with Sonner toast feedback. Pair with [Shadcn Kanban](/docs/components/base/kanban) for column-based boards and [Shadcn File Upload](/docs/components/base/file-upload) for drag-to-reorder uploaded images.

featureItems.title: DnD Kit Sortable Engine.
featureItems.description: Powered by `@dnd-kit/sortable`. SortableContext provides the item order and collision strategy; useSortable exposes transform, transition, and setNodeRef for each item. arrayMove handles order updates in onDragEnd.

featureItems.title: Vertical List, Grid, and Nested Layouts.
featureItems.description: Switch between vertical list, responsive CSS grid, and nested item structures without rebuilding drag logic. Layout changes are Tailwind class changes: the sortable context stays identical.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Accessible Reordering.
featureItems.description: DnD Kit's keyboard sensor lets users reorder items with arrow keys and confirm with Space or Enter. Meets WCAG 2.1 success criteria for drag-and-drop alternatives without extra code.

featureItems.title: Drag Handle Component.
featureItems.description: A dedicated drag handle icon activates the drag gesture, leaving the rest of the item interactive. Buttons, links, inputs, and toggle switches inside items work normally without triggering a drag.

featureItems.title: Practical Real-World Presets.
featureItems.description: Seven components cover playlist ordering, settings priority, sidebar navigation, and image gallery grids: all inside Frame containers with Sonner toast feedback for persisting the new order to a backend.

featureItems.title: Image Gallery Sorting.
featureItems.description: Grid sortable component with image overlays supports drag-to-reorder uploaded images: composable with the File Upload component for a complete image management flow.

featureItems.title: Smooth Drop Animation.
featureItems.description: DragOverlay renders a floating clone of the active item during drag. CSS transitions animate the surrounding items into their new positions as the dragged item moves, giving users clear spatial feedback.

sections.title: ReUI Sortable: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Sortable is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project: following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Sortable is a custom drag-and-drop reordering component not available in base shadcn/ui. Built on `@dnd-kit/sortable` using SortableContext and useSortable hooks, it handles pointer, touch, and keyboard drag with arrayMove state updates. Seven components cover vertical lists, grid galleries, nested layouts, playlist ordering, settings priority, sidebar navigation, and image gallery sorting.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling: only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version — API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version — API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: How do I persist sort order to a database after reordering?
faqs.answer: Call your Server Action or API endpoint in onDragEnd after arrayMove updates state. Handle optimistic updates in the UI while the backend saves. Use Sonner toast to show success or rollback on error.

faqs.question: Does sortable support keyboard drag-and-drop accessibility?
faqs.answer: Yes. DnD Kit's keyboard sensor lets users reorder items with arrow keys and confirm with Space or Enter. This meets WCAG 2.1 drag-and-drop accessibility requirements without extra code.

faqs.question: Can I use sortable inside a scroll container?
faqs.answer: Yes, but configure activationConstraint to prevent accidental drags during scrolling. Set a delay (100ms) or distance (10px) threshold. The auto-scroll plugin helps users drag items near scroll edges.

faqs.question: How do I implement optimistic reorder updates with rollback?
faqs.answer: Update local state immediately in onDragEnd. Call the API in a try/catch. If the API succeeds, you're done. If it fails, revert state to the previous order and show an error toast.

faqs.question: Can I create nested sortable lists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Nest multiple SortableContext components and handle onDragEnd at each level. Drag items between parent and child lists by checking the over index. The tree component demonstrates nested sorting.

faqs.question: How does sortable work with images and media files?
faqs.answer: Render image elements inside sortable items. The drag handle and transform still apply. Pair with File Upload to let users upload then reorder images. The image gallery component shows this flow.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Pair Sortable with [Shadcn File Upload](/docs/components/base/file-upload) to build an image management flow: users upload files, then drag-and-drop to reorder them before saving. The image gallery sortable component demonstrates this exact composition.

Use Sortable for single-list reordering and [Shadcn Kanban](/docs/components/base/kanban) for multi-column board reordering. Both share the same DnD Kit engine, so drag handle, overlay, and keyboard conventions are consistent across your app.

Wrap sortable lists in [Shadcn Frame](/docs/components/base/frame) for toolbar and action chrome, use [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) and [Shadcn Avatar](/components/avatar) for rich item content, and [Shadcn Sonner](/components/sonner) for async save feedback when order changes are persisted.

spinner.description: {{count}} free shadcn spinner components for loading states, async feedback, and skeleton combos. Indicate React loading operations with Tailwind CSS-styled animated spinners.
spinner.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn spinner components for loading indicators, progress feedback, and async operation states. Shadcn Spinner includes accessible ARIA labels and pairs seamlessly with Shadcn Create styles.

content.title: Shadcn Spinner: Loading Indicators for React Apps
summary: Shadcn Spinner is a minimal loading indicator that signals async operations or data fetches in progress. Built with Lucide icons and Tailwind animation utilities, it pairs with ARIA live regions and status roles so screen readers announce loading state.
Customize the icon from Lucide and the animation class to match your brand. Shadcn Create style options apply to spinner color and sizing so your design tokens flow into exported code.

ReUI lists spinner components inside buttons, data tables, form submissions, and skeleton loading states so you evaluate placement, timing, and animation rhythm next to real layouts. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how it pairs with other components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Spinner?
paragraphs: A spinner is a rotating or animated icon that conveys an in-progress operation. Shadcn Spinner uses a Lucide icon (default: LoaderIcon) with the Tailwind `animate-spin` class and wraps it with semantic HTML and ARIA attributes so assistive technologies understand the loading context.

Components here show spinners in buttons during form submission, floating spinners for page-level loads, spinners paired with text for clarity, and inline spinners in data table cells. They help you decide placement, size, and whether text or an icon alone is enough.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Spinner?
paragraphs: Teams use spinners to indicate that the app is fetching data or processing a request. A visual indicator reduces user frustration by confirming the app is responding, even if the operation is slow. Spinners are usually paired with disabled buttons or other UI cues to prevent duplicate submissions.

Searchers look for 'react loading spinner', 'tailwind spinner animation', and 'accessible loading indicator'. This page answers those intents with realistic layouts showing how to combine spinners with buttons, skeleton loaders, and progress text.

featureItems.title: Lucide Icon Foundation.
featureItems.description: Customizable SVG icon from Lucide; swap the LoaderIcon for another icon that fits your brand or context (e.g., MoreVertical for a rotating menu).

featureItems.title: Tailwind Animation.
featureItems.description: Uses the `animate-spin` class for smooth rotation. Customize duration or easing via Tailwind theme or inline utilities.

featureItems.title: Accessible Announcements.
featureItems.description: Pair with role="status" and aria-live="polite" so screen readers announce the loading state. Include visually-hidden text for clarity.

featureItems.title: Size and Color Variants.
featureItems.description: Control spinner size via Lucide size props and color via Tailwind text utilities. Match your [Shadcn Button](/components/button) or other UI elements.

featureItems.title: Button and Inline Placement.
featureItems.description: Render inside [Shadcn Button](/components/button) (with data-icon) or inline with text, list items, or table cells for contextual loading feedback.

faqs.question: Should I show a spinner or skeleton loader for page-level loads?
faqs.answer: Use a spinner for quick, short-lived operations (less than 2 seconds). Use Skeleton loaders for longer waits so the UI feels more responsive with placeholders. Combine both: skeleton content with spinners at strategic points.

faqs.question: How do I show a spinner in a button during async operations?
faqs.answer: Set a loading state in React. When true, render the spinner inside the button and disable it with the disabled prop. For example: `<Button disabled={isLoading}><Spinner /> {isLoading ? 'Saving...' : 'Save'}</Button>`.

faqs.question: Is a spinner accessible to screen readers?
faqs.answer:
By default, an animated icon is invisible to screen readers. Wrap the spinner with `
Loading...
` so the status is announced.

faqs.question: What's the difference between a spinner and a progress bar?
faqs.answer: Spinners indicate something is loading but don't show progress percentage. Progress bars show a completion percentage (0–100%). Use spinners for fast, indeterminate operations; use progress for long uploads/downloads.

faqs.question: Can I customize the spinner animation speed?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use Tailwind utilities like `animate-spin` and customize duration in your Tailwind config, or add inline CSS animation properties. Avoid very fast animations that may trigger vestibular motion issues.

faqs.question: Do spinners work inside disabled button states?
faqs.answer: Yes. The spinner animates even inside a disabled button. The disabled state prevents clicks while the loading indicator shows the operation is in progress. Once complete, enable the button and remove the spinner.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Place spinners inside [Shadcn Button](/components/button) components with the disabled prop to prevent double-clicks during async operations. Disable the button and show a spinner until the operation completes.
Pair spinners with [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) loaders for faster perceived performance. Skeleton placeholders fill the layout while spinners indicate data is still fetching.

Use spinners in [Shadcn Card](/components/card) or [Shadcn Data Table](/components/data-table) to show item-level loading. Combine with [Shadcn Alert](/docs/components/base/alert) or [Shadcn Sonner](/components/sonner) to show completion or errors once the operation finishes.

stepper.description: {{count}} free shadcn stepper components: progress bars, segmented bars, vertical, loading state, controlled, icons, badges, and inline titles. Copy-ready for React and Next.js. Walk React users through multi-step forms with Tailwind CSS-styled step indicators.
stepper.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn stepper components for multi-step forms, onboarding flows, and checkout processes. Covers progress bar, segmented progress, vertical orientation, completed state, loading state, controlled navigation, icons and badges, step descriptions, and inline title variants: a custom ReUI component.

content.title: Shadcn Stepper: Multi-Step Forms and Onboarding Flows
summary: ReUI Stepper is a custom shadcn component for guiding users through multi-step processes. It renders step indicators: numbered circles, icons, or progress segments: connected by a progress line, and controls which step is active, completed, or pending.

Fifteen components cover basic steppers, step content panels, progress bar indicator, segmented progress bar, vertical orientation with descriptions, vertical with titles, completed state, loading state per step, controlled navigation, step with titles and indicator, icons and badges per step, step descriptions, and inline title layouts.
The sections below explain the component model, use cases in checkout and onboarding, features, and how Stepper composes with Form, Button, and Badge.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Stepper?
paragraphs: ReUI Stepper is a custom component: not part of base shadcn/ui: that renders a multi-step progress indicator with controlled step navigation. Each step has an index, title, optional description, and content panel. The active step is set from outside via props (controlled mode) or managed internally (uncontrolled). Steps transition between pending, active, completed, loading, and error states.

Fifteen components show steppers in realistic multi-step form layouts: a checkout stepper with address, payment, and confirm steps; an onboarding wizard with account setup, profile, and preferences; a form with per-step validation where the next button is disabled until the current step's fields pass Zod validation.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Stepper?
paragraphs: Multi-step forms reduce cognitive load by breaking long forms (checkout, registration, onboarding, application) into smaller focused screens. A clear step indicator shows users where they are, how many steps remain, and which steps are complete: reducing abandonment. ReUI Stepper provides all visual states (completed checkmark, loading spinner, error icon) and orientation variants without custom CSS.

The controlled stepper component is critical for form validation: only advance when the current step's fields pass validation, and allow back navigation to any completed step. Pair with React Hook Form for per-step validation and [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for optional step labels.

featureItems.title: Custom ReUI Component.
featureItems.description: ReUI Stepper is a custom shadcn component not available in base shadcn/ui. It is purpose-built for multi-step forms, onboarding wizards, and checkout flows with full state management and accessibility.

featureItems.title: Progress Bar and Segmented Indicator.
featureItems.description: Two progress display modes: a continuous progress bar that fills proportionally as steps complete, and a segmented bar where each step occupies an equal segment: useful for checkout indicators.

featureItems.title: Vertical and Horizontal Orientation.
featureItems.description: Horizontal orientation for compact headers; vertical orientation for sidebars and step-by-step guides where each step has a description and content area below the indicator.

featureItems.title: Completed, Loading, and Error States.
featureItems.description: Steps transition through pending, active, loading (async operations with Spinner), completed (checkmark icon), and error states. Each state has a distinct visual indicator.

featureItems.title: Controlled Navigation.
featureItems.description: Fully controlled via activeStep prop. Pass onStepChange to handle navigation. Disable the next button until the current step validates. Allow back navigation to any completed step.

featureItems.title: Icons and Badges per Step.
featureItems.description: Replace number indicators with custom icons (upload, payment, confirm) and add Badge labels (optional, required, new) to step indicators without breaking the progress line layout.

featureItems.title: Step Descriptions and Inline Titles.
featureItems.description: Each step supports title and description text. Inline title variant places the title on the same line as the indicator for compact layouts; separate title variant stacks them vertically for detailed guides.

featureItems.title: Step Content Panels.
featureItems.description: Each step renders a content panel: form fields, information, or confirmation UI: that shows when the step is active. Transition between steps with smooth content swap or page-level routing.

sections.title: ReUI Stepper: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Stepper is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project: following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Stepper is a custom multi-step progress component not available in base shadcn/ui. It renders step indicators in horizontal and vertical orientations with distinct visual states: pending, active, loading (Spinner), completed (checkmark), and error. Fifteen components cover all common multi-step form, checkout, and onboarding wizard layouts with controlled and uncontrolled navigation modes.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling: only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version — API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version — API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: How do I validate each step before advancing to the next?
faqs.answer: Use React Hook Form with mode: 'onChange' and call trigger(['fieldA', 'fieldB']) when the user clicks Next. If trigger returns true, advance. If false, errors display and navigation is blocked.

faqs.question: Can I navigate back without losing the data I entered in previous steps?
faqs.answer: Yes. Controlled stepper keeps all step values in form state (React Hook Form maintains them). Clicking Back navigates to the prior step. The form state persists, so previous entries remain intact.

faqs.question: Can I create a non-linear stepper where users skip steps?
faqs.answer: Yes. In controlled mode, allow onStepChange to set activeStep to any index, not just current +/- 1. Disable steps that shouldn't be accessed yet. Show conditional step content based on earlier choices.

faqs.question: How do I persist step completion state if the user closes and returns?
faqs.answer: Save step state to localStorage or session storage after each step completes. On page reload, restore the state and resume from the last completed step. Use a Server Action to persist to a database.

faqs.question: Can I embed a stepper inside a dialog?
faqs.answer: Yes. The stepper renders inside the dialog body. Content panels swap as users navigate steps. The dialog footer has Previous and Next buttons. Use a callback to close the dialog on completion.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Radix and Base UI versions of ReUI Stepper?
faqs.answer: Both versions have identical visual output and features. Radix UI version uses Radix primitives for button, form, and accessibility. Base UI version uses Base UI primitives. Choose the version that matches your existing primitive stack.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Pair Stepper with React Hook Form: use trigger() for per-step field validation before advancing, and handleSubmit() on the final step. Wrap each step's fields in [Shadcn Field](/components/field) with [Shadcn Label](/components/label) and error message slots.

Use [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for Previous and Next navigation controls below the step content, [Shadcn Spinner](/components/spinner) inside the Next button during async step transitions, and [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for optional or required step labels on indicators.

Embed Stepper in [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) for modal multi-step flows like payment setup or account creation, or in [Shadcn Card](/components/card) for inline setup wizards on dashboard pages.

switch.description: {{count}} free shadcn switch components for toggles, feature flags. Toggle React boolean settings with Tailwind CSS-styled accessible switch controls.
switch.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn switch components for boolean toggles, settings panels, and feature flags. Shadcn Switch is built on Radix UI primitives and fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options.

content.title: Shadcn Switch: Toggle Control for React Forms
summary: Shadcn Switch is a boolean control that toggles between checked and unchecked states. Built on Radix UI primitives, it follows WAI-ARIA switch semantics and keyboard navigation so screen readers announce state and keyboard users can toggle via Space or Enter.

Use in settings panels, feature flags, notification preferences, and form checkboxes where yes/no or on/off semantics are clearer than [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox). Shadcn Create style options apply to switch colors and sizing so your design tokens flow into exported code.

ReUI lists switch components inside preference panels, settings dashboards, and form layouts so you evaluate label placement, spacing, and state clarity next to real workflows. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how it pairs with other components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Switch?
paragraphs: A switch is a control that toggles a boolean value on and off. Unlike a [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox), a switch explicitly conveys an on/off or enabled/disabled state. Shadcn Switch uses the Radix UI Switch primitive, which manages focus, keyboard input, and live region updates for assistive tech.

Components here show switch with labels, descriptions, and disabled states inside settings panels, form groups, and feature toggles. They help you decide whether a switch or checkbox is clearer for your use case and where to place the label for scanning.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Switch?
paragraphs: Teams use switches for settings and feature flags where the semantic is clearly on/off or enabled/disabled. Switches feel more intuitive than checkboxes for toggles because the visual design conveys state change.

Searchers look for 'react switch component', 'toggle switch react', and 'radix ui switch'. This page answers those intents with realistic layouts showing how to pair switches with labels, descriptions, and form validation.

featureItems.title: Radix UI Foundation.
featureItems.description: Built on Radix UI Switch primitive. Handles keyboard navigation, focus management, and ARIA attributes automatically.

featureItems.title: Boolean State.
featureItems.description: Checked/unchecked or enabled/disabled. Use with React state or form libraries like React Hook Form for validation and submission.

featureItems.title: Label Association.
featureItems.description: Pair with [Shadcn Label](/components/label) via htmlFor attribute so clicking the label toggles the switch and screen readers associate them.

featureItems.title: Disabled State.
featureItems.description: Set disabled prop to prevent user interaction. Style the disabled switch differently so users understand it's not available.

featureItems.title: Size and Color Variants.
featureItems.description: Customize via Tailwind utilities and Shadcn Create style options so switches match your design system's colors and sizing.

faqs.question: Should I use a switch or a checkbox for a boolean form field?
faqs.answer: Use Checkbox for affirmations (I agree to terms). Use a switch for toggles that clearly imply state change (Enable notifications, Dark mode). Context matters more than the component choice.

faqs.question: How do I pair a switch with a label?
faqs.answer: Use Label with the same id as the switch's id prop: `<Label htmlFor="notifications">Enable Notifications</Label><Switch id="notifications" />`.

faqs.question: Can I use a switch with React Hook Form for form validation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use useController or register to bind the switch to form state. The switch onChange callback updates the field value. On form submit, the boolean value is included in the payload.

faqs.question: Should I show a description under a switch label?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially for settings. Use a Field component with FieldDescription to explain what the toggle does (e.g., 'Send notifications when someone comments on your post').

faqs.question: When should a switch take effect immediately vs on form submit?
faqs.answer: Immediate effect works for settings you want to apply right away (theme toggle, notification preference). Form submit works for multi-field forms where you gather all changes before persisting. Use onChange callbacks for immediate saves.

faqs.question: Is a switch keyboard accessible?
faqs.answer: Yes. Tab to focus the switch, then Space or Enter to toggle. Radix UI handles all keyboard semantics. Ensure the label is clickable and the switch announces state to screen readers.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair switches with [Shadcn Label](/components/label) and [Shadcn Field](/components/field) so labels, descriptions, and error states stay organized in a settings panel or form.
Place switches inside [Shadcn Card](/components/card) sections for grouped preferences (e.g., Email notifications, In-app notifications, Push notifications).

Combine switches with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to save settings or apply changes. Use [Shadcn Sonner](/components/sonner) or [Shadcn Alert](/docs/components/base/alert) to confirm the toggle was saved.

table.description: {{count}} free shadcn table components for data grids, sorting, filtering. Display React tabular data with Tailwind CSS-styled, semantic HTML table components.
table.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn table components with sorting, filtering, and pagination powered by TanStack Table (react-table v8). Fully compatible with Radix UI and Shadcn Create style options.

content.title: Shadcn Table: Data Grids with Sorting and Filtering
summary: Shadcn Table is a semantic HTML table wrapper paired with TanStack Table (react-table v8), a headless library for sorting, filtering, pagination, and column management. Together they handle complex data display without forcing you into a monolithic component.

Define columns, data, and features (sorting, filtering) via TanStack Table API. Render rows and cells using Shadcn Table semantic HTML. Shadcn Create style options apply to table surfaces so your design tokens flow into exported code.

ReUI lists table components inside dashboards, admin interfaces, and data-heavy workflows so you evaluate column density, action menus, and pagination next to real layouts. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how TanStack Table powers the component.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Table?
paragraphs: A table is a structured layout for displaying rows and columns of data. Shadcn Table uses semantic HTML `<table>`, `<thead>`, `<tbody>`, and `<tr>` elements, paired with TanStack Table for client- or server-side sorting, filtering, and pagination. TanStack Table is a headless library, meaning it manages state but leaves rendering to you.

Components here show simple tables, sortable columns, column filters, row selection, pagination, and inline actions. They help you decide which TanStack Table features your product needs and how to style them with Tailwind and your design tokens.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Table?
paragraphs: Teams use tables when data is columnar and users benefit from sorting, filtering, or searching. Dashboards, admin interfaces, data exports, and analytics all rely on tables. TanStack Table handles the stateful logic so you focus on styling and UX.

Searchers look for 'shadcn table', 'tanstack table react', and 'data table pagination sorting'. This page answers those intents with realistic layouts showing how TanStack Table integrates with [Shadcn Button](/components/button), [Shadcn Select](/components/select), and [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) for column selection and row actions.

featureItems.title: TanStack Table (react-table V8) Foundation.
featureItems.description: Headless table library handling sorting, filtering, pagination, grouping, and column management. Semantic HTML rendering stays your responsibility.

featureItems.title: Sorting by Column.
featureItems.description: Click column headers to sort ascending/descending. Customize sort icons and behavior. Support multi-column sort with Shift+Click.

featureItems.title: Client or Server Pagination.
featureItems.description: TanStack Table supports both. Client-side uses all data in memory; server-side fetches pages on demand. Choose based on dataset size.

featureItems.title: Column Visibility and Resizing.
featureItems.description: Show/hide columns via checkbox menu. Drag column borders to resize. Store preferences in localStorage so they persist across sessions.

featureItems.title: Row Selection and Bulk Actions.
featureItems.description: Select rows with checkboxes. Show bulk actions (delete, archive) when rows are selected. Pair with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for confirm dialogs.

faqs.question: Should I use table, data-table, or data-grid?
faqs.answer: Table: semantic HTML for simple structured data. Data-table (TanStack Table): adds sorting, filtering, pagination for complex datasets. Data-grid: spreadsheet-like with inline editing and column resizing. Choose by feature complexity.

faqs.question: How do I make a table responsive without a data-table library?
faqs.answer: Horizontal scroll on mobile: `<div className="overflow-x-auto"><table>`. Or stack rows as cards on mobile using CSS grid: `@media (max-width: 640px) { table { display: block; } tr { display: block; margin-bottom: 1rem; } }`.

faqs.question: Can I sort a table without TanStack Table?
faqs.answer: Yes, but you manage state manually. Click a header to toggle sort direction, filter the data array, then re-render. TanStack Table abstracts this complexity and handles edge cases like multi-column sorting and server-side sorting.

faqs.question: How do I add a sticky header to a table?
faqs.answer: Use CSS `position: sticky; top: 0;` on the `<thead>` or header rows. Pair with `z-index` and `background-color` so the header stays visible while scrolling. Works with horizontal and vertical scrolling.

faqs.question: How do I show loading state in a table?
faqs.answer: Render skeleton rows while data loads, or show a spinner in the table body. Disable pagination and sort controls until data arrives. Once loaded, replace skeletons with actual data rows.

faqs.question: Can I add row selection and bulk actions to a table?
faqs.answer: Yes. TanStack Table's row selection API manages checkboxes and state. Add a toolbar above the table with bulk action buttons (delete, export, archive). Enable buttons only when rows are selected.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Add [Shadcn Dropdown Menu](/components/dropdown-menu) to each row for Edit, Delete, and other actions. Pair with [Shadcn Dialog](/components/dialog) or [Shadcn Alert Dialog](/components/alert-dialog) for confirmations.
Use [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) for row selection. Show a bulk action bar with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) when rows are selected.
Combine [Shadcn Pagination](/components/pagination) controls with TanStack Table pagination state. Use [Shadcn Select](/components/select) for page size selection (10, 25, 50 rows per page).

tabs.description: {{count}} free shadcn tabs components for navigation, content switching. Switch React content views with Tailwind CSS-styled accessible tab navigation.
tabs.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn tabs components for multi-panel navigation, content switching, and tabbed dashboards. Shadcn Tabs is built on Radix UI primitives and fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options.

content.title: Shadcn Tabs: Tabbed Navigation and Content Switching
summary: Shadcn Tabs is a tabbed navigation primitive for switching between parallel sections of content. Built on Radix UI, it follows WAI-ARIA tab semantics and keyboard navigation so screen readers announce tabs and users can navigate with arrow keys.

Compose Tabs with TabsList (the tab bar) and TabsTrigger and TabsContent (the tab and panel). Manage active tab via React state or URL parameters. Shadcn Create style options apply to tab bar and content surfaces so your design tokens flow into exported code.

ReUI lists tabs components inside dashboard settings, multi-section forms, product detail pages, and analytics dashboards so you evaluate horizontal/vertical orientation, icon+text triggers, and content density next to real layouts. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how it pairs with other components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Tabs?
paragraphs: A tab set is a control that switches between multiple sections of content on the same page. Each tab is a trigger (button or link), and each panel is revealed when its tab is active. Shadcn Tabs uses the Radix UI Tab primitive, which manages focus, keyboard navigation, and ARIA attributes.

Components here show horizontal and vertical tab layouts, icon-only and text-only triggers, disabled tabs, and keyboard-driven navigation. They help you decide whether tabs or a full page navigation (e.g., a sidebar) fits your workflow.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Tabs?
paragraphs: Teams use tabs when content is parallel (not hierarchical) and users frequently switch between sections. Dashboard settings, product details (description, reviews, specs), and form sections all use tabs to keep related content together without a full page navigation.

Searchers look for 'shadcn tabs', 'react tab navigation', and 'accessible tabs component'. This page answers those intents with realistic layouts showing how to combine tabs with [Shadcn Field](/components/field) groups, [Shadcn Button](/components/button) actions, and form validation.

featureItems.title: Radix UI Foundation.
featureItems.description: Built on Radix UI Tab primitive. Handles keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Home, End), focus management, and ARIA attributes automatically.

featureItems.title: Horizontal and Vertical Orientation.
featureItems.description: Default is horizontal. Set orientation="vertical" for sidebar-style tabs where triggers stack above content.

featureItems.title: Icon and Text Triggers.
featureItems.description: Tabs can show text only, icons only (with [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) hints), or both. Customize styling via Shadcn Create or Tailwind utilities.

featureItems.title: Disabled Tabs.
featureItems.description: Set disabled attribute on TabsTrigger to prevent users from activating that tab. Show a [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) explaining why it's disabled.

featureItems.title: Dynamic Content and Forms.
featureItems.description: Each TabsContent can hold independent form sections, tables, or media. Validation applies per section or across all tabs before final submission.

faqs.question: Should I use controlled or uncontrolled tabs?
faqs.answer: Uncontrolled tabs manage state internally: simpler for static layouts. Controlled tabs receive activeTab as a prop and call onValueChange: necessary for URL syncing or cross-component coordination. Controlled is more flexible.

faqs.question: How do I sync tabs with the URL in Next.js App Router?
faqs.answer: Use the `nuqs` library for URL state: `const [tab, setTab] = useQueryState('tab', parseAsString.withDefault('home')); <TabsList><TabsTrigger onClick={() => setTab('home')}>`. The URL updates when tabs change.

faqs.question: Can I lazy-render tab content?
faqs.answer:
Yes. Only render content for the active tab: `{activeTab === 'home' &&
Home content
}`. This improves performance if tabs contain heavy components. Keep inactive content mounted for smooth switching, or unmount to save memory.

faqs.question: How do I create vertical tabs instead of horizontal?
faqs.answer: Use CSS flexbox with flex-col: `<Tabs className="flex flex-col"><TabsList className="flex flex-col">`. Place tab triggers on the left, content on the right. Adjust width and layout to match your design.

faqs.question: What keyboard shortcuts should tabs support?
faqs.answer: Arrow keys navigate between tabs (left/right for horizontal, up/down for vertical). Home goes to the first tab, End to the last. Enter or Space activates the focused tab. Most tab libraries handle this automatically.

faqs.question: How do I announce active tab changes to screen readers?
faqs.answer: Use role="tablist", role="tab", and aria-selected on triggers, and role="tabpanel" on content. Libraries like Radix UI handle this automatically. Focus moves to the active tab on change, announcing state to assistive tech.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Place [Shadcn Field](/components/field) groups and [Shadcn Input](/components/input) components inside each TabsContent for multi-step or grouped forms.

Use [Shadcn Button](/components/button) inside tabs for actions like Save or Export. Pair with [Shadcn Sonner](/components/sonner) or [Shadcn Alert](/docs/components/base/alert) to show submission feedback.
Combine tabs with [Shadcn Card](/components/card) or [Shadcn Table](/components/table) inside each content panel for dashboard layouts with detailed views per section.

textarea.description: {{count}} free shadcn textarea components for multi-line text input, comments, and form fields. Accept React multi-line text input with Tailwind CSS-styled textarea components.
textarea.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn textarea components for multi-line text input, form submission, and accessible form fields. Textarea is fully compatible with React Hook Form and Shadcn Create style options.

content.title: Shadcn Textarea: Multi-Line Text Input for Forms
summary: Shadcn Textarea is a semantic HTML textarea element styled with Tailwind and ready for form binding. It follows accessibility best practices by pairing with labels and supporting ARIA invalid states for validation feedback.

Use in contact forms, comment sections, feedback fields, and any form that requires multi-line text input. Pair with React Hook Form or TanStack Form for validation. Shadcn Create style options apply to textarea borders and colors so your design tokens flow into exported code.

ReUI lists textarea components inside contact forms, comment interfaces, and settings with character counts so you evaluate sizing, placeholder clarity, and validation messaging next to real workflows. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how it pairs with form components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Textarea?
paragraphs: A textarea is an HTML form control that accepts multi-line text input. Unlike an [Shadcn Input](/components/input), it allows line breaks and is taller by default. Shadcn Textarea is a styled wrapper around the native `<textarea>` element with Tailwind utilities for borders, padding, and focus states.

Components here show textarea with labels, descriptions, character counters, and placeholder hints. They help you decide sizing (rows prop), whether to allow resizing, and how to handle overflow for long text.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Textarea?
paragraphs: Teams use textareas for user-generated content: feedback, comments, descriptions, and long-form text. A textarea signals to users that multi-line input is expected, unlike a single-line input which suggests brief text only.

Searchers look for 'shadcn textarea', 'react textarea component', and 'form textarea validation'. This page answers those intents with realistic layouts showing how to combine textarea with [Shadcn Label](/components/label), [Shadcn Field](/components/field), and form validation.

featureItems.title: Native HTML Textarea.
featureItems.description: Semantic `<textarea>` element with Tailwind styling for borders, padding, and focus states. Supports all native HTML attributes.

featureItems.title: Resize Control.
featureItems.description: Allow users to resize via the resize handle (default) or disable with CSS to maintain layout. Customize via Tailwind resize utilities.

featureItems.title: Row Height.
featureItems.description: Set the rows prop to control initial height. Let CSS flex or auto-expand handle dynamic sizing for growing text.

featureItems.title: Label and Description.
featureItems.description: Pair with [Shadcn Label](/components/label) via htmlFor and [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for descriptions and error messages to guide user input.

featureItems.title: Placeholder and Validation.
featureItems.description: Use clear placeholder text (not a substitute for labels). Add aria-invalid and data-invalid for validation states in forms.

faqs.question: How do I auto-resize a textarea as the user types?
faqs.answer: Set the rows attribute dynamically based on content height, or use CSS `resize: none` with max-height and overflow-y: auto for a scrollable box. A common component: `useEffect(() => { textarea.style.height = textarea.scrollHeight + 'px' }, [value])`.

faqs.question: Can I enforce a character limit on textarea input?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use the maxLength HTML attribute: `

faqs.question: How do I integrate textarea with React Hook Form?
faqs.answer: Use register to bind the textarea to form state: `const { register } = useForm(); <Textarea {...register('message')} />`.

faqs.question: When should I use textarea vs a single-line input?
faqs.answer: Use Input for short single-line responses (name, email, search). Use Textarea for longer multi-line content (comments, descriptions, bio, feedback). Consider placeholder text to guide user expectations.

faqs.question: How do I create a markdown input component with a live preview?
faqs.answer: Pair the textarea with a preview pane that renders markdown via a library like `markdown-it` or `react-markdown`. Update the preview on textarea onChange. Lay out the textarea on the left, preview on the right.

faqs.question: Is textarea accessible to screen readers?
faqs.answer: Yes, if paired with a Label via htmlFor. Use ARIA attributes for helper text: `aria-describedby="help-text"`. Announce character count updates with live regions.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Pair textarea with [Shadcn Label](/components/label) and [Shadcn Field](/components/field) for organized form layouts with descriptions and validation errors.
Use textarea inside a [Shadcn Card](/components/card) for comment or feedback sections. Add a [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to submit the comment.
Combine textarea with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) to create comment or message submission workflows. Show [Shadcn Sonner](/components/sonner) feedback after submission.

timeline.description: {{count}} free shadcn timeline components for deployment logs, activity feeds, git history, milestones, roadmaps, and pipeline steps. Copy-ready for React and Next.js. Visualize React event sequences with Tailwind CSS-styled timeline components.
timeline.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn timeline components for deployment logs, activity feeds with user avatars, horizontal milestone timelines, roadmaps, order status, git activity, pipeline steps, and vertical and horizontal orientations: a custom ReUI component composable with Avatar, Badge, Collapsible, and Frame.

content.title: Shadcn Timeline: Activity Feeds, Logs, and Milestones
summary: ReUI Timeline is a custom shadcn component: not in the base shadcn/ui library: for rendering sequential event data in vertical and horizontal orientations. It composes shadcn Avatar, Badge, Collapsible, Frame, and Spinner to produce activity feeds, deployment logs, audit trails, milestone trackers, and pipeline status indicators.

Twelve components cover basic timelines, deployment log with collapsible log entries, activity feed with user avatars, compact horizontal milestone timeline, roadmap timeline, order status tracker, git activity history, milestone list, pipeline steps, vertical and horizontal orientations, and timelines with leading labels.
The sections below explain the component architecture, real use cases in DevOps and SaaS products, feature details, and integration guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Timeline?
paragraphs: ReUI Timeline is a composable list-based component that renders event nodes connected by a vertical or horizontal line. Each node has an icon, avatar, or status indicator on the line, and a content panel beside it containing timestamp, title, description, metadata, and optional actions. The component does not prescribe a data format: pass any array of events and render each node with the content you need.

Twelve components show the Timeline in realistic production contexts: a deployment log where each deploy event is collapsible to reveal raw log output; an activity feed where commits, comments, and deployments share the same timeline with user Avatar nodes; a horizontal milestone tracker for release or project progress; and a pipeline step indicator for CI/CD status.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Timeline?
paragraphs: Timelines are essential in DevOps dashboards (deployment history, CI/CD pipeline steps), project management tools (milestone progress, sprint activity), e-commerce (order status), and SaaS audit logs (user activity, billing history). A consistent, accessible timeline component prevents ad-hoc list implementations that vary in spacing, icon size, and line rendering across the product.

Collapsible log entries let users expand individual timeline events for detail without navigating away: critical for deployment logs where the event title is a summary and the full output is hundreds of lines. Pair with [Shadcn Collapsible](/components/collapsible) for expandable nodes and [Shadcn Frame](/docs/components/base/frame) for timeline panels in dashboards.

featureItems.title: Vertical and Horizontal Orientation.
featureItems.description: Switch between vertical (events stacked top to bottom) and horizontal (events left to right) layout. Horizontal orientation works well for milestone trackers and pipeline step indicators.

featureItems.title: Deployment Log with Collapsible Entries.
featureItems.description: Timeline nodes wrap shadcn Collapsible so users can expand a deployment event to read the full build log inline without page navigation. Spinner shows active deployment state.

featureItems.title: Activity Feed with Avatars.
featureItems.description: Activity feed component renders user Avatar nodes on the timeline line for commits, comments, reviews, and deploys: the standard GitHub-style activity feed component.

featureItems.title: Roadmap and Milestone Variants.
featureItems.description: Roadmap timeline groups milestones by quarter or status with Badge labels. Milestone list shows completion state with icon changes from pending to completed.

featureItems.title: Order Status and Pipeline Steps.
featureItems.description: Order status component tracks shipment stages with color-coded state icons. Pipeline steps show CI/CD stage progression: build, test, deploy: with per-step status.

featureItems.title: Git Activity History.
featureItems.description: Git activity component renders commits, merges, and branches in timeline order with commit hash, message, and author avatar: adaptable to any version control activity feed.

featureItems.title: Leading and Trailing Labels.
featureItems.description: Horizontal timeline variant supports leading labels (above or below the line) for date and milestone labels, keeping the timeline line clean while surfacing temporal context.

featureItems.title: Frame Container Integration.
featureItems.description: Timeline components inside Frame containers include a toolbar, header, and optional action button. Renders as a self-contained dashboard widget for activity sidebars and DevOps panels.

sections.title: ReUI Timeline: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Timeline is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project: following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Timeline is a custom chronological event component not available in base shadcn/ui. It composes Avatar, Badge, Collapsible, Frame, and Spinner to render deployment logs, activity feeds, order status, git history, roadmaps, and pipeline step indicators. Twelve components cover vertical, horizontal, and leading-label layouts with Collapsible-wrapped log entries for expandable detail.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling: only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version — API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version — API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: Should I use horizontal or vertical timeline?
faqs.answer: Vertical timeline is the default for chronological event streams (history, activity log, story flow). Horizontal timeline works for milestones or project phases with fewer events. Vertical scales better on mobile.

faqs.question: How do I show loading state while events are being fetched?
faqs.answer: Render timeline items with skeleton content while loading. Replace skeletons with actual events once data arrives. Use a Spinner at the bottom if more events are loading on scroll.

faqs.question: Can I make timeline items collapsible to show/hide details?
faqs.answer: Yes. Wrap each timeline item content in a Collapsible or Accordion. Users click to expand and see full event details. This reduces visual clutter for long event streams.

faqs.question: How do I make timeline content accessible to screen readers?
faqs.answer: Use semantic HTML for events (article, time, header). Add aria-label to each item with a timestamp. Use role="list" and role="listitem" if rendering as a list. Announce state changes when events are added.

faqs.question: Can I use timeline for real-time event streams?
faqs.answer: Yes. Append new timeline items at the bottom or top as events arrive. Autoscroll to the latest event or let users scroll freely. Avoid constant scrolling: let users control their view.

faqs.question: What's the difference between ReUI timeline and base shadcn/ui?
faqs.answer: ReUI Timeline is a custom in-house component with 12 components for vertical, horizontal, and collapsible layouts. Base shadcn/ui doesn't include a timeline component. ReUI's version integrates with other ReUI components for rich event displays.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Use [Shadcn Avatar](/components/avatar) for user-attributed timeline nodes in activity feeds, [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for status and category labels on events, and [Shadcn Spinner](/components/spinner) for in-progress nodes in deployment and pipeline timelines.

Wrap expandable timeline nodes in [Shadcn Collapsible](/components/collapsible) to show summary headers with on-demand detail expansion: the deployment log component. Use [Shadcn Scroll Area](/components/scroll-area) around the full timeline list in constrained-height sidebar panels.

Pair Timeline with [Shadcn Data Grid](/docs/components/base/data-grid) for views that switch between timeline and table: both rendering the same event dataset. Use [Shadcn Frame](/docs/components/base/frame) to give timeline panels a toolbar, header title, and action button in dashboard layouts.

toggle.description: {{count}} free shadcn toggle components for button toggles, text formatting. Switch React binary states with Tailwind CSS-styled accessible toggle buttons.
toggle.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn toggle components for two-state buttons, text formatting controls, and view mode switching. Shadcn Toggle is built on Radix UI primitives and fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options.

content.title: Shadcn Toggle: Two-State Button Control
summary: Shadcn Toggle is a button that toggles between on and off states. Built on Radix UI primitives, it follows WAI-ARIA button semantics and announces state to screen readers via aria-pressed attribute.

Use for text formatting (bold, italic), view mode switching (list vs. grid), or any toggle-like action that doesn't fit a [Shadcn Switch](/components/switch) or [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox). Shadcn Create style options apply to button colors and sizing so your design tokens flow into exported code.

ReUI lists toggle components inside text editors, content filters, and view controllers so you evaluate icon clarity, sizing, and spacing next to real workflows. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how it pairs with other components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Toggle?
paragraphs: A toggle is a button that switches between two states: pressed (on) and unpressed (off). Unlike a [Shadcn Button](/components/button), a toggle retains its state visually after being clicked. Shadcn Toggle uses the Radix UI Toggle primitive, which manages focus, aria-pressed, and keyboard interaction.
Components here show toggle with icons, text labels, and sizing variants. They help you decide when a toggle (stateful) versus a regular button (action-oriented) is semantically correct.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Toggle?
paragraphs: Teams use toggles for view options (show/hide, list/grid), text formatting (bold, italic, underline), and feature toggles in dense interfaces like editors or dashboards. A toggle signals that clicking it changes a UI state, not just triggers an action.

Searchers look for 'shadcn toggle button', 'react toggle component', and 'text formatting button'. This page answers those intents with realistic layouts showing how to combine toggles with icons, labels, and groups.

featureItems.title: Radix UI Foundation.
featureItems.description: Built on Radix UI Toggle primitive. Handles aria-pressed, focus, and keyboard navigation automatically.

featureItems.title: Icon and Text Support.
featureItems.description: Render icons from Lucide, text labels, or both. Customize sizing and spacing via Tailwind utilities and Shadcn Create options.

featureItems.title: Pressed State Styling.
featureItems.description: Visually distinct when pressed (e.g., different background color). Use data-state="on" or data-state="off" for conditional styling.

featureItems.title: Size Variants.
featureItems.description: Small, default, and large sizes for different contexts. Choose based on density and hit target needs.

featureItems.title: Disabled State.
featureItems.description: Set disabled prop to prevent user interaction. Style disabled toggles differently so intent is clear.

faqs.question: What's the difference between toggle, switch, and checkbox?
faqs.answer: Checkbox is for multi-select or affirmations. Switch is for on/off state changes (immediately effective). Toggle is for single-select UI controls (bold, italic, alignment). Use based on the semantic intent.

faqs.question: Can I use toggle as a form field?
faqs.answer: Yes, though it's less common than checkbox or switch. Use a toggle group for multi-option selection (alignment: left, center, right). For single boolean fields, switch is clearer.

faqs.question: How do I manage toggle state in React?
faqs.answer: Use useState for uncontrolled mode or pass pressed/onPressedChange props for controlled mode: `<Toggle pressed={isActive} onPressedChange={setIsActive}>`.

faqs.question: How do I make a toggle icon-only and still accessible?
faqs.answer: Use an icon without text and add an aria-label: `<Toggle aria-label="Bold"><Bold /></Toggle>`. Screen readers will announce 'Bold button, toggle' on focus.

faqs.question: Can I disable a toggle?
