faqs.answer: Yes. Set disabled prop: `<Toggle disabled>Bold</Toggle>`. Style it to look unavailable. Pair with a Tooltip to explain why it's disabled.

faqs.question: Should I use toggle or toggle-group for multiple selections?
faqs.answer: Use Toggle for single binary controls. Use Toggle-Group for multi-option selection or single-select options (alignment, text formatting). Toggle-Group manages state across multiple toggles.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Use toggles with text formatting controls in rich text editors. Pair with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for text color or font size actions.
Combine toggles with [Shadcn Toggle Group](/components/toggle-group) to create exclusive or multiple-select button groups (e.g., view mode: list, grid, kanban).
Show [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) on icon-only toggles to clarify their purpose for screen readers and new users.

toggle-group.description: {{count}} free shadcn toggle group components for exclusive selection, multi-select buttons. Group React toggle controls into Tailwind CSS-styled single or multi-select sets.
toggle-group.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn toggle-group components for exclusive and multi-select button groups. Toggle Group is built on Radix UI primitives and fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options.

content.title: Shadcn Toggle Group: Exclusive and Multi-Select Buttons
summary: Shadcn Toggle Group is a wrapper for multiple [Shadcn Toggle](/components/toggle) buttons that enforces exclusivity (radio-like) or allows multiple selections. Built on Radix UI primitives, it follows WAI-ARIA radio and button group semantics.

Use for view modes (list, grid, kanban), sorting options, or filter states where one or more selections are active. Shadcn Create style options apply to button colors and spacing so your design tokens flow into exported code.

ReUI lists toggle-group components inside dashboards, editors, and view controllers so you evaluate button sizing, icon clarity, and responsiveness next to real workflows. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how it pairs with other components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Toggle Group?
paragraphs: A toggle group is a set of two-state buttons where one or more can be active at once. Shadcn Toggle Group uses the Radix UI Toggle Group primitive with exclusive (radio) or multiple selection modes. It manages focus and ARIA attributes so keyboard and screen reader users can navigate and select.

Components here show exclusive (only one active) and multiple-selection groups, icons, text labels, and responsive sizing. They help you decide whether toggle group or [Shadcn Radio Group](/components/radio-group) or [Shadcn Button Group](/components/button-group) fits your use case.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Toggle Group?
paragraphs: Teams use toggle groups for view switching (list, grid, details), sorting order (ascending, descending), or status filters (pending, active, completed). A toggle group makes the selected state explicit and avoids dropdown fatigue when only 2–4 options exist.

Searchers look for 'shadcn toggle-group', 'exclusive button selection', and 'multi-select button group'. This page answers those intents with realistic layouts showing how toggle groups integrate with filters, view modes, and dashboards.

featureItems.title: Radix UI Toggle Group Foundation.
featureItems.description: Built on Radix UI. Enforces exclusivity via type="single" or allows multiple selections via type="multiple".

featureItems.title: Exclusive (Radio-like) Selection.
featureItems.description: type="single" ensures only one button can be active. Clicking a different button deselects the previous one.

featureItems.title: Multi-Select Mode.
featureItems.description: type="multiple" allows multiple buttons to be active simultaneously. Useful for filter combinations.

featureItems.title: Icon and Text Support.
featureItems.description: Each ToggleGroupItem can show icons from Lucide, text labels, or both. Customize via Tailwind utilities.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Navigation.
featureItems.description: Arrow keys navigate between buttons. Space or Enter activates the button. Full WCAG compliance for radio or button group components.

faqs.question: Should I use toggle-group vs radio-group vs checkbox-group?
faqs.answer: Toggle-group for single-select visual controls (alignment, text formatting). Radio-group for single-select with clear options (payment method). Checkbox-group for multi-select with clear options. Choose based on visual design intent.

faqs.question: How do I implement single vs multiple selection in toggle-group?
faqs.answer: Single-select: `<ToggleGroup type="single" value={selected} onValueChange={setSelected}>` (only one toggle active). Multiple-select: `type="multiple"` allows multiple toggles active simultaneously.

faqs.question: How do I make toggle-group icons-only and accessible?
faqs.answer: Use icons without visible text and add aria-label to each toggle: `<Toggle value="bold" aria-label="Bold"><Bold /></Toggle>`. The group announces selection state to screen readers.

faqs.question: Can I disable specific toggles in a toggle-group?
faqs.answer: Yes. Set disabled on individual Toggle elements: `<Toggle value="italic" disabled>`. Disabled toggles look unavailable and don't respond to clicks.

faqs.question: How do I integrate toggle-group with forms?
faqs.answer: Use React Hook Form's register: `<ToggleGroup {...register('alignment')} type="single">`. The selected value is submitted as form data.

faqs.question: What ARIA roles are needed for accessible toggle-group?
faqs.answer: The group needs role="group" or role="toolbar" depending on context. Individual toggles need aria-pressed="true/false" to announce state. ToggleGroup components handle this automatically.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Use toggle-group for view mode switching alongside [Shadcn Table](/components/table) or [Shadcn Card](/components/card) content to let users choose list, grid, or kanban display.
Combine toggle-group with filters: pair view toggles with a [Shadcn Select](/components/select) or [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) for additional filter options.
Add [Shadcn Tooltip](/components/tooltip) on icon-only toggle group items to clarify their purpose for keyboard and screen reader users.

tooltip.description: {{count}} free shadcn tooltip components for contextual help, hints. Add React contextual hints with Tailwind CSS-styled accessible tooltip overlays.
tooltip.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn tooltip components for contextual help hints, keyboard shortcuts, and icon labels. Shadcn Tooltip is built on Radix UI primitives and fully compatible with Shadcn Create style options.

content.title: Shadcn Tooltip: Contextual Help and Hints
summary: Shadcn Tooltip is a popover that shows brief contextual information when users hover, focus, or interact with an element. Built on Radix UI, it follows WAI-ARIA tooltip semantics and respects keyboard focus so screen readers and keyboard users can access the content.

Wrap a trigger element (icon, button, link) and define the tooltip content. Control delay (when tooltip appears) and positioning (top, right, bottom, left). Shadcn Create style options apply to tooltip background, text, and sizing so your design tokens flow into exported code.

ReUI lists tooltip components on icon buttons, help text, keyboard shortcuts, and form hints so you evaluate timing, positioning, and content length next to real interfaces. The sections below explain what it is, why teams use it, which features matter, and how it pairs with other components.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Tooltip?
paragraphs: A tooltip is a non-modal, non-interactive popover that appears on hover or focus to provide brief information. Unlike [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) (interactive, can contain buttons), tooltips are read-only hints that disappear when focus leaves the trigger.

Components here show tooltips on icon buttons, help icons, keyboard shortcuts, and disabled controls. They help you decide whether a tooltip, [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover), or inline text is clearer for your hint.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Tooltip?
paragraphs: Teams use tooltips to avoid clutter. Rather than label every icon, a tooltip explains on demand. Keyboard shortcuts, button purposes, and disabled reasons are common tooltip uses.

Searchers look for 'shadcn tooltip', 'react tooltip component', and 'accessible tooltip'. This page answers those intents with realistic layouts showing how tooltips pair with icons, buttons, and form fields.

featureItems.title: Radix UI Foundation.
featureItems.description: Built on Radix UI Tooltip primitive. Manages focus, delay, positioning, and ARIA attributes automatically.

featureItems.title: Hover and Focus Trigger.
featureItems.description: Tooltip appears on mouse hover or keyboard focus. Respects user preferences (prefers-reduced-`motion`) for animations.

featureItems.title: Configurable Delay.
featureItems.description: Control when the tooltip appears after hover or focus. Default delay prevents tooltips from flashing on quick interaction.

featureItems.title: Positioning Options.
featureItems.description: Display tooltip above, below, left, or right of the trigger. Auto-adjust if near viewport edge to prevent cutoff.

featureItems.title: Brief, Non-Interactive Content.
featureItems.description: Tooltips are read-only hints. For interactive content (buttons, links), use [Shadcn Popover](/components/popover) instead.

faqs.question: What's the difference between a tooltip, popover, and hover-card?
faqs.answer: Tooltip shows a brief label on hover (e.g., button icon explanation). Popover is interactive and persistent, triggered by click (e.g., a filter panel). Hover-card is richer than a tooltip but simpler than a popover. Choose based on content richness and interaction.

faqs.question: How do I show a tooltip on a disabled button?
faqs.answer: Wrap the disabled button in a span with pointer-events: none removed, or use a container with the tooltip trigger. The issue: disabled buttons don't receive mouse events. Solution: wrap with a div and attach the tooltip to the wrapper.

faqs.question: Can I customize tooltip delay and animation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Most tooltip libraries expose delayOpen and delayClose props in milliseconds. Adjust the animation duration via Tailwind or CSS transitions. Common component: fast open (100ms), longer close (300ms) to prevent flicker.

faqs.question: Do tooltips work on touch devices?
faqs.answer: No. Hovering doesn't exist on touch. Show tooltips on long-press or tap-and-hold, or replace them with visible labels. For mobile-first design, consider always-visible text instead of tooltips for critical information.

faqs.question: How do I create a tooltip for an icon-only button?
faqs.answer: Pair the icon button with a tooltip. The button has no visible text, only an icon (e.g., a gear icon). The tooltip reveals the label ('Settings') on hover. Use aria-label on the button for screen readers.

faqs.question: Can I trigger a tooltip programmatically?
faqs.answer: Yes. Most tooltip libraries expose open and close functions via a ref or callback. Trigger on focus, after an error, or after a user action. Use sparingly so tooltips feel helpful, not disruptive.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating with Other Components
integrationBody: Add tooltips to icon-only [Shadcn Button](/components/button) components to clarify their purpose: `<Button variant="ghost"><HelpIcon /></Button>` with a tooltip hint.
Pair tooltips with [Shadcn Toggle](/components/toggle) and [Shadcn Toggle Group](/components/toggle-group) buttons to explain what each option does.
Use tooltips on disabled form fields to explain why they're unavailable (e.g., 'disabled: complete step 1 first').

tree.description: {{count}} free shadcn tree components built on Headless Tree. File explorer, org chart, permissions tree, indented lines, and keyboard navigation. Copy-ready for React and Next.js. Navigate React hierarchical data with Tailwind CSS-styled collapsible tree views.
tree.intro: Browse {{count}} production-ready shadcn tree components using `@headless-tree/core` and `@headless-tree/react`. Covers basic tree, indented guide lines, custom indentation, file explorer with type icons, organization chart with avatars, and permissions tree with checkboxes: all keyboard accessible with expand and collapse animations.

content.title: Shadcn Tree: File Explorer and Hierarchical Navigation
summary: ReUI Tree is a custom shadcn component built on `@headless-tree/core` and `@headless-tree/react`: a fully accessible, headless tree view library that handles expand/collapse state, keyboard navigation, selection model, async child loading, and WAI-ARIA tree roles without dictating markup.

Seven components cover a basic tree, indented guide lines, custom indent sizes, file explorer with file type icons, organization chart with avatar-based nodes, and a permissions tree with per-node checkbox selection.
The sections below explain the Headless Tree foundation, use cases across file systems, org charts, and permission editors, feature details, and composition guidance.

sections.title: What is Shadcn Tree?
paragraphs: ReUI Tree is a custom shadcn component: not part of the base shadcn/ui library. It is built on `@headless-tree/core` for framework-agnostic tree state management and `@headless-tree/react` for React bindings. The library handles expand/collapse state, keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Home/End, typeahead character search), multi-selection, async child loading, drag-and-drop reordering, and WAI-ARIA tree and treeitem roles.

ReUI wraps the Headless Tree API in shadcn-compatible markup, using Tailwind for indentation guides, expand icons, hover and selection states. Components show trees inside realistic containers: a file explorer with file type icons and folder collapse, an organization chart with Avatar nodes, and a permissions editor with Checkbox nodes.

sections.title: Why Use Shadcn Tree?
paragraphs: Hierarchical data appears in file systems, folder browsers, org charts, permission editors, navigation menus, category taxonomies, and code editors. Implementing a keyboard-accessible, WAI-ARIA-compliant tree from scratch is error-prone: Headless Tree handles all state and ARIA management. ReUI components give you production-ready markup and Tailwind styling you can adapt.

The file explorer component is directly applicable to document management, CMS folder browsers, and code editors. The permissions tree with checkboxes handles role-based access control UIs where permissions nest by resource and action. Pair with [Shadcn Avatar](/components/avatar) for org charts and [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) for permission nodes.

sections.title: Libraries and ReUI Tree Primitives
paragraphs: ReUI Tree imports `@headless-tree/core` for the framework-agnostic tree state engine and `@headless-tree/react` for the React integration layer. It composes shadcn Avatar for org chart nodes and Checkbox for permission tree nodes.
The library supports sync and async child loading, virtual scroll integration, and drag-and-drop: features ReUI exposes as needed across the seven tree components.

featureItems.title: Headless Tree Core and React.
featureItems.description: Powered by `@headless-tree/core` and `@headless-tree/react`. Full WAI-ARIA tree and treeitem roles, expand/collapse state, keyboard navigation (ArrowUp, ArrowDown, ArrowLeft to collapse, ArrowRight to expand, Home, End, typeahead), and multi-selection.

featureItems.title: File Explorer with Type Icons.
featureItems.description: File explorer component shows folder and file nodes with type-specific icons: distinguishing TypeScript, JSON, image, and folder nodes. Folders collapse and expand; files are leaf nodes with click actions.

featureItems.title: Organization Chart with Avatars.
featureItems.description: Org chart component renders each node with shadcn Avatar, name, and role. The tree structure represents reporting relationships and is collapsible at each manager level.

featureItems.title: Permissions Tree with Checkboxes.
featureItems.description: Permissions component adds shadcn Checkbox to each tree node for role-based access control UIs. Parent checkboxes reflect indeterminate state when only some children are checked.

featureItems.title: Indented Guide Lines.
featureItems.description: Optional vertical indentation guide lines clarify nesting depth for deep trees. Line style, color, and indent width are Tailwind utilities: customize per tree instance.

featureItems.title: Keyboard Navigation.
featureItems.description: Arrow keys navigate and expand/collapse nodes. Home and End jump to first and last visible node. Typeahead search jumps to nodes starting with the typed character. All navigation follows WAI-ARIA tree keyboard interaction models.

featureItems.title: Custom Indent and Node Rendering.
featureItems.description: Indent width is configurable per tree and per level. Each node renders any React content: icon, avatar, badge, action button: without breaking keyboard focus management.

sections.title: ReUI Tree: In-House Custom Shadcn Component
paragraphs: ReUI Tree is an in-house component built and maintained by the ReUI team as part of the ReUI open-source library. It is designed to integrate seamlessly into any shadcn/ui project: following the same copy-and-own installation model. The component source lands directly in your repository; you own it, extend it, and style it with your Tailwind CSS tokens. ReUI Tree is a custom hierarchical navigation component not available in base shadcn/ui. Built on `@headless-tree/core` for framework-agnostic state management and `@base-ui/react` for React bindings, it handles expand/collapse, keyboard navigation (full WAI-ARIA tree semantics), multi-selection, and async child loading. Seven components cover basic tree, indented guide lines, file explorer with type icons, org chart with Avatar nodes, and a permissions tree with Checkbox selection.

The component is available in two API flavors to match your primitive stack: a Radix UI version for teams using Radix-based shadcn primitives, and a Base UI version for teams on `@base-ui/react`. Both versions share identical visual output and Tailwind CSS styling: only the underlying headless primitive differs. ReUI maintains 10+ such in-house components, all available in both Radix and Base UI versions, each with dedicated documentation covering the full component API, usage examples, and prop references.

docsLinks.label: Radix UI Version — API, Props and Usage
Base UI Version — API, Props and Usage

faqs.question: How do I lazy-load child nodes on expand?
faqs.answer: Set a loading state when the user expands a node. Fetch children via API. Once loaded, update the node's children and transition from loading to expanded. Use an onExpandChange callback to trigger loads.

faqs.question: Can I control which nodes are expanded or collapsed?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use a controlled expandedIds state: `<Tree expandedIds={expandedIds} onExpandedIdsChange={setExpandedIds}>`. This lets you expand/collapse nodes programmatically or sync expansion state across sessions.

faqs.question: How do I add checkboxes to tree nodes for multi-select?
faqs.answer: Render a Checkbox inside each node's content. Track selected node IDs in state. On checkbox change, update state with the node ID. Include children in selection if needed (cascade).

faqs.question: Can I drag-and-drop nodes to reorder or move them to other branches?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use DnD Kit's tree adapter or wrap the tree in a SortableContext. On drop, update the parent-child relationships in state. The tree component demonstrates drag-reordering within a hierarchy.

faqs.question: How do I handle keyboard navigation in a deep tree?
faqs.answer: Arrow keys navigate up/down/left/right (expand/collapse on right, move to parent on left). Home and End jump to first/last visible nodes. Enter expands/collapses. The tree library handles focus and announcements automatically.

faqs.question: What makes ReUI Tree different from standard shadcn/ui?
faqs.answer: ReUI Tree is a custom in-house component with 9 components for nested, collapsible, and draggable trees. It uses `@headless-tree/react` for keyboard navigation and ARIA roles. Both Radix and Base UI versions are available.

relatedComponents.title: Integrating With Other Components
integrationBody: Use [Shadcn Avatar](/components/avatar) for org chart tree nodes, [Shadcn Checkbox](/components/checkbox) for permissions tree nodes, and [Shadcn Badge](/docs/components/base/badge) for status or count indicators on folder nodes.

Pair Tree with [Shadcn Scroll Area](/components/scroll-area) for constrained-height containers that scroll the tree list without growing the layout, and [Shadcn Skeleton](/components/skeleton) for async child loading states.

Combine with [Shadcn Button](/components/button) for node actions (add child, rename, delete) and [Shadcn Context Menu](/components/context-menu) for right-click node action menus: a common component in file explorers and CMS folder trees.
